When it was over they left without comment. I have no idea what that was about.
reactions: Silence can be louder than words. And more unsettling.

entries.intro: {npc_name} collects smooth stones. Has a whole shelf of them, all different colors.
details: They pick them up on walks and hold each one up to the light before deciding to keep it.
I asked what makes a stone worth keeping and they said 'you just know.' Very helpful.
reactions: Odd hobbies are the best kind. At least it's harmless.

entries.intro: {npc_name} mended my coat and left it on the hook without a word. I only noticed because the stitching style was different.
details: Neat work too, tight and even. The tear had been bothering me for weeks and now it's stronger than the rest of the seam.
I thanked them and they looked embarrassed, like they'd been caught doing something silly.
reactions: Quiet acts of care are worth more than grand gestures.

entries.intro: I think {npc_name} resents how much work I get done. I've seen the looks.
details: When someone mentions my progress, their jaw tightens. Barely noticeable, but it's there.
Last week I finished the frame early and {npc_name} found a reason to leave the room. Didn't say a word, just walked out.
reactions: I'm not going to slow down to make someone else comfortable. But it makes things awkward.

statCheck.pass: They measure themselves against everyone around them. It's not about me specifically: I just happen to be visible.
statCheck.fail: I could be wrong. Body language is easy to misread.

entries.intro: {npc_name} tells stories about their own mistakes like they're proud of them.
details: Had us laughing about the time they built a shelf upside down and didn't notice for a week.
I can't tell if it's genuine humility or just a way to control the narrative.
reactions: Either way, it works. People warm up to someone who doesn't take themselves too seriously.

entries.intro: {npc_name} has been whispering with {npc_name_2} a lot lately and they go quiet when anyone approaches.
details: It could be nothing. People have private conversations. But the timing feels deliberate.
Both of them have been acting slightly off since it started.
reactions: I'm telling myself it's not about me. Mostly I believe that.

statCheck.pass: I think they're planning something for the settlement: possibly a surprise for someone. The secrecy has that flavor.
statCheck.fail: I have no clue. Could be anything from a gift to a grievance.

entries.intro: {npc_name} is proof that good people exist. Plain and simple.
details: No agenda, no score-keeping. They're kind because it's how they're built.
I've watched them long enough to know it's not an act. Some people are just decent to the bone.
reactions: Hold onto people like that. They're the reason the rest of us keep trying.

entries.intro: {npc_name} always smells like woodsmoke. Even indoors, even in summer.
details: I asked about it once and they shrugged like they hadn't noticed. I think it's just part of them at this point.
It's not unpleasant, honestly: just distinctive. You always know when {npc_name} has been in a room.
reactions: Funny how you start associating a smell with a person after long enough.

entries.intro: {npc_name} helped me carry water this morning without saying a word. Just fell into step beside me.
details: Didn't ask, didn't wait for me to struggle. Just saw it and handled it.
We walked the whole way back in silence. It was one of the most comfortable silences I've had.
reactions: Some people express friendship through presence, not words. I appreciate that.

statCheck.pass: I think {npc_name} does it because they remember what it felt like to need help and get none.
statCheck.fail: I don't know why they do it. Some people are just naturally decent.

entries.intro: {npc_name} acts like they're the only one here with real problems. It gets old.
details: You try to mention something going on with you and it circles back to them in two sentences flat.
I've stopped bringing things up around {npc_name}. It's easier to just nod and wait for it to be over.
reactions: We've all got burdens. Some of us just carry them quieter.

statCheck.pass: I think {npc_name} genuinely doesn't realize they do it. It's not malice: it's a blind spot the size of a barn.
statCheck.fail: I try not to hold it against them. We all have our flaws.

entries.intro: I've noticed {npc_name} always lets other people go first through doorways. Every time.
details: Not in a showy way. They just step back half a pace and gesture you through.
It's the kind of thing you only notice after you've seen it a dozen times.
reactions: Small courtesies like that say more about a person than big gestures.

statCheck.pass: I think it's deliberate: {npc_name} once told me they believe small courtesies keep a place civilized.
statCheck.fail: I couldn't tell you if it's habit or intention. Either way, it's nice.

entries.intro: {npc_name} has a temper that flares out of nowhere. You never know what'll set it off.
details: One minute everything's fine, the next they're snapping over something trivial. Then it's over like it never happened.
I've learned to wait about ten seconds after an outburst. They usually apologize, to their credit.
reactions: I don't think they enjoy it either. But knowing that doesn't make it less exhausting.

statCheck.pass: Between you and me, I think they're frustrated with their own situation and it leaks out sideways.
statCheck.fail: I couldn't tell you what causes it. Moods are moods.

entries.intro: {npc_name} walked all the way across the settlement to return a bowl I'd forgotten I lent them.
details: It was months ago and I'd completely written it off. But there they were, bowl in hand, apologizing for the delay.
Even washed it. Cleaner than when I gave it to them, honestly.
reactions: You don't meet many people who bother returning the small things.

entries.intro: {npc_name} talks to their plants. Full conversations, like they're expecting a reply.
details: I heard them scolding a vine for growing the wrong direction the other day. Dead serious about it too.
The strange part is their garden's one of the best around here. So maybe the plants are listening.
reactions: I've stopped finding it odd. If it works, it works.

statCheck.pass: I think {npc_name} started doing it after losing a crop years back. Talks to the plants like they'll listen if asked nicely enough.
statCheck.fail: I have no idea why they do it. Maybe it's just comfort.

entries.intro: {npc_name} told me something personal the other day and I haven't known what to do with it since.
details: It wasn't anything dramatic. Just honest in a way people around here usually aren't.
I wanted to say the right thing back. I'm not sure I managed it.
reactions: When someone trusts you like that, it changes how you see them.

statCheck.pass: I think they wanted someone to know before they made a decision about something. Like they needed a witness.
statCheck.fail: I'm not sure why they told me. Maybe it was just the moment.

entries.intro: {npc_name} never puts anything back where they found it. Not once, not ever.
details: Chairs, tools, baskets: everything ends up three feet from where it started. Consistently.
I reorganized the storehouse last month. Took {npc_name} two days to undo all of it.
reactions: I've accepted it. Fighting it is like arguing with the tide.

entries.intro: I overheard {npc_name} say something nice about me to someone else. Made my whole week.
details: They didn't know I was around the corner. That's how I know they meant it.
It wasn't anything big: just that I was dependable. But coming from {npc_name}, that carries weight.
reactions: The best compliments are the ones you're not meant to hear.

entries.intro: {npc_name} and {npc_name_2} trade barbs all day long, but I think they actually enjoy it.
details: Yesterday {npc_name} called {npc_name_2} the laziest person in the settlement and they both laughed.
If anyone else said half those things there'd be a real fight. Between those two it's just how they talk.
reactions: Took me a while to realize they weren't actually angry. Just their way of being friendly.

entries.intro: {npc_name} eats the same thing every single day. Doesn't seem to bother them at all.
details: I asked if they wanted to try something different once. They looked at me like I'd suggested jumping off a roof.
There's something oddly admirable about knowing exactly what you want and never wavering.
reactions: To each their own. I'd go out of my mind, but that's me.

statCheck.pass: I think it's a comfort thing. When everything else is uncertain, at least the meal is the same.
statCheck.fail: Could just be they don't care much about food. Some people don't.

entries.intro: I think {npc_name} genuinely doesn't care what people think of them. I wish I had that.
details: They wear what they like, say what they think, and it doesn't seem to cost them a moment of sleep.
I've seen people try to embarrass them and it just rolls off. Like water off stone.
reactions: It's either confidence or obliviousness, and honestly I'm not sure it matters which.

entries.intro: {npc_name} keeps track of every favor they do. I can feel the ledger being updated in real time.
details: Helped me fix a bucket last week and I could already see them filing it away for later.
It's never outright 'you owe me.' More of a look, a pause, a carefully timed mention.
reactions: Generosity with strings attached isn't really generosity.

statCheck.pass: I think they learned it young: never give anything without knowing what you'll get back. Sad, when you think about it.
statCheck.fail: Maybe I'm being unfair. Not everyone shows kindness the same way.

entries.intro: {npc_name} always asks about my day before talking about theirs. Every time.
details: It sounds simple, but you'd be amazed how few people do it. Most jump straight to their own news.
I've started doing it too. {npc_name} rubbed off on me and I didn't even notice.
reactions: Small habits like that shape a place more than people realize.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} told me once that they learned it from someone who did the same for them. Said it changed how they talked to people.
statCheck.fail: I don't know where they picked it up. Just a good habit, I suppose.

entries.intro: {npc_name} walks too fast. {npc_name_2} and I can never keep up when we're all going the same direction.
details: I've tried matching their pace and I'm winded in thirty steps. {npc_name_2} just gave up and fell behind.
They're always three paces ahead, talking over their shoulder like we're side by side.
reactions: Not a flaw, really. Just means every walk together is a light jog for me.

entries.intro: {npc_name} dropped everything to help me when my storage shelf collapsed. Spent the whole evening on it.
details: Had plans of their own, I could tell. Didn't say a word about it until we were done.
Built it back sturdier than before too. I don't think the old one was ever that solid.
reactions: That's the kind of thing that makes you rethink who your real friends are.

entries.intro: {npc_name} can hold a grudge longer than anyone I've ever met.
details: I accidentally knocked over their water pail weeks ago and I'm still getting frost from across the path.
I apologized twice, clean and sincere. Didn't matter.
reactions: Some people carry slights around like trophies. I'll never understand it.

statCheck.pass: I've heard they're still sore about something someone said months back. So it's not just me they hold onto.
statCheck.fail: I don't know if it's personal or if that's just how they're built.

entries.intro: {npc_name} laughs at their own jokes before they even finish telling them.
details: You can see it building: the shoulders start shaking before the punchline is halfway out.
The jokes aren't even that funny most of the time. But the laugh is infectious, so you end up laughing anyway.
reactions: I'd rather be around someone who laughs too easily than someone who never does.

statCheck.pass: I think the laughing is their way of making sure everyone's having a good time. It's generous, in a way.
statCheck.fail: I don't know why they do it. Just how they're built, I suppose.

entries.intro: {npc_name} treats every minor inconvenience like a personal insult from the world itself.
details: Stubbed a toe last week and you'd think the ground had done it on purpose.
Burned a meal the other night and blamed the weather, the firewood, and the pot. Not in that order.
reactions: Living next to that kind of energy is tiring, even when it's not aimed at you.

statCheck.pass: Between you and me, I think {npc_name} is dealing with more than they let on. The anger comes from somewhere deep.
statCheck.fail: I try not to think about what's behind it. Not my place.

entries.intro: {npc_name} has the steadiest hands of anyone I've seen. Never a tremble, never a slip.
details: I watched them thread a needle on the first try in dim light. I can barely do it in broad daylight.
They're the same way with everything: measured, precise, unhurried.
reactions: I envy that kind of control. My hands shake if I've had too much tea.

entries.intro: I think {npc_name} gossips about everyone and then acts surprised when it gets back around.
details: I've been told twice this month that {npc_name} had opinions about my work. Not to my face, of course.
The thing is, they're perfectly pleasant in person. That's what makes it worse.
reactions: I just assume everything I say near them will travel.

statCheck.pass: I tested it once: told {npc_name} something made up. Heard it from someone else three days later.
statCheck.fail: I can't prove anything. Just a pattern I've noticed.

entries.intro: {npc_name} has a talent for showing up at exactly the right moment.
details: I was about to drop an armful of supplies yesterday and there they were, hands out, catching the lot.
It happens too often to be coincidence. I think they just pay attention to the world around them.
reactions: People like that make everything run a little smoother without anyone noticing.

statCheck.pass: I think they plan their day around other people's routines. It's not luck: it's attention.
statCheck.fail: Maybe they're just observant. Hard to say.

entries.intro: {npc_name} always has mud on their boots, and {npc_name_2} has started complaining about the tracks.
details: You can follow {npc_name}'s morning route by the footprints alone. {npc_name_2} mopped the common area twice last week.
I mentioned it once, gently. {npc_name} looked down at their boots like they'd never seen them before.
reactions: Some battles aren't worth fighting. The mud is one of them.

entries.intro: {npc_name} made a point of welcoming the last traveler who came through. Walked right up and introduced themselves.
details: Most of us just watched from our doorways. {npc_name} actually went and talked to them.
Got them sorted with a place to rest and a meal before anyone else had even decided whether to say hello.
reactions: I should be more like that. But I never seem to be the first one to move.

statCheck.pass: I think {npc_name} knows what it feels like to arrive somewhere new and be ignored. That's why they reach out first.
statCheck.fail: I don't know what motivates it. Just a kind impulse, I suppose.

entries.intro: {npc_name} is impossible to argue with because they never raise their voice.
details: You end up looking like the unreasonable one no matter what your point was.
They just stay calm and repeat themselves in slightly different words until you give up.
reactions: I've started picking my disagreements more carefully. Not every hill is worth it.

statCheck.pass: I think they know exactly what they're doing. That calm is a weapon and they wield it well.
statCheck.fail: Maybe they're just naturally patient. Hard to say.

entries.intro: {npc_name} remembered something I mentioned in passing weeks ago and brought it up like it mattered.
details: I'd said something about wanting to try a different way of storing grain. Forgot I'd even said it.
They came to me with an idea about it, fully thought out. I was genuinely touched.
reactions: It's not the grain that mattered. It's that someone was actually listening.

statCheck.pass: I think {npc_name} writes things down. I've seen a little notebook they carry, full of other people's ideas.
statCheck.fail: I don't know how they do it. My memory is half that sharp on a good day.

entries.intro: {npc_name} makes a face every time someone else's food is being served. Subtle, but I've seen it.
details: This little wince, like they're bracing for something. Even before tasting it.
Meanwhile {npc_name}'s own cooking is nothing to celebrate. But you won't catch me saying that out loud.
reactions: I've started eating before they arrive. Saves me the editorial.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name}'s mother was apparently a very particular cook. I think the standards come from growing up with someone who judged every meal.
statCheck.fail: I have no idea where the pickiness comes from. Just seems to be how they are.

entries.intro: {npc_name} has a gift for making hard work look easy. I've never seen them strain.
details: Hauled twice the load I did yesterday and wasn't even breathing hard at the end.
I asked their secret and they said 'pacing.' That's it. One word.
reactions: Either they're stronger than they look or I'm weaker than I think.

entries.intro: {npc_name} has started asking everyone for their opinion on every little thing.
details: Where to put a bucket, which side of the path to walk on, whether a cloud looks like rain. All of it.
I think they're trying to be considerate, but it's had the opposite effect.
reactions: Just make a choice: any choice. I'm begging.

statCheck.pass: I think it started after someone accused them of being bossy. Now they overcompensate in the other direction.
statCheck.fail: I don't know what started it. Could just be how they are now.

entries.intro: {npc_name} always sits in the same spot. Every gathering, every meal, same chair.
details: I sat in it by accident once and the look I got could've curdled milk.
Now everyone knows it's {npc_name}'s chair. We've all just accepted it.
reactions: It's harmless, really. Everyone's got their little territories.

entries.intro: {npc_name} patched {npc_name_2}'s roof after the last storm without being asked. Wouldn't take payment.
details: {npc_name_2} tried to insist, even offered a week of meals in return. {npc_name} just waved it off.
I think it embarrassed {npc_name_2} a little, actually. Some people have a hard time being on the receiving end.
reactions: The world needs more people who help and then move on without making it a thing.

entries.intro: {npc_name} has a way of steering conversations to exactly where they want them.
details: You start talking about your day and ten minutes later you're agreeing to something you hadn't planned on.
It's smooth, I'll give them that. You don't realize it's happening until it's happened.
reactions: I've learned to pause and think before answering {npc_name}. That helps.

statCheck.pass: I watched them do it to someone else last week. Same technique: questions that feel casual but aren't.
statCheck.fail: Could be I'm overthinking it. Some people are just good talkers.

entries.intro: {npc_name} always stops working to greet people properly. Full eye contact, genuine smile.
details: Most of us give a nod or a wave while we keep moving. {npc_name} actually pauses.
It slows them down, I'm sure. But nobody feels rushed or ignored around them.
reactions: I tried doing it for a day and gave up by noon. I don't have the patience.

statCheck.pass: I think they do it because they were ignored a lot when they were younger. Now they make sure nobody else feels that way.
statCheck.fail: I don't know the reason. Some people are just naturally warm.

entries.intro: {npc_name} and {npc_name_2} have very different opinions about how firewood should be split.
details: {npc_name} insists on one angle, {npc_name_2} swears by another. I've heard this debate four times now.
The wood burns the same either way, but neither will admit that.
reactions: I split mine however I please and keep quiet about it.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} once admitted that their parent taught them and they can't unlearn it. The method is inherited stubbornness.
statCheck.fail: I don't know where the conviction comes from. Probably just years of practice.

entries.intro: {npc_name} left a thank-you note tucked under my door after I helped with the fence.
details: Wasn't expecting it. I'd forgotten I'd even helped until I read it.
It was short: just a few lines. But they took the time to write it by hand and that says something.
reactions: I keep it on the shelf. Nice to be reminded that small things get noticed.

entries.intro: {npc_name} is one of those people who hears everything. Walls, distance: doesn't matter.
details: I mentioned something to a friend across the yard and {npc_name} brought it up an hour later.
It's not that they're eavesdropping on purpose. They just have ears like a fox.
reactions: I've started whispering as a default. It's a new habit I didn't ask for.

entries.intro: {npc_name} gave me honest feedback on my work and it stung, but they were right.
details: Pointed out a problem I'd been ignoring because I didn't want to redo the whole thing.
Didn't dress it up, didn't soften it. Just told me straight and moved on.
reactions: I respect it more now than I did in the moment. That kind of honesty is rare.

entries.intro: {npc_name} complains about being tired but {npc_name_2} says they saw the lamplight on well past midnight. By choice.
details: {npc_name_2} mentioned it at breakfast and {npc_name} got defensive, saying 'I was reading.' Every night, apparently.
Then in the morning it's all yawns and 'I barely slept.' You chose that.
reactions: I don't have sympathy for self-inflicted exhaustion. Get some sleep.

statCheck.pass: I think they can't sleep even when they try. The staying up isn't a choice: it's a pattern they can't break.
statCheck.fail: Maybe they're a night person. Not everyone runs on the same clock.

entries.intro: {npc_name} and {npc_name_2} can't be in the same room without one of them making a snide comment.
details: It's always disguised as a joke, but the edge is real. Everyone feels it.
The rest of us just look at each other and wait for it to blow over.
reactions: I've stopped inviting them to the same things. It's easier for everyone.

statCheck.pass: I think it goes back to a disagreement about shared labor assignments. Neither one will let it go.
statCheck.fail: I have no idea what started it and I'm not going to ask.

entries.intro: I was wrong about {npc_name}. I thought they were cold, but they're just private.
details: Took me the better part of a year to realize the difference. Some people take time to warm up.
Once they opened up even a little, I found someone thoughtful and funnier than I expected.
reactions: Makes me wonder how many other people I've misjudged by taking the surface at face value.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} mentioned once, quietly, that people always assume the worst about them. It costs them more than they show.
statCheck.fail: I never asked why they keep to themselves. Some things aren't my business.

entries.intro: {npc_name} has this nervous laugh that comes out at the worst possible moments.
details: Someone was talking about a serious problem last week and {npc_name} just let out this giggle. The silence that followed was painful.
I don't think they can help it. It's a reflex, not a joke.
reactions: I feel bad for them. They always look horrified right after it happens.

statCheck.pass: They told me once that the laugh has happened since they were a child. They've tried to stop and can't.
statCheck.fail: I don't know if it's nerves or something else. Not my place to diagnose.

entries.intro: {npc_name} shared their last bit of dried meat with me when supplies were thin. Just halved it and handed it over.
details: I tried to refuse and they wouldn't hear it. Said they'd had plenty already, which I know wasn't true.
Smallest gesture, biggest impact. That's the kind of thing you don't forget.
reactions: I owe them one. And I intend to pay it back properly.

statCheck.pass: I think {npc_name} has gone without before: real hunger, not just skipping a meal. You can tell by how carefully they handle food.
statCheck.fail: I don't know their history. Just that it was a generous thing to do.

entries.intro: {npc_name} has opinions about everything, and {npc_name_2} has stopped even pretending to listen.
details: {npc_name} will tell you how to build a shed despite never lifting a hammer. {npc_name_2} just walks away now.
The worst part is {npc_name} is occasionally right, which makes it impossible to dismiss entirely.
reactions: I've learned to filter. Take the one useful thing and let the rest blow past.

entries.intro: I've never heard {npc_name} say a bad word about anyone. Not once.
details: Even when others are openly complaining, {npc_name} just listens or changes the subject.
I brought up someone we both find difficult and they managed to say something genuinely kind about them.
reactions: Either they're a saint or they've mastered the art of keeping things to themselves.

statCheck.pass: I think they've seen what gossip does to a place and decided they won't be part of it. Quietly principled.
statCheck.fail: I can't tell if it's discipline or just their nature.

entries.intro: {npc_name} slams doors. Every door, every time, like they've got a grievance with hinges.
details: I flinch every time now. You can hear it from the other end of the settlement.
I don't think it's anger. I think they genuinely don't know how hard they're pulling.
reactions: My nerves were fine before {npc_name} moved in next door.

entries.intro: {npc_name} keeps a tidy schedule. You could set a sundial by their routine.
details: Same path at the same time every morning. Same spot for lunch, same evening walk.
There's something reassuring about it. When you see {npc_name} pass by, you know what time it is.
reactions: I admire the discipline, even if I could never live that way myself.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} told me the routine started after a bad stretch where nothing felt under control. The schedule was how they got through it.
statCheck.fail: I don't know why they stick to it so tightly. Habit, maybe.

entries.intro: {npc_name} stood up for {npc_name_2} when nobody else would. That took guts.
details: It wasn't a popular position to take. A few people gave {npc_name} looks afterward.
But they didn't flinch. Said their piece, stood by it, and moved on.
reactions: I should have spoken up too. I think about that.

entries.intro: {npc_name} uses way more than their share of the communal water. I've been keeping count.
details: Three trips to the well while the rest of us make do with one. Every day.
I brought it up at a gathering and they acted like they'd never considered it. Maybe they hadn't.
reactions: It's the lack of awareness that bothers me more than the water itself.

entries.intro: I think {npc_name} is the wisest person here, and they'd be the last to claim it.
details: They don't give advice unless you ask. And when you do, it's practical and honest, not puffed up.
I've gone to them three times with problems and left each time feeling like I had a path forward.
reactions: Good judgment is quiet. The loud ones usually don't have it.

statCheck.pass: I noticed they only give advice when asked because they've been wrong before and it cost them a friendship.
statCheck.fail: I don't know why they hold back. Some people just don't push.

entries.intro: {npc_name} scratches carvings into the communal table. Little marks, lines, shapes.
details: I asked about it and got a shrug. I don't think they're even aware they're doing it.
The table looks like it lost a fight with a cat. It was smooth before {npc_name} started sitting there.
reactions: Not worth an argument. But I liked that table the way it was.

entries.intro: {npc_name} always takes the longest to decide anything. Meals, routes, what to work on first.
details: You can see the wheels turning, weighing and comparing and reconsidering. It takes forever.
The frustrating part is that once they decide, it's usually the right call.
reactions: I've made my peace with waiting. The alternative is worse.

statCheck.pass: I think {npc_name} saw someone make a hasty decision once that went badly. They've been overcorrecting ever since.
statCheck.fail: Could be they just think slowly. Not everyone runs at the same pace.

entries.intro: {npc_name} waited outside my door in the cold because they thought I might need company. I hadn't asked.
details: I'd had a rough few days and apparently it showed more than I realized.
They just sat on the step and said 'thought you might want to talk.' Simplest kindness imaginable.
reactions: Sometimes the people who notice are the ones who've been through it themselves.

entries.intro: {npc_name} talks over people constantly. I don't think a full sentence has survived their company.
details: I tried counting how many times they cut me off in a single conversation. Lost track after seven.
What makes it worse is that they're always circling back to their own topic. Your words just get swallowed.
reactions: I've resorted to holding up a finger when they start. It works about half the time.

statCheck.pass: I think they genuinely don't hear themselves doing it. When I pointed it out once, they were mortified.
statCheck.fail: I don't know if they realize. I'm not going to be the one to tell them.

entries.intro: {npc_name} keeps their promises. Every one, no matter how small.
details: Said they'd bring me some twine last week and I'd forgotten about it. They showed up two days later with the twine.
It's not about the twine. It's about knowing someone means what they say.
reactions: Reliability doesn't make for exciting conversation, but it makes for a good neighbor.

entries.intro: {npc_name} leaves crumbs everywhere they eat. Not just their spot: the path getting there too.
details: I followed a trail of bread bits once and it led straight to their door. Like a riddle with no mystery.
I'm not sure they're even eating the food or just carrying it around for decoration.
reactions: It attracts pests. That's the part that actually bothers me.

entries.intro: I barely know {npc_name}, but every interaction has been decent. That counts for something.
details: We've exchanged maybe twenty words total, but each time it felt genuine.
Not everyone needs to be a close friend. Sometimes a respectful distance is its own kind of bond.
reactions: I think well of them and I hope they think well of me. That's enough.

statCheck.pass: I get the sense {npc_name} keeps a respectful distance on purpose. Like they've been burned by getting too close before.
statCheck.fail: Could be they're just private. Not everyone opens up easily.

entries.intro: {npc_name} told {npc_name_2} they'd handle something and then dumped it on me instead.
details: I found out because {npc_name_2} thanked me for 'volunteering.' I had no idea what they were talking about.
By the time I sorted out what happened, the work needed doing anyway, so I just did it.
reactions: I haven't said anything yet. But there's a conversation coming.

entries.intro: {npc_name} always has a warm drink ready when I stop by. Without fail.
details: I don't know if they keep it hot all day or if the timing is just uncanny.
It's one of those small rituals that makes visiting them feel like coming home.
reactions: Hospitality like that costs almost nothing but means everything.

entries.intro: {npc_name} does this thing where they agree with you and then do whatever they were going to do anyway.
details: I suggested a different approach to stacking the supply crates. Got a 'great idea.' Nothing changed.
Three people have told me the same experience. It's practically a signature move.
reactions: At least they're polite about ignoring you. I suppose that's something.

entries.intro: {npc_name} taught me a trick for getting a fire started in the rain. Works every time now.
details: Simple thing, just the way you stack the kindling. But I never would have thought of it on my own.
They showed me once, patiently, and didn't make me feel stupid for not knowing.
reactions: The people who teach without condescending are worth their weight in gold.

entries.intro: {npc_name} gets quiet in groups but won't stop talking one-on-one. {npc_name_2} noticed it too.
details: In a gathering they just nod along. Catch them alone and suddenly they've got a hundred things to say.
{npc_name_2} and I compared notes and the pattern is identical with both of us.
reactions: I've started having my best conversations with them on walks, just the two of us.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} told me once that they freeze up in groups and their mind goes blank. One-on-one, they feel safe.
statCheck.fail: I'm not sure what causes the shift. Just seems like two different people.

entries.intro: {npc_name} somehow always picks the worst time to start a conversation.
details: Arms full of supplies, halfway up a ladder, elbow-deep in a repair: that's when {npc_name} wants to chat.
And it's never a quick question. It's always something that needs a thoughtful answer.
reactions: I've tried hinting. The hints don't land.

statCheck.pass: I think they're lonely and conversation is how they connect. The timing is just terrible because the need is always there.
statCheck.fail: I don't know why they can't read a room. Some people just miss those signals.

entries.intro: {npc_name} is the reason I started taking better care of my tools. Their example shamed me into it.
details: I saw their workspace once: everything oiled, sharpened, in its place. Mine looked like a disaster by comparison.
They didn't say a word about mine, but they didn't have to. I went home and reorganized that evening.
reactions: A good example is worth more than a lecture.

entries.intro: You'd think the {npc_role} would have thicker skin. {npc_name} takes every bit of criticism to heart.
details: I mentioned one small thing about how the work was going and they were upset for the rest of the day.
It wasn't even a complaint, more of an observation. But you'd think I'd called their mother a goat.
reactions: I've learned to be very careful with my words around them.

entries.intro: {npc_name} waved me over for a meal out of nowhere. Turns out they'd made too much and didn't want it to go to waste.
details: We ended up talking for two hours. I learned more about them in one evening than in months of passing hellos.
The food was good, but the company was better. I hadn't realized how much I'd needed that.
reactions: Sometimes the unplanned evenings are the best ones.

entries.intro: {npc_name} borrows ideas the way some people borrow tools: quietly, without attribution.
details: I suggested a way to improve the drainage last month. Two weeks later {npc_name} proposed the exact same thing as their own.
I wouldn't mind if they'd said 'I heard this idea.' It's the pretending that stings.
reactions: I still share ideas around here. I just don't share them with {npc_name} first anymore.

statCheck.pass: I've noticed others have had the same experience. {npc_name} is a collector of other people's good thinking.
statCheck.fail: Could be a coincidence. People do have the same ideas independently.

entries.intro: {npc_name} hums different tunes depending on their mood. {npc_name_2} and I have learned to read the signals.
details: Quick, bouncy tune means good day. Slow, low one means stay clear.
{npc_name_2} mentioned it first and now I can't unhear it. It's like a weather forecast.
reactions: Useful, honestly. Saves me from walking into a bad conversation.

statCheck.pass: There's a third tune I've only heard once: very slow, almost a dirge. I think that's the one to really worry about.
statCheck.fail: I couldn't tell you what the different tunes mean exactly. Just a feeling.

entries.intro: {npc_name} stayed to help me clean up after the communal dinner while everyone else wandered off.
details: Didn't complain, didn't rush. We just worked through the pile together.
We talked about nothing in particular while washing up. It was a good end to the evening.
reactions: The people who stay to clean are the people who actually care about the place.

entries.intro: {npc_name} acts like every shared decision needs their approval before it can proceed.
details: We agreed as a group to move the woodpile. {npc_name} spent twenty minutes explaining why it should stay put.
It's not about the issue. It's about being the one who gets to say yes or no.
reactions: I've started making decisions without announcing them. Faster that way.

statCheck.pass: I think it comes from a fear of being left out. If they're not consulted, they feel invisible.
statCheck.fail: I don't know what drives it. I just know it slows everything down.

entries.intro: I thought {npc_name} was strange at first. Now I think they might be the most interesting person here.
details: They see things differently than the rest of us. Not wrong: just at an angle nobody else thinks to look from.
Once you get past the surface, their observations are surprisingly sharp.
reactions: I've learned to listen when {npc_name} speaks up, even if the phrasing is odd.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} once said something so sharp about a situation that I realized they understand more about people than they let on.
statCheck.fail: I can't point to anything specific. Just a growing sense that they see more than most.

entries.intro: {npc_name} never closes the gate behind them. The animals get out every time because of it.
details: I've put a sign up, reminded them in person, and stood right there pointing at the gate. Nothing works.
The next morning: gate open, chickens in the road. It's like talking to the wind.
reactions: I've accepted that my mornings now include a chicken roundup.

entries.intro: {npc_name} and I disagree about nearly everything. But they're fair about it, and I respect that.
details: We had a long back-and-forth about water usage last week. Neither budged, but neither got mean about it.
At the end they said 'I see your point even if I don't agree.' That's more than most people offer.
reactions: You don't have to agree with someone to get along with them.

entries.intro: {npc_name} taps their foot when they're impatient. {npc_name_2} started doing it too, like it's contagious.
details: I was explaining something the other day and both of them were tapping. Synchronized impatience.
It's distracting enough with one person. Two is unbearable.
reactions: I've started keeping my explanations short. Probably for the best, honestly.

entries.intro: {npc_name} apologized to me today for something that happened weeks ago. I'd already let it go.
details: They'd clearly been carrying it around. You could see the relief once the words were out.
It wasn't a big thing to begin with. But the fact that it mattered to them says a lot.
reactions: People who circle back to make things right are rare. I'll remember that.

statCheck.pass: I think {npc_name} apologized because they'd been losing sleep over it. The relief on their face told the whole story.
statCheck.fail: I'm glad they said something. The reason doesn't much matter to me.

entries.intro: {npc_name} takes the best portions at meals and doesn't think anyone notices.
details: Always first in line, always picking through for the choice pieces. Every single time.
The rest of us get what's left and nobody says anything because it feels too petty to bring up.
reactions: It is petty, but it's also true. And I notice every time.

statCheck.pass: I think {npc_name} grew up without enough. Habits like that come from somewhere real, even if they're frustrating now.
statCheck.fail: Maybe they just have a big appetite. I'm trying not to read too much into it.

entries.intro: I don't think I've ever seen {npc_name} sit still for more than a minute.
details: Always tinkering, adjusting, fixing something. Their hands are never idle.
Even during meals they're fiddling with a loose thread or straightening the dishes.
reactions: I find it energizing to watch. And a little exhausting.

statCheck.pass: I think the restlessness comes from anxiety, not energy. Their hands need to be busy or the worry creeps in.
statCheck.fail: Could just be a high-energy person. Not everyone can sit still.

entries.intro: {npc_name} lent me a blanket during the cold snap and told me to keep it as long as I needed.
details: I tried to return it the next day and they waved me off. Said they had another one.
Turned out later they didn't. They slept cold that night so I wouldn't.
reactions: That's the kind of person you want in your corner.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} does this more than people realize. Gives things away quietly and pretends they don't need them.
statCheck.fail: I didn't find out about the blanket until someone else told me. They'd never have said.

entries.intro: {npc_name} has a way of making you feel guilty without saying a single accusatory word.
details: It's all in the tone. This mild, disappointed calm that's worse than shouting would be.
I came late to help with a project once and the look alone had me apologizing before I'd put my tools down.
reactions: Effective, I'll give them that. But it wears on you.

entries.intro: {npc_name} and {npc_name_2} always end up on opposite sides of every discussion. Like clockwork.
details: If one says the sky is blue, the other will find a reason to argue it's closer to grey.
I've started betting with myself on how long it takes. The record is four seconds.
reactions: I think they'd both be bored without the other to disagree with.

entries.intro: {npc_name} notices when something's different about me. New patch on my coat, a limp, a haircut.
details: Most people walk right past without a glance. {npc_name} stops and says something.
It's not prying. Just 'new boots?' or 'you look well today.' Brief and genuine.
reactions: Being seen is a surprisingly nice feeling when you're used to being overlooked.

statCheck.pass: I think they do it because they know how it feels to go unnoticed. They're making up for what nobody did for them.
statCheck.fail: I don't know why they pay such close attention. Just glad someone does.

entries.intro: {npc_name} sneezes like they're trying to alert the entire settlement. It's startling every time.
details: I was carrying a full water pail yesterday when it happened behind me. Lost half of it.
They apologize after, always, but the damage is done and my nerves are shot.
reactions: I've started bracing myself whenever I see them wrinkle their nose.

entries.intro: {npc_name} shared something they'd been working on in private. It was genuinely beautiful.
details: I had no idea they had that in them. They'd been keeping it to themselves for months.
There was this nervous look on their face when they showed me, like they expected criticism.
reactions: I told them it was wonderful and I meant every word. Some people hide the best parts of themselves.

statCheck.pass: I think they've been hurt before by sharing something personal and having it dismissed. That's why the nervousness.
statCheck.fail: I don't know why they kept it hidden. Shyness, maybe.

entries.intro: {npc_name} forgets names constantly. Called me the wrong name twice last week.
details: I don't take it personally anymore: they do it to everyone. I've heard them get {npc_name_2}'s name wrong too.
The odd thing is they remember every other detail about you: what you said, what you were doing. Just not the name.
reactions: I've started answering to whatever they call me. Easier for everyone.

entries.intro: {npc_name} is the first person to laugh at their own mistakes. Loudly and publicly.
details: Dropped an entire basket of supplies the other day and was laughing before it hit the ground.
It disarms people. Hard to be annoyed at someone who's already making fun of themselves.
reactions: I wish I could handle my own mistakes that gracefully.

statCheck.pass: I think laughing at their own mistakes is how they keep from being hard on themselves. A defense that doubles as charm.
statCheck.fail: I don't know if it's natural or learned. It works either way.

entries.intro: {npc_name} always seems to need something from me. There's never just a hello.
details: Every conversation starts friendly and then pivots to a request. I can feel it coming now.
I started walking faster when I see them heading my way. Terrible, but it's a reflex at this point.
reactions: I don't mind helping, but I'd like to be talked to, not talked at with a purpose.

entries.intro: {npc_name} keeps a list of everyone's preferred meals. Checked it the last time they cooked for us.
details: I don't even remember telling them what I liked. But there it was, exactly right.
That kind of thoughtfulness isn't showy. It's just quiet care, the hardest kind to fake.
reactions: I should return the favor. I don't even know what {npc_name} prefers.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} keeps notes about everyone: preferences, dislikes, allergies. I saw the list once and it's remarkably detailed.
statCheck.fail: I don't know how they keep track of it all. Impressive memory, I suppose.

entries.intro: {npc_name} has a way of making everything about themselves. Even your good news.
details: I mentioned finishing a project I was proud of and somehow it became about a similar project they did years ago.
I don't think they're trying to be rude. But the redirect happens every single time.
reactions: I've stopped sharing things with them. It's easier to just nod and listen.

entries.intro: {npc_name} checked in on me every day for a week after I hurt my ankle. Just a knock and a word.
details: Never lingered, never made it into a whole thing. Just made sure I had what I needed.
On the last day they left a walking stick by the door with a bow tied on it.
reactions: Consistent kindness is the kind that actually matters.

entries.intro: {npc_name} always has to be the expert. Even on things they clearly know nothing about.
details: We were talking about roof repairs and they chimed in with advice that would have collapsed the whole thing.
When I pointed out the problem, they doubled down. That's the part that gets me.
reactions: I've learned when to argue and when to just let it go. Life's too short.

statCheck.pass: I think it's insecurity. They'd rather be wrong loudly than admit they don't know something.
statCheck.fail: Maybe they genuinely believe what they're saying. Either way, I don't listen anymore.

entries.intro: {npc_name} asked me how I really was the other day. Not the usual greeting: actually meant it.
details: I started to give the standard 'fine' and they just waited, like they knew there was more.
I ended up telling them about the week I'd been having. Felt lighter afterward.
reactions: Good listeners are rarer than good talkers.

statCheck.pass: I think {npc_name} asks because they've needed someone to really listen to them and nobody did. So they became that person for others.
statCheck.fail: I don't know what makes them such a good listener. Some people just are.

entries.intro: {npc_name} has no volume control. Everything they say sounds like an announcement.
details: I told them something private once and half the path overheard the response.
It's not aggression. They just talk at the volume some people save for open fields.
reactions: I've adjusted. Private conversations happen indoors only now.

entries.intro: {npc_name} and {npc_name_2} have this easy, effortless friendship I'm a little envious of.
details: They don't finish each other's thoughts or anything dramatic. They just seem comfortable.
I've watched them work side by side in complete silence for an hour, both perfectly content.
reactions: That kind of ease takes time, or maybe the right two people. Hard to say which.

entries.intro: {npc_name} sharpens their tools obsessively every evening. {npc_name_2} finally asked them to stop.
details: Scrape, scrape, scrape: sometimes for an hour straight. {npc_name_2} lives closest and bears the worst of it.
{npc_name} agreed to do it earlier in the day. That lasted exactly one evening.
reactions: I've gotten used to it. The way you get used to any persistent noise: reluctantly.

entries.intro: I've been watching {npc_name} and I think they do more for this place than anyone realizes.
details: Little things: a loose board nailed down, a leaky barrel patched, weeds pulled from the common paths.
Never mentions it, never takes credit. You just notice the place looking better and eventually trace it back to them.
reactions: I mentioned it to them once and they just shrugged. That's how you know it's genuine.

statCheck.pass: I've been paying attention: {npc_name} does at least three maintenance tasks a day that aren't assigned to anyone. Completely self-directed.
statCheck.fail: I can't say for sure how much they do. I just notice the results.

entries.intro: {npc_name} is the type who shows up uninvited and stays longer than anyone wanted.
details: We were having a quiet evening with a few neighbors and {npc_name} appeared, sat down, and settled in for the night.
Nobody had the heart to say anything. So we all went to bed two hours late.
reactions: I've started locking the door when I want a quiet night. It's come to that.

entries.intro: {npc_name} is the only person who laughs at my terrible jokes. I love them for it.
details: Everyone else groans or walks away, but {npc_name} actually laughs. Might be pity, but I'll take it.
There's something freeing about having at least one person who appreciates your worst material.
reactions: I've started saving my worst ones specifically for them.

entries.intro: {npc_name} takes forever to say goodbye. What should take a moment turns into a whole event.
details: You stand up to leave and suddenly there's three more topics, a detour, and a final thought.
I've started timing it. The record is twenty minutes from 'well, I should go' to actually going.
reactions: I think they just don't want the conversation to end, which is sweet and also exhausting.

statCheck.pass: I think they dread the quiet that comes after someone leaves. The long goodbye is them pushing that moment back.
statCheck.fail: Maybe they just enjoy talking. Not the worst trait in the world.

entries.intro: {npc_name} brought me my favorite kind of bread out of nowhere. Said they'd been experimenting.
details: The fact that they knew it was my favorite without me ever saying so: that's what got me.
It was good too. Better than what I make myself, and I'll deny saying that if asked.
reactions: Thoughtful gestures don't need to be grand. They just need to be right.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} once mentioned paying close attention to what people eat at gatherings. That's how they figured out my favorite.
statCheck.fail: I have no idea how they knew. It was a lucky guess or something more.

entries.intro: {npc_name} has this habit of giving you a task and then hovering while you do it.
details: Asked me to sort some supplies and then stood three feet away watching the entire time.
Every few minutes: 'You might want to try it this way.' I know how to sort crates.
reactions: I'd rather do it wrong alone than right with someone breathing down my neck.

entries.intro: {npc_name} calls everyone 'friend.' I used to think it was generic until I realized they mean it.
details: They use it the way other people use your name: naturally, without thinking about it.
I tested it once by being a bit rude. They still called me 'friend.' Not a flinch.
reactions: I think some people decide to be warm and then just are. Must be nice.

entries.intro: I think {npc_name} might be avoiding work on purpose. The timing of their disappearances is too precise.
details: Every time something heavy needs doing, they've just left to 'check on something.' Always urgent, always vague.
I mentioned it to someone else and they said the same thing. So it's not just me.
reactions: I'm not going to confront them, but I notice and I keep track.

statCheck.pass: I asked around. At least three people have seen {npc_name} just sitting behind the storehouse during work calls.
statCheck.fail: Maybe they really are busy with other things. I can't be sure.

entries.intro: {npc_name} and I don't see each other often, but when we do it's always pleasant.
details: No drama, no catching up on gossip. Just a genuine 'how are things?' and an honest answer.
Some friendships don't need frequency. They just need sincerity.
reactions: I'm glad {npc_name} is around, even if we're not close.

statCheck.pass: I think {npc_name} is careful with closeness because they've lost people before. The distance is protective, not cold.
statCheck.fail: I don't read into it. Not every relationship needs to be deep.

entries.intro: {npc_name} picks at food like a bird. Takes an hour to eat what I'd finish in five minutes.
details: Little bites, long pauses. You'd think the plate was a puzzle they were solving.
Mealtimes with them take twice as long because nobody wants to be the one to get up first.
reactions: It's a minor thing. But when you're hungry and waiting, it feels major.

statCheck.pass: I think eating slowly is {npc_name}'s way of savoring things. They told me once that meals are the only time they feel no pressure.
statCheck.fail: Could be they're just a slow eater. Some people are.

entries.intro: {npc_name} stuck up for me during a disagreement even though it cost them an ally.
details: They sided with me knowing it wouldn't be popular. That's not a small thing around here.
Afterward they just said 'you were right' and walked off. No drama, no speeches.
reactions: That kind of loyalty isn't something you forget.

statCheck.pass: I found out later it cost them a closer friendship with someone else. They chose my side knowing what it would mean.
statCheck.fail: I don't know the full picture. Just that they stood up when it counted.

entries.intro: {npc_name} leaves wet boots by the communal fire and {npc_name_2} keeps moving them. Neither has said a word to the other.
details: Every morning {npc_name} puts them there, and every afternoon {npc_name_2} moves them outside. It's been going on for weeks.
The rest of us just watch. Nobody wants to be the one to break the silence on it.
reactions: I sit on the other side of the fire now. Upwind.

entries.intro: {npc_name} says 'good morning' to absolutely everyone. Even when it's clearly not a good morning.
details: Pouring rain, freezing wind, mud everywhere: 'good morning!' Same tone, same warmth.
I've tried being grumpy back and it has no effect. The cheerfulness is impenetrable.
reactions: I'd find it annoying if it weren't so obviously genuine.

entries.intro: {npc_name} asked to borrow my rope three weeks ago. Even {npc_name_2} has noticed I'm still waiting.
details: I've hinted twice and {npc_name_2} tried hinting on my behalf too. Nothing.
It's good rope, hard to replace. But apparently that doesn't register.
reactions: I'll ask directly next time I see them. No more hints.

entries.intro: I think {npc_name} worries too much about what everyone else thinks.
details: They asked me three times if their contribution to the communal work was good enough. It was fine the first time.
I wish they could see themselves the way the rest of us do. They'd worry a lot less.
reactions: Confidence doesn't come from other people telling you you're doing well. But I try to tell them anyway.

statCheck.pass: I think someone important to them was very critical when they were young. The worry comes from a voice that isn't even here anymore.
statCheck.fail: I don't know why they doubt themselves. They have no reason to.

entries.intro: {npc_name} makes everything they touch look better. Doesn't matter what it is.
details: They reorganized the tool storage last week and now I can actually find things. Revolutionary.
It's not fussy either. Just sensible changes that make you wonder why nobody thought of them before.
reactions: Some people see the world in terms of how it could be improved. I admire that.

entries.intro: {npc_name} treats communal supplies like their personal stash. Nobody else does this.
details: I saw them take an extra share of lamp oil last week without marking it down.
It's not that we can't afford it. It's the assumption that they're entitled and the rest of us should just cope.
reactions: One of these days someone's going to say something. I hope it's not me.

statCheck.pass: I've been quietly tracking it. Over the last month, {npc_name} has taken roughly double what anyone else does.
statCheck.fail: I might be paying more attention to it than I should. Could be everyone does it and I only notice when {npc_name} does.

entries.intro: {npc_name} talks to their animals like they're people. Full sentences, polite requests.
details: I heard them ask a chicken to please move out of the way yesterday. Said 'excuse me' and everything.
The strange part is the animals seem to respond better to them than to anyone else around here.
reactions: I tried it with my animals but it didn't work. Maybe it's the voice.

statCheck.pass: I think it calms them down. {npc_name} gets anxious around animals and the talking is how they manage it.
statCheck.fail: I have no idea why they do it. Just a quirk.

entries.intro: {npc_name} and {npc_name_2} have been working on something together. Won't tell anyone what it is.
details: They go quiet whenever someone walks by. Lots of whispering and conspiratorial looks.
I'm not worried about it. If anything, it's nice to see them collaborating.
reactions: I'll find out eventually. Secrets don't stay secret long around here.

statCheck.pass: I think it might be something for the communal gathering. {npc_name_2} was asking about decorations the other day.
statCheck.fail: Your guess is as good as mine. I'm staying out of it.

entries.intro: {npc_name} walked an extra mile with me just because I mentioned not wanting to go alone.
details: They had nowhere to be in that direction, I could tell. But they came anyway.
We didn't even talk much, just walked. That was enough.
reactions: Actions like that cost time and gain nothing. That's how you know they're real.

entries.intro: {npc_name} chews their lip when they're lying. I've confirmed it enough times to be certain.
details: I asked who ate the last of the smoked fish and there it was: the lip chew. Dead giveaway.
I almost feel bad knowing about it. Like I've found a key to a door I shouldn't open.
reactions: I haven't told them. Knowing someone's tell is useful, and revealing it is just cruel.

entries.intro: {npc_name} insists on doing everything the hard way. {npc_name_2} and I showed them a shortcut and they refused.
details: {npc_name_2} and I finished our fences in half the time. {npc_name} took three times as long doing it 'the proper way.'
Stubborn, yes. But I'll admit their work holds up longer than ours does.
reactions: I respect it even when it drives me up the wall.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} once showed me something they'd built years ago, still standing. Said 'that's why I do it the hard way.' Fair point.
statCheck.fail: I couldn't say if the extra time is worth it. Results speak for themselves, I suppose.

entries.intro: {npc_name} gave me a warning about a loose step on the common path. Saved me a nasty fall.
details: They'd already fixed it by the time I went back to look. Just mentioned it so I'd be careful in the meantime.
That kind of unprompted attentiveness: you can't teach it. You either have it or you don't.
reactions: I thanked them and meant it. Small things save big problems.

entries.intro: {npc_name} showed up to the communal meal with nothing to contribute and no apology.
details: Everyone else brought something, even just bread or a few herbs. {npc_name} came empty-handed and ate well.
It's happened before. I'm starting to see a pattern.
reactions: I don't mind sharing. But I do mind when it only goes one direction.

entries.intro: {npc_name} has the most infectious enthusiasm I've seen. They get excited about the smallest things.
details: Found a particularly round stone yesterday and showed it to three people. Genuinely delighted.
It's impossible not to smile around them when they get like that.
reactions: The world could use more people who find joy in the small stuff.

statCheck.pass: I think {npc_name} chooses to be enthusiastic. They told me once that they decided to start noticing good things on purpose.
statCheck.fail: I don't know where the energy comes from. Natural optimism, maybe.

entries.intro: {npc_name} criticizes how others spend their free time like it's any of their business.
details: I was sitting in the sun for ten minutes and got a comment about how that time could be better used.
I didn't realize rest required justification. Apparently to {npc_name} it does.
reactions: I rest when I please and I don't need a lecture about productivity.

entries.intro: I don't know what I'd do without {npc_name} around here. They make the hard days bearable.
details: Not through big gestures. Just by being present and steady when things feel chaotic.
There are people who make a place worse by leaving. {npc_name} is one of the ones who'd leave a hole.
reactions: I should tell them that sometime. People don't hear it enough.

entries.intro: {npc_name} is weirdly territorial about the communal bench. I sat on it once and even {npc_name_2} warned me to move.
details: Apparently they've been sitting there every evening for years. {npc_name_2} told me the history before {npc_name} could.
It's a bench, outside, shared by everyone. But the whole settlement treats it as reserved.
reactions: I use the other bench now. Not worth the hassle.

entries.intro: {npc_name} keeps their word even when it would be easier not to. I've seen it firsthand.
details: They promised to help with a job that turned out much bigger than expected. Stayed until it was done anyway.
Didn't complain, didn't renegotiate. Just rolled up their sleeves and got through it.
reactions: You learn what someone's made of when the work is harder than the promise.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} told me they never break a promise because someone they cared about broke one to them. Changed how they see commitment.
statCheck.fail: I don't know what drives it: integrity, I suppose. The old-fashioned kind.

entries.intro: {npc_name} and {npc_name_2} seem to communicate without words sometimes. A look, a nod, and they're moving.
details: I was working alongside them the other day and they coordinated a whole task in glances.
Makes the rest of us feel clumsy by comparison.
reactions: That kind of understanding takes years. Or a particular kind of trust.

entries.intro: {npc_name} hangs laundry at the worst possible angles. Blocks the path every time.
details: I've walked face-first into a wet shirt twice this week. Twice.
There's plenty of room to hang things off to the side. But somehow the path is always the chosen spot.
reactions: I'm taking a different route until laundry day is over.

entries.intro: {npc_name} sends their regards even when they can't visit. Always through someone, always specific.
details: Got a message yesterday through a neighbor: '{npc_name} hopes your knee is better.' They remembered.
It takes effort to keep track of people like that. Most of us don't bother.
reactions: I never feel forgotten when {npc_name} is around, even when they're not.

entries.intro: {npc_name} brings up old disagreements at the worst times. Nothing ever truly stays settled.
details: We resolved the tool-sharing issue months ago, or so I thought. It came back up over dinner last week.
I think they keep a mental ledger of every time they felt wronged. Must be exhausting to carry.
reactions: I've stopped engaging. A shrug and a subject change: that's my new approach.

entries.intro: {npc_name} leaves notes for people. Little things tucked under doors or left on workbenches.
details: I found one on my bench yesterday: 'Nice work on the gate.' Four words. Made my afternoon.
Not everyone gets them. It's like a quiet recognition that you can't buy or earn on purpose.
reactions: I keep mine. I don't know if anyone else does, but I keep mine.

entries.intro: {npc_name} cleared their throat seventeen times during the last meeting. I counted.
details: Not sick, not speaking: just clearing their throat into the silence at regular intervals.
The person next to me started mouthing the count after the tenth one. We're losing our minds.
reactions: I sat farther away this time. Helped a little, but not enough.

entries.intro: {npc_name} gives feedback the way you'd want it: honest, private, and without making you feel small.
details: They pulled me aside after a job I'd botched and walked me through what went wrong. Calmly, clearly.
Didn't bring it up again after that. Didn't mention it to anyone else.
reactions: That's leadership whether they'd use the word or not.

entries.intro: {npc_name} paces when they're anxious. Back and forth, same stretch of path, for ages.
details: I can tell from my window when something's bothering them. The path in front of their door is worn smoother than anywhere else.
I offered to talk once during one of those pacing sessions. They said they were 'just stretching their legs.' At midnight.
reactions: I leave them to it now. Some people work through things by moving.

statCheck.pass: They let it slip once that the pacing started after they lost someone close. It's how they think through things they can't say out loud.
statCheck.fail: I don't know what's on their mind during those walks. Probably best left alone.

entries.intro: {npc_name} tried to reorganize the communal kitchen and it caused more arguments than the old arrangement ever did.
details: They meant well, genuinely. But when you move everything without asking, people get territorial.
Three people have put things back where they were. {npc_name} keeps moving them again.
reactions: At this point it's a silent war and I'm staying out of it.

entries.intro: {npc_name} is the best storyteller I've ever heard. Even the dull stuff sounds interesting coming from them.
details: They told me about fixing a cart wheel the other day and I was genuinely riveted. It's the pauses, the timing.
I think they could describe watching paint dry and I'd ask for the sequel.
reactions: I always make time when {npc_name} starts talking. Always worth it.

statCheck.pass: I think they practice. I heard them telling the same story to a fence post once, trying different pauses.
statCheck.fail: I don't know if it's natural talent or work. Either way, it lands.

entries.intro: {npc_name} never admits when they're wrong. {npc_name_2} had the exact same experience last month.
details: We had a disagreement about how much rope a job needed, and I was right. The extra pile is still sitting there.
{npc_name_2} told me they've given up trying to get an acknowledgment. Apparently it's a pattern.
reactions: I don't need a grand apology. Just a nod or a shrug: something.

entries.intro: {npc_name} always seems to have exactly what you need. Thread, a spare nail, a bit of twine.
details: I've asked three times for something obscure and every time they've pulled it from somewhere on their person.
I'm starting to think they carry the entire storehouse in their pockets.
reactions: Handy to know. I've stopped going to the supply shed and just go to {npc_name} instead.

statCheck.pass: I peeked once and they've got pockets sewn into places I didn't know pockets could go. It's practically an art form.
statCheck.fail: I have no idea where they keep it all. Must be a knack.

entries.intro: {npc_name} and {npc_name_2} keep comparing their gardens. It's become an unspoken contest.
details: Every morning one of them peers over at the other's plot. They think nobody notices.
I saw {npc_name} measuring their tallest plant against {npc_name_2}'s with a piece of string. Completely serious.
reactions: It's harmless. And honestly, both gardens look better for the rivalry.

entries.intro: {npc_name} caught me struggling with a stuck lid and opened it without a word. Just handed it back.
details: Didn't laugh, didn't make it a thing. Just fixed it and kept walking.
It's the kind of small dignity you don't forget: someone could have made a joke, but they chose not to.
reactions: Grace in the small moments. I try to do the same for others.

entries.intro: {npc_name} always has to one-up everything. {npc_name_2} and I tested it once on purpose.
details: {npc_name_2} mentioned catching a big fish and within seconds {npc_name} was describing one twice the size.
We looked at each other and tried not to laugh. The pattern is flawless.
reactions: I've started keeping my small victories to myself. It's simpler that way.

entries.intro: I was in a foul mood the other day and {npc_name} just sat near me without trying to fix it.
details: Didn't ask what was wrong, didn't try to cheer me up. Just sat there whittling.
After a while I started feeling better anyway. Turns out company is sometimes enough.
reactions: Most people try too hard to help. {npc_name} understood that presence was the thing I needed.

statCheck.pass: They told me later that someone did the same for them once, during a hard time. They were just passing it along.
statCheck.fail: I don't know what made them decide to stay. Instinct, maybe.

entries.intro: {npc_name} is the worst at keeping time. {npc_name_2} warned me about it and they were absolutely right.
details: I agreed to wait for them once. 'A moment' turned into me standing there watching the sun move.
{npc_name_2} said they now add an hour to any timeline {npc_name} gives. Apparently it's accurate.
reactions: I now add an hour to any timeline {npc_name} gives me. It's accurate so far.

entries.intro: {npc_name} takes the same walk every evening just before dark. Rain or shine.
details: I asked about it once. They said it was 'the only quiet part of the day.' I can understand that.
Sometimes I see them stop and just look at the sky for a while before heading home.
reactions: Everyone needs their ritual. That one seems like a good one.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} told me once the walk is when they talk to someone they've lost. Just a quiet conversation with the sky.
statCheck.fail: I don't know what the walk is really about. Probably just a habit.

entries.intro: {npc_name} and {npc_name_2} bicker like they've been married for thirty years. It's relentless.
details: Who left the gate open, whose turn it was to sweep, which way the wind was blowing: all of it is an argument.
And then an hour later they're sharing a meal like nothing happened. Every time.
reactions: I think the bickering IS the friendship. Take it away and they'd miss it.

entries.intro: {npc_name} sharpened my axe and left it on my doorstep. I didn't even know it was dull.
details: Used it the next day and the difference was night and day. Like a brand new tool.
They must have taken it while I was out. Returned it before I noticed.
reactions: I've started checking for other things {npc_name} might have quietly fixed. Found two.

statCheck.pass: I asked a neighbor and apparently {npc_name} does this for several people. Quiet maintenance on everyone's behalf.
statCheck.fail: I don't know who else they help. But I wouldn't be surprised if it's more than just me.

entries.intro: {npc_name} refuses to ever be the first to apologize. {npc_name_2} has been on the receiving end of that too.
details: We both had words with {npc_name} at different times and neither of us has gotten a sorry yet.
{npc_name_2} said they've accepted it, but I haven't. It's the principle.
reactions: Pride's an expensive habit. It costs more than people think.

entries.intro: {npc_name} cracks a joke when tension's building and it always works. Every time.
details: There was a real argument brewing at the last gathering and they said one thing: the whole mood shifted.
It's never mean-spirited either. Just the right observation at the right moment.
reactions: That's a skill you can't practice. You either have the instinct or you don't.

statCheck.pass: I think {npc_name} reads a room faster than anyone I know. They see the tension before it peaks and act.
statCheck.fail: I don't know how they do it. Timing like that might just be instinct.

entries.intro: {npc_name} peels fruit so slowly it borders on performance art. I've watched in disbelief.
details: One continuous strip of peel, not a single break. I timed it once: nearly five minutes for a single apple.
They're proud of it too. Held up the finished peel like a trophy.
reactions: I don't understand it. But I respect the commitment.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} told me it was something their grandmother did. They keep the tradition going without really knowing why.
statCheck.fail: I have no idea where the habit came from. Just one of those things.

entries.intro: {npc_name} invited me to eat with them when I was clearly having a bad day. Didn't pry into why.
details: We just ate in comfortable quiet. They talked about normal things: the weather, what they were working on.
By the end of the meal I felt better, and I don't think I said more than twenty words the whole time.
reactions: That's what good company looks like: knowing when to just be there.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} mentioned later that they've been in that exact spot before: too low to talk, just needing someone near.
statCheck.fail: I don't know how they read the situation so well. Some people just can.

entries.intro: {npc_name} has started sighing heavily every time someone suggests a new way of doing things.
details: I proposed a different approach to sorting the storehouse and got a sigh so deep it could have moved clouds.
It's not that they resist change. It's that they want you to know how much the idea of change burdens them.
reactions: I've started proposing ideas when {npc_name} isn't in the room. Much smoother.

entries.intro: {npc_name} and {npc_name_2} do this thing where they check in on each other every morning. Without fail.
details: A quick stop by, a few words, and then they both go about their days. Takes less than a minute.
I've watched it happen every day for months now and they've never missed. Not once.
reactions: There's something quietly beautiful about that kind of consistency.

entries.intro: {npc_name} ate my clearly labeled meal from the communal storage. Didn't even pretend it was an accident.
details: I asked them about it and they said they 'thought it was for everyone.' My name was written on it, clearly.
Now I keep my food at home. It's less convenient, but at least it's still there when I want it.
reactions: I'm not angry anymore. I'm just never leaving my food in that storage again.

entries.intro: {npc_name} has this habit of humming while they work. Same tune, every day, without variation.
details: I don't think they realize they're doing it. It just starts up the moment their hands are busy.
I've caught myself humming it on my own now. That's when I knew it had gone too far.
reactions: It's oddly comforting once you stop fighting it.

statCheck.pass: Someone told me the tune is from a lullaby their mother used to sing. They probably don't even realize what it is.
statCheck.fail: No idea where the tune comes from. Probably nothing.

entries.intro: {npc_name} remembered the name of my dog who died years ago. Nobody remembers that.
details: Brought it up in passing like it was the most natural thing in the world. I nearly choked on my drink.
It's a small detail, but it tells you everything about how they listen.
reactions: I think about that moment more than I probably should.

entries.intro: {npc_name} never waves back. I've tried a dozen times.
details: I wave, they look at me, and then they just keep walking. Every single time.
I asked around and apparently it's not personal. They just don't wave.
reactions: I still wave. It's become a personal challenge at this point.

statCheck.pass: I heard {npc_name} grew up somewhere isolated, where greetings weren't a custom. The not-waving isn't rudeness, it's just how they were raised.
statCheck.fail: Could be they're just not a wave-back person. Wouldn't know.

entries.intro: {npc_name} told me I looked tired the other day. I wasn't tired until they said that.
details: Something about someone pointing it out just makes it true. I went home and napped.
They probably meant well. But I'd rather not hear it.
reactions: I've started avoiding {npc_name} on mornings when I haven't slept well.

entries.intro: {npc_name} and {npc_name_2} finish each other's sentences. It should be endearing but it's mostly unsettling.
details: I was having a conversation with {npc_name} and {npc_name_2} completed the thought from across the room.
They didn't even make eye contact when it happened. Like it was automatic.
reactions: I don't know if that's closeness or something stranger. Either way, I noticed.

entries.intro: {npc_name} always smells like wood smoke. Not unpleasant, just constant.
details: I can tell where they've been sitting because the bench still smells like a campfire an hour later.
I mentioned it once and they looked confused. Apparently they can't smell it on themselves.
reactions: You get used to it. I actually associate the smell with good company now.

entries.intro: {npc_name} lent me their coat without being asked when the weather turned. Just draped it over my shoulders.
details: I started to protest and they waved me off like it was nothing. They walked home in the cold.
The coat smelled like their home: warm, a bit smoky. I gave it back clean the next day.
reactions: That kind of automatic generosity is harder to find than people think.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} told me once they were cold for a long time before coming here. Being cold doesn't bother them anymore, but watching someone else shiver does.
statCheck.fail: I don't know why they're so quick to give. Just seems to be their way.

entries.intro: {npc_name} interrupts constantly. I can't get through a single thought without them jumping in.
details: I counted four interruptions in one conversation yesterday. Four. And none of them were urgent.
The worst part is they seem to think they're helping: finishing my point before I've made it.
reactions: I've started holding up a finger when I'm talking. Seems to help, barely.

statCheck.pass: I think {npc_name} grew up in a loud family where you had to talk over people or not be heard at all.
statCheck.fail: I don't know why they do it. Just wired that way, I suppose.

entries.intro: {npc_name} can fix almost anything. I've never seen them stumped by a broken object.
details: Brought them a hinge that was completely rusted shut and they had it moving in under an hour.
They don't make a production of it either. Just quiet competence.
reactions: There's something reassuring about knowing {npc_name} is around when things break.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} told me they taught themselves because they couldn't afford to replace things growing up. Every repair was necessity.
statCheck.fail: I don't know where they learned it. Natural talent or years of practice: either way, impressive.

entries.intro: {npc_name} talks about the weather like it's a personal insult directed at them specifically.
details: Rain isn't just rain: it's the sky conspiring to ruin their plans. Wind is a targeted attack.
I tried agreeing that it was windy once and they corrected me. 'This isn't windy. This is hostile.'
