reactions: I've learned not to bring up weather near {npc_name}. It opens a door you can't close.

entries.intro: {npc_name} and {npc_name_2} split firewood together every week. Neither of them needs to, but they do it anyway.
details: They've got a rhythm to it: one splits, one stacks, and they switch halfway through without a word.
I watched them once and it looked more like a routine than a chore. Comfortable, practiced.
reactions: Some friendships are built on shared work more than shared words.

entries.intro: {npc_name} never closes the communal gate behind them. I've lost count of the times I've had to do it.
details: I've mentioned it politely, then less politely, and now I just close it in defeated silence.
It takes three seconds. I know because I've timed it while gritting my teeth.
reactions: Small things shouldn't bother me this much. But here we are.

statCheck.pass: I think they genuinely don't notice. Their mind's always somewhere else when they pass through. The gate doesn't register.
statCheck.fail: I can't explain it. Maybe they grew up without gates.

entries.intro: {npc_name} gave me the best piece of advice I've ever gotten. Just one sentence.
details: They said 'stop deciding what other people think of you.' I've been turning it over in my head ever since.
Didn't elaborate, didn't follow up. Just dropped it into conversation and moved on.
reactions: Simple things hit hardest when they come from someone who means them.

entries.intro: {npc_name} eats the loudest of anyone I've ever shared a meal with. It's remarkable.
details: Soup sounds like a waterfall. Bread sounds like someone walking on gravel.
I've started timing my own bites to match theirs so I can't hear it over my own chewing.
reactions: I eat earlier now. It's just easier that way.

statCheck.pass: I think they can't hear themselves doing it. {npc_name} mentioned once that sounds seem quieter to them than to other people.
statCheck.fail: Could just be how they eat. Not everyone notices their own noise.

entries.intro: I asked {npc_name} how they were doing and they gave me an honest answer. I mean genuinely honest.
details: Not 'fine,' not 'getting by.' They told me exactly what was hard and what was good, in about thirty seconds.
I wasn't prepared for it. Most people deflect. {npc_name} just answered the question.
reactions: I respect that more than I can say. Honest people are rare.

statCheck.pass: I think {npc_name} decided a while back to stop pretending. They told me once that they got tired of performing 'fine' for everyone.
statCheck.fail: I don't know what made them so direct. It's just who they are.

entries.intro: {npc_name} stands too close when they talk to you. I've backed up and they've followed without noticing.
details: Had a conversation yesterday that was basically a slow walk backward across the entire common area.
I don't think they're trying to be aggressive. They just have no sense of personal distance.
reactions: I've started talking to them from the other side of a table when possible.

entries.intro: {npc_name} has a laugh that carries across the whole settlement. You can always tell when they're happy.
details: I was indoors with the door shut and still heard it clear as day. Unmistakable.
It's not obnoxious, just loud. The kind of laugh that makes you want to know the joke.
reactions: I like hearing it, honestly. Means someone's having a good time.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} told me once the laugh used to embarrass them. Then they decided they'd rather be loud and happy than quiet and careful.
statCheck.fail: I don't think they can control it. Some laughs are just built big.

entries.intro: {npc_name} apologized for something so minor I'd already forgotten about it. That says a lot about them.
details: They bumped my arm three days ago and brought it up like it had been weighing on them.
The apology was longer and more heartfelt than the bump warranted, but I appreciated it.
reactions: People who apologize for small things tend to be careful about the big ones too.

entries.intro: {npc_name} snores. I know this because I can hear it from next door.
details: I've tried sleeping on the other side of my room. Doesn't help.
The worst part is they deny it completely. Absolute refusal to acknowledge the evidence.
reactions: I've made peace with it. Sort of. The wall between us is doing its best.

statCheck.pass: I mentioned it to them once and they got genuinely upset. I think they're embarrassed but can't control it.
statCheck.fail: I don't bring it up. No point starting a fight over something they can't change.

entries.intro: {npc_name} saved me a seat at the last gathering without me asking. Just knew I'd want to sit there.
details: Had a coat draped over the spot and everything. Waved me over the moment I arrived.
It's the kind of thoughtfulness that doesn't announce itself. You just notice it after the fact.
reactions: I'm going to start doing the same for them. Seems like a good habit to pick up.

entries.intro: {npc_name} and {npc_name_2} had a disagreement last week and now they're communicating through me.
details: {npc_name} says 'tell {npc_name_2} the firewood is low' while standing three paces from them.
I've asked both of them to just talk directly. Neither will budge.
reactions: I'm not a messenger service. But apparently today I am.

entries.intro: {npc_name} keeps a tidy workspace. Everything in its place, tools lined up, not a scrap on the ground.
details: I left a mug on their bench once and it was cleaned and returned within the hour.
It's not fussiness exactly. More like they can't think clearly unless things are ordered.
reactions: I wish some of that would rub off on me, but it never does.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} let slip that the tidiness started after they lost everything once. Rebuilding from nothing makes you careful about what you keep.
statCheck.fail: Some people are just tidy. I don't think it goes deeper than that.

entries.intro: {npc_name} told me something in confidence last week. I haven't told a soul and I won't.
details: It wasn't anything dramatic, just personal. The kind of thing you only share when you trust someone.
The fact that they chose me to tell: that means something.
reactions: Trust is built in small moments like that. I won't waste it.

entries.intro: {npc_name} whistles the same three notes over and over. I can't get them out of my head.
details: I asked what the tune was and they said they don't know. They've just always done it.
I've started hearing it when they're not even around. It lives in my head now, rent-free.
reactions: I've accepted it as the background music of my life here.

statCheck.pass: I found out from a neighbor that those three notes are from a song {npc_name} used to sing with someone who's gone now. The melody is all that's left.
statCheck.fail: Probably just a tune that got stuck. Happens to everyone.

entries.intro: {npc_name} always offers to carry the heavy end. Every time, no exceptions.
details: Even when they're clearly tired, they grab the bigger share and start walking before you can argue.
I've tried insisting on taking the heavy end and they physically steer me to the lighter side.
reactions: Stubbornly generous. That's the only way I can describe it.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} hurt their back years ago from exactly this habit. They know it costs them and they still do it.
statCheck.fail: I don't know if it's pride or kindness. Probably both.

entries.intro: {npc_name} gave me a look yesterday that could have curdled milk. I still don't know what I did.
details: I replayed the entire day in my head trying to figure it out. Nothing.
I saw them be perfectly pleasant to {npc_name_2} an hour later. So it was definitely aimed at me.
reactions: I'm not going to ask. Some things are better left unexplored.

statCheck.pass: I think I accidentally used their favorite mug. I saw it sitting by my workstation afterward. Didn't realize it was claimed.
statCheck.fail: Could be nothing. Some people just have a sharp resting face.

entries.intro: {npc_name} makes the worst tea I've ever had but serves it with such pride that I drink every cup.
details: It's bitter, it's murky, and I'm fairly sure they oversteep it on purpose because they think that's how it should taste.
I've seen other people accept a cup and then pour it out when {npc_name} isn't looking.
reactions: I drink it. Every time. The gesture matters more than the taste.

statCheck.pass: I found out the recipe was their mother's. That's why they make it exactly that way: changing it would feel like letting go.
statCheck.fail: I don't question the recipe. It's clearly important to them.

entries.intro: {npc_name} covered my shift when I was sick without being asked and without telling anyone.
details: I didn't find out until I came back and someone mentioned it. They never said a word.
That's the kind of help that doesn't need an audience. Rare thing.
reactions: I owe them one. And I intend to pay it back the same way: quietly.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} told someone they do it because being sick and alone was the worst thing they ever went through. They won't let that happen to someone else.
statCheck.fail: They're just a helpful person. Not sure there's more to it.

entries.intro: {npc_name} always has an opinion on what I'm eating. Always.
details: I had a perfectly good meal yesterday and got a full commentary on what I should have done differently.
They once told me my bread was 'adequate.' Not good, not bad. Adequate.
reactions: I eat facing the other direction now. It's improved my meals considerably.

entries.intro: {npc_name} defended me in a conversation I wasn't part of. Someone told me about it afterward.
details: Apparently someone was being unfair about my work and {npc_name} set them straight. Calmly, but firmly.
They never mentioned it to me. I only know because a neighbor thought I should hear about it.
reactions: That's the kind of loyalty you can't repay. You can only try to match it.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} told me later they did it because they know what it's like to have nobody speak up for you. Said they promised themselves they'd be the one who does.
statCheck.fail: I don't know what motivated them. Just grateful they did.

entries.intro: You'd think the {npc_role} would be better at settling disputes around here.
details: Last disagreement I brought to them, they told both sides to 'work it out.' That's not settling, that's dodging.
I've had more productive conversations with a fence post.
reactions: I handle my own problems now. Faster and less frustrating.

statCheck.pass: I think they avoid taking sides because they're afraid of making enemies. Being liked matters more to them than being useful.
statCheck.fail: Maybe the job's harder than it looks. I wouldn't want it.

entries.intro: {npc_name} tells the same stories and somehow they get funnier every time.
details: I've heard the one about the stuck cart wheel at least six times. Still laughed last night.
They add a new detail each telling: a pause here, a face there. It's craft, not repetition.
reactions: I'd listen to the same ten stories from {npc_name} for the rest of my life and not complain.

entries.intro: {npc_name} and {npc_name_2} are so polite to each other it's actually uncomfortable.
details: 'After you.' 'No, after you.' 'I insist.' It takes them five minutes to get through a doorway.
I've started going around them when this happens. Life's too short to wait for the courtesy loop to end.
reactions: There's polite and then there's whatever that is.

statCheck.pass: I think there's tension underneath the politeness. Like if either one dropped the formality, something real would come out.
statCheck.fail: Maybe they're just extremely mannered people. Who am I to judge.

entries.intro: {npc_name} puts everyone else's needs before their own. I admire it, but it worries me.
details: They skipped their own meal to help someone finish a task last week. Second time this month.
I've tried telling them to sit down and eat. They wave it off like it doesn't matter.
reactions: Generosity shouldn't come at the cost of your own health.

statCheck.pass: I think they feel guilty resting when others are still working. Like they don't deserve to stop.
statCheck.fail: I don't know what drives them so hard. Maybe that's just their nature.

entries.intro: {npc_name} tracks mud everywhere they go. I could follow their path with my eyes closed.
details: From the gate to the storehouse to the fire pit: a perfect trail of boot prints.
I cleaned the floor two days ago and it was muddy again by noon. I know exactly whose boots did it.
reactions: I've put a scraper by the door. They haven't noticed it yet.

entries.intro: {npc_name} noticed I was struggling before I said anything and just showed up to help.
details: I was trying to move something too heavy for one person and there they were. No greeting, just grabbed the other end.
We finished in a few minutes and they went on their way. Like it was nothing.
reactions: The people who help without being asked are the ones worth keeping close.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} said once they watch for struggling because they spent too long being too proud to ask for help themselves.
statCheck.fail: Maybe they're just observant. Good quality either way.

entries.intro: {npc_name} critiques everyone else's cooking but won't cook a meal themselves.
details: I've heard opinions on salt levels, timing, texture: the full critique. Never seen them hold a ladle.
I offered them the kitchen once and they said they 'prefer to advise.' Advise.
reactions: Easy to have high standards when you're not the one doing the work.

statCheck.pass: I think they actually can't cook at all. Someone saw them burn water once and they've hidden behind criticism ever since.
statCheck.fail: Maybe they just have strong opinions. Doesn't mean they're wrong about the salt.

entries.intro: {npc_name} has this way of making everyone feel included, even the quiet ones.
details: At the last gathering they made a point of pulling me into a conversation I'd been hovering outside of.
They do it naturally: not forced, not performative. Just a 'what do you think?' aimed your way at the right moment.
reactions: I think they notice who's on the edges because they've been there themselves.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} told me they spent years feeling invisible in groups. Now they watch for the ones standing just outside the circle.
statCheck.fail: I don't know where that instinct comes from. Some people are just wired for it.

entries.intro: {npc_name} and {npc_name_2} have this unspoken rule about who gets the last piece of bread. It alternates.
details: I watched for a full week and the pattern is perfect. One day {npc_name} takes it, next day {npc_name_2}.
Neither of them has ever acknowledged this system. It just works.
reactions: I don't know how it started but I'm not going to be the one to disrupt it.

entries.intro: {npc_name} borrows tools and returns them in better condition than they left. Sharpened, oiled, wrapped.
details: I lent them a saw last week and it came back with a new handle wrap. I didn't ask for that.
People have started lending to {npc_name} on purpose just to get their things improved.
reactions: That's how you earn trust: by returning things better than you found them.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} mentioned they were taught that borrowing something is a promise to return it better. Apparently their parent drilled it into them.
statCheck.fail: I think it's just habit. A very good habit.

entries.intro: {npc_name} corrects my pronunciation of things. In front of people.
details: I said a word slightly wrong at dinner and got a full correction, volume and all.
The table went quiet. I went red. {npc_name} went right back to eating.
reactions: I choose my words more carefully around them now. Mostly out of spite.

entries.intro: {npc_name} walks with purpose. You can hear them coming from the speed of their footsteps alone.
details: I've never seen them stroll. Every trip is an errand, even if it's just going to sit down.
They look slightly offended when other people walk slowly in front of them.
reactions: I keep to the side of the path now. Safer for everyone.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} mentioned being slow once cost them something important. Since then, urgency is their default setting.
statCheck.fail: Some people just move fast. Part of who they are.

entries.intro: {npc_name} wrote my name on a list for a communal task I never agreed to. Found out the day of.
details: They volunteered me without asking and then acted surprised when I was upset.
Apparently in their mind, putting someone's name down is the same as asking. It is not.
reactions: I check the board every morning now. Trust but verify.

entries.intro: {npc_name} is genuinely happy for other people's good news. No jealousy, no qualifications.
details: I told them about something going well for me and their face lit up like it was their own success.
I've watched them do it with others too. The enthusiasm is always real.
reactions: People who can celebrate someone else's good fortune without a trace of envy are precious.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} told me they practice it on purpose. Said envy poisoned too many of their friendships growing up, so they chose to be glad instead.
statCheck.fail: Natural warmth, probably. Some people are just built generous.

entries.intro: {npc_name} and {npc_name_2} both claim credit for the same idea. I've heard each version.
details: {npc_name} says they thought of it first. {npc_name_2} says they suggested it weeks ago.
The idea wasn't even that good. But the argument over ownership has taken on a life of its own.
reactions: I'm staying neutral. Picking a side here would be a mistake.

statCheck.pass: Honestly, I think {npc_name_2} said it first, but {npc_name} was the one who did something with it. Both have a point.
statCheck.fail: I wasn't there when it started. No use guessing.

entries.intro: {npc_name} is always the last one working. The sun goes down and they're still at it.
details: I've passed their spot after dark and heard tools still going. Steady, unhurried, thorough.
It's not urgency. They just don't seem to notice when the day ends.
reactions: I hope they rest eventually. That kind of pace catches up with you.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} told me once that idle hours are the hardest for them. Keeping busy keeps the quiet out.
statCheck.fail: I don't know what keeps them going so late. Dedication, I suppose.

entries.intro: {npc_name} taps their foot during every conversation. Tap, tap, tap, the entire time.
details: I've tried focusing on what they're saying but my eyes drift down to the foot every time.
I don't think they know they do it. Or they know and they can't stop.
reactions: It's not the end of the world. But I notice it every single time.

entries.intro: I told {npc_name} a secret and they've kept it. Months now. Not a whisper.
details: I've watched for signs: odd looks from neighbors, knowing glances. Nothing. They're a vault.
In a place where gossip moves fast, that kind of silence is worth more than gold.
reactions: They've earned the right to hear the next one too.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} told me once they've been burned by loose mouths before. They decided early on to be the person they needed and didn't have.
statCheck.fail: Some people are just discreet. I'm glad they are.

entries.intro: {npc_name} asks for favors the way other people give orders. Always has.
details: 'Can you move this for me' doesn't sound like a question when they say it. It sounds like a schedule update.
The tone is polite but the implication is clear: this is happening, with or without your agreement.
reactions: I've started saying no occasionally. Just to remind us both that it's an option.

statCheck.pass: I think they grew up somewhere where asking directly was the only way to get things done. The bluntness isn't malice, it's habit.
statCheck.fail: Some people just communicate like that. I try not to take it personally.

entries.intro: {npc_name} always knows the right thing to say after a hard day. Not advice, just the right words.
details: I came back exhausted yesterday and they said 'you did enough today.' Four words. Exactly what I needed.
They never try to fix anything. Just acknowledge that things are hard, and that's enough.
reactions: I think some people have a gift for knowing what others need to hear.

entries.intro: {npc_name} leaves conversations without warning. Mid-sentence sometimes. Just walks away.
details: I was telling them about my morning and they turned and left. I finished the sentence to empty air.
I've asked about it and they said they leave when they're done listening. Brutally honest.
reactions: I keep my stories short around them now. Seems safer.

statCheck.pass: I think they have trouble knowing when conversations should end. Someone told me {npc_name} panics about overstaying, so they leave early instead.
statCheck.fail: Maybe they're just blunt. Not everyone follows the same social rules.

entries.intro: {npc_name} and {npc_name_2} always sit next to each other at gatherings. If one is late, the other saves the spot.
details: I watched {npc_name_2} guard an empty seat for twenty minutes once. Wouldn't let anyone take it.
When {npc_name} arrived, they just sat down like it was expected. Probably was.
reactions: It's a small loyalty but it's consistent. That counts for a lot.

entries.intro: {npc_name} has an answer for everything. Even things nobody asked about.
details: I mentioned it was cloudy and got a full explanation of why. I just wanted to make conversation.
The knowledge is impressive. The delivery is exhausting.
reactions: I nod and wait for it to end. It's the path of least resistance.

statCheck.pass: I think they're lonely and facts are the only way they know how to connect. The talking is how they try to be useful.
statCheck.fail: Some people just like to explain things. I try to be patient.

entries.intro: {npc_name} brought me food when I was too busy to stop for a meal. Just left it on my workbench.
details: Didn't say anything, didn't wait around for thanks. I found it still warm when I finally sat down.
That's twice now they've done this. They must watch for when I skip meals.
reactions: I don't know how to repay that kind of attentiveness. But I'll figure something out.

entries.intro: {npc_name} lied to me about something small and I can't stop thinking about it.
details: It wasn't important: just whether they'd finished a chore or not. But the lie was smooth, practiced.
If they'll lie about a chore, what else comes that easy?
reactions: I haven't confronted them. I'm just more careful now about what I believe.

statCheck.pass: I mentioned it to a neighbor and they said {npc_name} does this often: small lies to avoid small confrontations.
statCheck.fail: Maybe I misread the situation. People have off days.

entries.intro: {npc_name} cracks their knuckles before starting any task. Every time, without fail.
details: It's become a signal now: you hear the cracking and you know {npc_name} is about to do something.
I've tried it myself and it just hurts. They make it look natural.
reactions: It's become weirdly motivating. Like a starting bell.

entries.intro: {npc_name} walked with me in the rain just so I'd have company. Got soaked and didn't complain once.
details: They had no reason to be out. I saw them grab a coat and follow me the moment the sky opened up.
We talked about nothing important. That was the point, I think.
reactions: Choosing discomfort so someone else doesn't have to be alone: that's character.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} told me once they hated being alone in bad weather when they were young. Now they make sure nobody else has to be.
statCheck.fail: Probably just kindness. Some people have it to spare.

entries.intro: {npc_name} takes credit for group efforts like they did it alone.
details: We all worked on the same project last week and when someone praised it, {npc_name} accepted the compliment without mentioning anyone else.
I didn't say anything at the time. Wish I had.
reactions: I know what I contributed. That's going to have to be enough.

statCheck.pass: I don't think they mean to do it. {npc_name} struggles to share credit because they're not sure they matter without it.
statCheck.fail: Maybe they just forgot to mention the rest of us. It happens.

entries.intro: {npc_name} organized a meal for everyone when morale was low. Didn't announce it, just did it.
details: Set everything out, made sure there was enough, and then sat down like they were a guest at their own table.
The food wasn't fancy but the timing was perfect. We needed it.
reactions: Leadership shows up in different ways. That was one of the quiet ones.

entries.intro: {npc_name} and {npc_name_2} both told me the same gossip about each other. Within the same hour.
details: {npc_name} said {npc_name_2} was being difficult. Then {npc_name_2} said {npc_name} was being difficult.
The stories were identical but with the names swapped. I didn't know whether to laugh or worry.
reactions: I agreed with both of them and kept my head down. Survival instinct.

entries.intro: {npc_name} remembers every favor they've done and brings them up at convenient moments.
details: I asked them for help with something and the first thing I heard was a list of previous help they'd given.
It makes you not want to ask again. Which might be the point.
reactions: Favors with strings attached aren't favors. They're transactions.

statCheck.pass: I think they were taken advantage of a lot before they came here. The ledger of favors is protection, not pettiness.
statCheck.fail: Maybe they just have a long memory. Not necessarily a bad thing.

entries.intro: {npc_name} has the steadiest hands I've ever seen. Nothing shakes them.
details: I watched them thread a needle after working all day. Not a tremor. Like their hands are carved from stone.
Meanwhile I can barely pour water into a cup without spilling after noon.
reactions: Some people are built for fine work. I'm built for loud work.

entries.intro: {npc_name} congratulated me on something and I could tell they meant every word.
details: No undertone, no qualifier. Just genuine warmth. I checked their face for sarcasm and found none.
In a place where praise usually comes with a 'but,' that simplicity stood out.
reactions: A clean compliment is a rare thing. I'm holding onto it.

entries.intro: {npc_name} sighs before answering any question. Even easy ones.
details: I asked what they wanted for dinner and got a sigh deep enough to fill a sail.
It's not that they're annoyed: it's more like every question requires a moment of existential preparation.
reactions: I've stopped asking and started guessing. Fewer sighs that way.

entries.intro: {npc_name} dropped everything to sit with me after I got bad news. Stayed until I was alright.
details: They didn't ask what happened, didn't offer solutions. Just sat there and let the quiet do the work.
I don't know how long it was. An hour, maybe longer. They didn't check the time once.
reactions: That's a debt I can never fully repay. And {npc_name} would say there's nothing to repay.

entries.intro: {npc_name} never compliments anyone. Not once, not ever, as far as I've seen.
details: You could build them a house and the best you'd get is 'it'll do.' Maybe a nod if you're lucky.
I finished a difficult job last week and they walked past it without a word. Looked right at it.
reactions: I've stopped looking for approval from that direction. Healthier that way.

statCheck.pass: I think they were raised by someone who never praised them. They literally don't know how to do it.
statCheck.fail: Maybe they're just hard to impress. Not everyone shows appreciation the same way.

entries.intro: {npc_name} and {npc_name_2} cook for each other on alternating days. Been doing it for as long as I've been here.
details: One cooks, the other cleans. No discussion, no negotiation. Just a system that works.
I asked to join the rotation once and they politely declined. It's a two-person arrangement.
reactions: Some partnerships run on simple agreements. Theirs is the smoothest I've seen.

entries.intro: {npc_name} uses my tools without asking and then denies it. The sawdust on their hands says otherwise.
details: I've started marking my tool handles with a small notch. The notch marks keep appearing in their workspace.
They looked me dead in the eye and said 'I have my own tools.' While holding mine.
reactions: At this point it's almost impressive. The commitment to denial is extraordinary.

entries.intro: {npc_name} makes eye contact when you talk to them. Properly, not the kind that drifts.
details: It's disarming at first. Most people look away or past you, but {npc_name} looks at you.
You find yourself being more honest with them because of it. Hard to be vague under that attention.
reactions: I think real listening starts with the eyes. They've figured that out.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} told me once their mother insisted on eye contact during conversations. Said it was how you showed respect.
statCheck.fail: Some people are just good at listening. Comes naturally.

entries.intro: {npc_name} talks about themselves in every conversation. Every topic becomes about them eventually.
details: I mentioned my sore shoulder and within two sentences we were discussing their sore shoulder, their recovery, their approach.
I've tested it: start with any subject and count how long before it circles back. Usually three sentences.
reactions: I don't think they realize they do it. But I've stopped sharing personal things.

statCheck.pass: I think they relate to people by connecting through their own experience. It's not selfishness, it's the only way they know to show they understand.
statCheck.fail: Maybe that's just how they talk. Some people process everything through themselves.

entries.intro: {npc_name} helped me learn something I'd been struggling with for weeks. Made it look simple.
details: They watched me fail once, asked if I wanted a hand, and then showed me in about five minutes.
No condescension, no 'well obviously.' Just patient, clear instruction.
reactions: Good teachers don't make you feel small for not knowing. {npc_name} gets that.

entries.intro: {npc_name} folds their arms during every disagreement. Even when they're wrong.
details: The arms cross, the chin lifts, and the listening stops. You can see the exact moment it happens.
I've tried continuing the conversation after the fold. It's like talking to a wall.
reactions: I wait for the arms to unfold before I try again. Sometimes it takes a day.

entries.intro: {npc_name} has a talent for making hard work feel lighter just by being there.
details: We were hauling supplies all morning and they cracked jokes the entire time. Made it go twice as fast.
Nobody complained, nobody dragged their feet. The mood was better because {npc_name} was in it.
reactions: I request to work alongside them now. Not for the help: for the company.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} mentioned they learned that from years of doing terrible jobs. Said if you can't change the work, change the mood.
statCheck.fail: I think they're just naturally cheerful. Lucky them.

entries.intro: {npc_name} and {npc_name_2} can't agree on how to organize anything. Not one thing.
details: Tools by type or by size. Firewood by length or by dryness. Every decision is a negotiation.
I've seen them spend more time arguing about the system than actually organizing.
reactions: I organize my own things and stay out of it. Life's too short.

entries.intro: {npc_name} squints at everything like they're reading fine print on the world.
details: Even in broad daylight, full squint. Like the sun is a personal challenge they refuse to lose.
I offered to lend them a wide-brimmed hat once and they said their eyesight was perfectly fine.
reactions: I've stopped worrying about it. If they say they can see, I'll take their word for it.

statCheck.pass: I think their eyes are going and they won't admit it. {npc_name} mentioned struggling to read in dim light when they didn't think anyone was listening.
statCheck.fail: Some people just squint. Maybe the light bothers them.

entries.intro: {npc_name} stood between me and someone who was getting aggressive. Didn't hesitate.
details: Stepped right in, calm as anything, and just said 'that's enough.' And it was.
I don't know what I would have done if they hadn't been there.
reactions: Courage isn't the absence of fear. It's stepping forward when you're afraid. {npc_name} did that.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} told me afterward that their hands were shaking the whole time. They just didn't let it show.
statCheck.fail: They looked completely calm. Maybe they're just made of sterner stuff than most.

entries.intro: {npc_name} hums disapprovingly when they disagree with something. No words, just a low hum.
details: I made a suggestion at a meeting and got the hum. Felt like a quiet verdict.
The hum is worse than an argument because you can't respond to it. There's nothing to push back against.
reactions: I'd prefer them to just say they disagree. The hum haunts me.

entries.intro: The {npc_role} does more than anyone realizes. Most of it happens before anyone else is awake.
details: I was up early once and saw them already at it, working through a list longer than my arm.
By the time the rest of us started our day, half the problems were already solved.
reactions: I try to say thanks more often now. That job earns less credit than it should.

statCheck.pass: They told me they start early because the quiet hours are when they can think. Once people are up, it's all interruptions.
statCheck.fail: I don't know their routine. Just that a lot gets done before dawn.

entries.intro: {npc_name} and {npc_name_2} have opposite opinions on everything. Everything.
details: If {npc_name} says the soup needs salt, {npc_name_2} says it doesn't. If one says left, the other says right.
I made the mistake of asking them both a question once. Got two answers and zero clarity.
reactions: I ask one at a time now and compare notes in private.

entries.intro: {npc_name} stacks firewood like it's an art form. Perfectly level, perfectly spaced.
details: I've seen them restack a pile because one log was slightly out of alignment. Slightly.
The pile looks better than most things I've built on purpose.
reactions: I'm somewhere between impressed and confused. But the firewood stays dry, so the method works.

entries.intro: {npc_name} says 'we'll see' to everything. It means no. I've decoded it.
details: I asked if they'd help with something next week. 'We'll see.' It's been three weeks.
I've tracked seven 'we'll see' responses now. Zero have turned into yes.
reactions: I'd rather hear an honest no. At least then I can make other plans.

statCheck.pass: I pushed once and got a real answer: they're afraid of committing and failing. 'We'll see' leaves an exit open.
statCheck.fail: Could be they're just undecided. Hard to tell.

entries.intro: {npc_name} shared their last bit of food with me when supplies were low. Didn't even think about it.
details: I protested and they pushed the plate toward me. Said 'I already ate.' I know they hadn't.
A small lie, but the generous kind. The kind you don't correct.
reactions: You learn who people really are when there's not enough to go around.

entries.intro: {npc_name} points out what's wrong with everything but never offers a solution.
details: The fence is crooked, the schedule is bad, the food's not right. But ask for a fix and you get a shrug.
It's easy to see problems. The hard part is doing something about them.
reactions: I've started responding to complaints with 'what do you suggest?' Ends the conversation fast.

statCheck.pass: I think they're afraid to propose solutions because they've been mocked for bad ideas before. Criticism is safer than vulnerability.
statCheck.fail: Maybe they just prefer to observe. Not everyone wants to lead.

entries.intro: {npc_name} draws in the dirt while they think. Little shapes, spirals, patterns.
details: I saw them work out a whole plan for something by scratching lines in the ground with a stick.
By the time they looked up, there was a full diagram at their feet. I couldn't read it but they clearly could.
reactions: Everyone thinks differently. {npc_name} thinks with their hands.

entries.intro: {npc_name} waited for me when I fell behind on a long walk. Didn't make a show of it.
details: Just slowed down until I caught up, then matched my pace the rest of the way.
Didn't mention it afterward. Like it was the most natural thing in the world.
reactions: Patience like that tells you what kind of person someone is.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} told me they were left behind on a walk as a child. They never let it happen to someone else.
statCheck.fail: Probably just a patient person. The world needs more of those.

entries.intro: {npc_name} talks about {npc_name_2} behind their back but is sweet as honey to their face.
details: I've heard two different versions of how {npc_name} feels about {npc_name_2}: one for public, one for me.
The gap between the two is wide enough to drive a cart through.
reactions: It makes me wonder what {npc_name} says about me when I'm not around.

statCheck.pass: I think {npc_name} is afraid of {npc_name_2}'s reaction if they were honest. The fakeness is self-preservation, not cruelty.
statCheck.fail: I don't know what's real and what's performance. Probably safest to assume a bit of both.

entries.intro: {npc_name} always takes the worst job on the rotation without being asked.
details: Hauling, scrubbing, the jobs nobody volunteers for: they just put their name down first.
I asked why once and they said 'someone has to.' As if that explained everything.
reactions: I try to beat them to the bad jobs now and then. Feels only fair.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} told me they do it because they feel they owe the settlement something. Won't say what for.
statCheck.fail: I don't know the reason. Some people carry debts only they can see.

entries.intro: {npc_name} yawns with their entire body. It's a production.
details: Arms out, head back, sound effects and all. Like their soul is trying to escape.
It's contagious too. One yawn from {npc_name} and the whole room follows.
reactions: I've caught myself yawning just thinking about it. That's power.

entries.intro: {npc_name} thanked me last week for something I did months ago. Out of nowhere.
details: They said they'd been meaning to say it and finally got around to it. Better late than never.
It was something so small I'd forgotten doing it. But they remembered.
reactions: Late gratitude still counts. In some ways it means more because it wasn't in the moment.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} told me they regret not thanking people enough in the past. Now they go back and do it even when it's late.
statCheck.fail: Maybe they just remembered. Good on them either way.

entries.intro: {npc_name} complained about the communal schedule and then didn't show up for the meeting to change it.
details: We waited. We adjusted. We made it better. {npc_name} wasn't there for any of it.
The new schedule came out and they complained about that too.
reactions: You lose the right to complain when you skip the meeting. That's my rule.

entries.intro: {npc_name} patches clothes so neatly you can't tell where the mend was.
details: I brought them a torn sleeve and it came back looking new. I checked it twice because I couldn't find the repair.
People bring their mending to {npc_name} from across the settlement now. Word got out.
reactions: There's something satisfying about seeing a broken thing made whole again.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} learned it from someone who passed. They told me once the stitching pattern is the same their teacher used.
statCheck.fail: I think it's just skill and practice. Years of it.

entries.intro: {npc_name} and {npc_name_2} look out for each other in a way that doesn't need words.
details: I saw {npc_name_2} leave extra firewood by {npc_name}'s door when the nights got cold. No note, no mention.
{npc_name} brought {npc_name_2} a hot meal the next day. Neither acknowledged the exchange.
reactions: Some debts are paid in kind, without a word. That's real friendship.

entries.intro: {npc_name} tells you what you want to hear instead of the truth. I've caught it three times now.
details: Ask them if your idea is good and it's always 'great idea.' Ask if the work looks right and it's always 'perfect.'
I tested it by presenting something obviously wrong. 'Looks wonderful,' they said. Didn't flinch.
reactions: I can't trust their praise anymore. And that's a lonely feeling.

statCheck.pass: I think they're terrified of conflict. Agreeing with everything is their way of keeping the peace at any cost.
statCheck.fail: Maybe they're just agreeable. Some people are wired to avoid friction.

entries.intro: {npc_name} counts things. Everything. I've seen them count fence posts, roof tiles, even birds.
details: I watched them count the stones in a path once. Moved their lips the whole way down.
When I asked how many there were, they told me instantly. Like they'd been waiting for someone to ask.
reactions: Some minds just work in numbers. I don't understand it, but I don't have to.

entries.intro: {npc_name} helped me move everything I own without me having to ask twice.
details: I mentioned I was relocating and the next morning they were at my door. 'Where do we start?'
We finished before noon. Would have taken me three days on my own.
reactions: That kind of follow-through on a casual mention: that's rare. I won't forget it.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} said they show up because someone did that for them once, when they had nobody. They're paying it forward.
statCheck.fail: They're just reliable. Not sure there's a deeper reason.

entries.intro: {npc_name} agreed to something in a meeting and then did the opposite. Didn't acknowledge the contradiction.
details: We all agreed on a plan. Next day, {npc_name} was doing their own version like the meeting never happened.
I brought it up and they said they'd 'thought about it more.' That's not how agreements work.
reactions: I write things down now. A record helps when memory gets selective.

entries.intro: {npc_name} peels bark off sticks when they're nervous. There's a pile of shavings wherever they've been sitting.
details: You can gauge how stressful a conversation was by the size of the pile they leave behind.
I found a completely bare stick on the bench after the last meeting. Must have been a tense one.
reactions: Everyone has their tells. At least {npc_name}'s is tidy.

entries.intro: {npc_name} checked on me every day for a week after I got hurt. Brought something different each time.
details: First day was food, second was a blanket, third was company. Like they had a plan for each visit.
By the fourth day I was feeling better but I didn't tell them. I wanted the visits to continue.
reactions: Being cared for like that is something you carry with you. It changes how you treat others.

entries.intro: {npc_name} butts into conversations they're not part of. Walks over and joins in like they were invited.
details: I was having a private word with someone and {npc_name} appeared from nowhere with an opinion.
It's not eavesdropping if you announce yourself, apparently. That's the defense.
reactions: I've started walking farther away for private conversations. It helps, mostly.

entries.intro: {npc_name} nods slowly when they agree with something. The nod has weight to it.
details: It's not the quick polite nod everyone does. It's a deliberate, full-head acknowledgment.
Getting a nod from {npc_name} feels earned. Like you've said something that actually landed.
reactions: I find myself trying to get that nod in conversations. It's become a quiet benchmark.

entries.intro: {npc_name} gave me honest feedback that stung at first. But they were right, and I'm better for it.
details: They told me my approach to a job was inefficient and showed me why. Plainly, not cruelly.
I was defensive for a day. Then I tried it their way and cut my time in half.
reactions: The truth is rarely comfortable. But people who bother telling it are worth listening to.

entries.intro: {npc_name} and {npc_name_2} haven't spoken in three days over whose turn it was to fetch water.
details: It was {npc_name}'s turn. Everyone knows it was {npc_name}'s turn. But now it's about principle.
Meanwhile the rest of us are fetching water around them and waiting for the thaw.
reactions: They'll sort it out. They always do. But the silence is loud in the meantime.

entries.intro: {npc_name} starts every morning stretching in the open where everyone can see. Full routine.
details: Arms up, bend down, twist left, twist right. Takes about ten minutes and they're very committed.
I walked past during the bending part once and made accidental eye contact. Awkward for me, not for them.
reactions: I admire the dedication. I'm just going to admire it from a distance.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} told me the routine started after an injury. Without the stretching, they can barely move by afternoon.
statCheck.fail: Probably just a health habit. Good for them.

entries.intro: {npc_name} taught me a knot I've used every day since. Took them thirty seconds to show me.
details: They tied it once, untied it, tied it again slowly, and handed me the rope. Simple as that.
I've been tying it wrong for years, apparently. Never knew until {npc_name} showed me the right way.
reactions: Thirty seconds of someone's time and I'll use that knot for the rest of my life. Good trade.

entries.intro: {npc_name} gossips more than anyone I've ever known. If you tell them something, assume it's public.
details: I made the mistake of sharing something personal and heard it back from two different people within a day.
They always preface the gossip with 'don't tell anyone but' which is the surest sign it'll travel.
reactions: I tell them nothing now. They get weather and work topics only.

statCheck.pass: I think the gossip is how they feel connected. Without it, they'd have nothing to talk about and they know it.
statCheck.fail: Some people just can't help sharing what they know. I don't think it's malicious.

entries.intro: {npc_name} sleeps with one eye open, or so it seems. Nothing gets past them at night.
details: I got up once in the middle of the night and they were already awake, watching the common area.
I asked if they sleep at all and they said 'enough.' That's not a number.
reactions: I feel safer knowing {npc_name} keeps an eye on things. Even if I'm concerned about when they rest.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} confided that they've had trouble sleeping for years. The watchfulness is half-habit, half-insomnia.
statCheck.fail: Maybe they're just a light sleeper. Some people run on less rest than others.

entries.intro: {npc_name} sang something under their breath the other day and it stopped me in my tracks.
details: They didn't know I was listening. Just a few notes, quiet, while they worked. Beautiful voice.
I've never heard them sing on purpose. Only when they think nobody's around.
reactions: Some talents hide in people who don't want the attention. I'll keep the secret.

statCheck.pass: I asked carefully once and they changed the subject fast. I think singing was something they did with someone they've lost.
statCheck.fail: I don't know the story behind it. Not everything needs one.

entries.intro: {npc_name} shushes people during conversations. Even when nobody is being particularly loud.
details: I was talking at a perfectly normal volume and got a 'shh' that felt like a slap.
Other people have gotten it too. The shush doesn't discriminate.
reactions: I lower my voice around them now. It's not worth the shushing.

entries.intro: {npc_name} and {npc_name_2} have started swapping chores without telling anyone. It's working perfectly.
details: {npc_name} does {npc_name_2}'s least favorite task and vice versa. Both are happier.
Nobody officially approved the swap but nobody's complained either. If it works, it works.
reactions: I should find someone to swap with. The system has merit.

entries.intro: {npc_name} has a way of making simple observations sound profound. I can't figure out how they do it.
details: They said 'the fire's low' last night and somehow it sounded like wisdom.
I tried saying the same thing and it just sounded like a fact about the fire.
reactions: Some people carry weight in their voice. {npc_name} is one of them.

entries.intro: {npc_name} rearranges things that don't belong to them. My workbench has been 'improved' twice.
details: I had a system. It worked for me. Now everything's where {npc_name} thinks it should go.
I moved it all back and the next day it was rearranged again. We're in a silent battle.
reactions: I appreciate that they care. I just wish they'd care about something else.

statCheck.pass: I think they genuinely believe they're helping. {npc_name} grew up being the one who organized everything at home. Old habits.
statCheck.fail: Maybe they don't realize other people have systems too.

entries.intro: {npc_name} laughs at their own jokes before finishing them. Sometimes the laugh IS the punchline.
details: They'll get halfway through a story and start wheezing. You end up laughing at the laughing, not the joke.
The joy is so pure it doesn't matter that the humor is questionable.
reactions: I've heard worse comedians with better delivery. But none with better enthusiasm.

entries.intro: {npc_name} split their rations with a stranger passing through. Didn't know them, didn't care.
details: Walked over, handed them half, and came back like it was nothing.
I asked if they knew the person. 'No. They looked hungry.' That was the whole explanation.
reactions: Kindness without reason or calculation. That's the purest form there is.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} told me they went hungry as a child. Seeing someone else go without triggers something in them they can't ignore.
statCheck.fail: I think they're just generous. Some people don't need a reason.

entries.intro: {npc_name} plays favorites and everyone can see it except them.
details: Certain people get smiles and extra help. The rest of us get efficiency and instructions.
I'm not in the favored group. I know because I've compared treatment.
reactions: I don't expect equal affection from everyone. But consistency would be nice.

statCheck.pass: Someone told me {npc_name} gravitates toward people who remind them of someone from before they lived here. The favoritism isn't personal: it's nostalgia.
statCheck.fail: Maybe they just click with certain people. Can't force chemistry.

entries.intro: {npc_name} cleans things nobody else thinks to clean. Hinges, handles, the underside of benches.
details: I caught them polishing a door handle at dawn once. They looked at me like I was the odd one.
The whole settlement is slightly cleaner because of them, even if nobody else notices.
reactions: Quiet maintenance is its own kind of love for a place.

entries.intro: {npc_name} made me laugh when I hadn't laughed in days. They probably don't even know they did it.
details: It was a stupid comment about the weather. Nothing clever. Just the right thing at the right time.
I went from flat to smiling in about two seconds. Hadn't realized how much I needed that.
reactions: Some people improve a room just by being in it. {npc_name} is one.

entries.intro: {npc_name} and {npc_name_2} had the same argument again. Word for word. I've memorized it.
details: Same opening line from {npc_name}, same response from {npc_name_2}. Same escalation, same uncomfortable ending.
I could mouth along at this point. I've started leaving the room when it begins.
reactions: Some people are stuck in a loop and the best thing I can do is not get caught in it.

entries.intro: The {npc_role} keeps odd hours but the work gets done. I can't argue with results.
details: Sometimes they're up before dawn, sometimes they start at noon. There's no pattern I can find.
But when something needs doing, it gets done. Usually before I realize it needed doing.
reactions: I've stopped questioning the schedule and started just appreciating the output.

statCheck.pass: They told me once they work when inspiration strikes and sleep when it doesn't. Not everyone fits a routine.
statCheck.fail: I couldn't tell you the logic behind it. But who am I to judge someone who delivers.

entries.intro: {npc_name} keeps asking me how I am and actually waits for the answer. Every time.
details: Most people ask as a greeting. {npc_name} asks as a question. There's a pause, an expectation.
I've started giving real answers because it feels wrong to waste the sincerity.
reactions: I didn't know how much I wanted someone to actually ask until {npc_name} started doing it.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} told me they started doing it because no one asked them for years. They know what it feels like when nobody checks in.
statCheck.fail: They're just a caring person. Not everyone needs a reason to ask.

entries.intro: {npc_name} blames their tools every time something goes wrong. The tools are fine.
details: I've used the same saw they complained about. It cuts perfectly well.
Last week it was the hammer. The week before that, the chisel. At some point the common factor becomes clear.
reactions: I don't point it out. Nobody wins that conversation.

entries.intro: {npc_name} collects smooth stones. Has a whole pile of them, sorted by size.
details: I watched them pick one up from the path, examine it, and pocket it with genuine satisfaction.
Asked to see the collection once. They pulled out at least thirty, all from different walks.
reactions: Everyone needs a thing that's just theirs. Stones are as good as anything.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} told me each stone is from a place they've been. The collection is a map of everywhere they've walked.
statCheck.fail: I think they just like stones. Everyone collects something.

entries.intro: {npc_name} quietly mended a fence I'd been putting off for weeks. Didn't say a word about it.
details: I noticed it on my way home. The post was straight, the wire was tight. Professional work.
They never brought it up. I only knew it was them because {npc_name_2} saw them doing it.
reactions: Help that asks for nothing in return is the purest kind.

entries.intro: {npc_name} argues about things that don't matter as if they matter enormously.
details: I watched them spend twenty minutes defending their opinion on which direction to stack logs.
The intensity was impressive. The stakes were not.
reactions: I let them win the small battles now. It costs me nothing and saves a lot of time.

statCheck.pass: I think the small arguments are a substitute for the big ones they won't have. Easier to fight about logs than feelings.
statCheck.fail: Maybe they just care deeply about everything. Must be tiring.

entries.intro: {npc_name} has the calmest voice I've ever heard. Like nothing could rattle them.
details: Even when things went sideways during a big task, they spoke at the same steady pace.
I think half the reason people stay calm around them is the voice. It's contagious.
reactions: I wish I had that composure. Mine cracks under much less pressure.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} confided that the calm is something they force. Inside, they said, they're always a bit panicked. The voice is a choice.
statCheck.fail: Maybe they're just naturally steady. Some people don't rattle easily.

entries.intro: {npc_name} introduced me to someone new and made sure I felt included. A small thing that meant a lot.
details: They didn't just say my name: they added context. 'This is someone you'd want to know.'
I went from standing on the outside to being part of the conversation in seconds.
reactions: A good introduction is a gift. {npc_name} gives them freely.

entries.intro: {npc_name} made promises at the last meeting that everyone knew wouldn't happen. Including {npc_name_2}.
details: Grand plans, ambitious timelines. {npc_name_2} caught my eye and shook their head slightly.
Sure enough, a week later: nothing. Not even a mention of why.
reactions: I've learned to judge people by their follow-through, not their proposals.

entries.intro: {npc_name} eats slowly, deliberately, like the meal deserves their full attention.
details: No talking, no distraction. Plate to mouth, pause, repeat. It's almost meditative.
I tried it once. Lasted about three bites before I went back to shoveling.
reactions: Maybe they're onto something. I just don't have the patience to find out.

entries.intro: {npc_name} stayed late to help me finish when everyone else had gone home.
details: They rolled up their sleeves and just started working alongside me. No negotiation.
We finished as the light faded and they shrugged like it was no trouble. It was trouble. I could tell.
reactions: The people who stay are the ones you remember.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} mentioned they know what it's like to be the last one left with work to do. Said they hated that feeling too much to leave someone else in it.
statCheck.fail: I don't know what made them stay. Just glad they did.

entries.intro: {npc_name} answers questions with questions. Every time. It's maddening.
details: I asked what time they wanted to start and they said 'what time works for you?' We went back and forth five times.
Neither of us made a decision. We just circled until one of us gave up.
reactions: Next time I'm just picking a time and telling them when.

entries.intro: {npc_name} winks at people to signal agreement. At least I think that's what it means.
details: I got one yesterday in the middle of a group discussion and I have no idea what I was agreeing to.
I've seen others get the wink and look equally confused.
reactions: I wink back now. I don't know what I'm communicating either.

entries.intro: {npc_name} lent me confidence I didn't have by saying 'you can do this' at exactly the right moment.
details: I was about to back out of something and those four words stopped me. They said it like a fact, not encouragement.
I did it. And they were right.
reactions: Sometimes the difference between doing something and not doing it is one person believing you can.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} told me they spent years doubting themselves and never had anyone say those words to them. They give others what they wish they'd gotten.
statCheck.fail: Probably just instinct. The right words at the right time.

entries.intro: {npc_name} 'helpfully' reorganized my supply of firewood by size. Now I can't find the kindling.
details: It was organized. Just not by their standards. Now it's organized by their standards and useless to me.
They were so pleased with themselves I couldn't bring myself to undo it.
reactions: I keep the kindling inside now. Learned my lesson.

entries.intro: {npc_name} remembers what I said weeks ago and brings it up when it's relevant. Properly listens.
details: I mentioned a concern in passing and they circled back to it two weeks later with an observation that helped.
That kind of listening takes effort. Most people hear you and move on.
reactions: I try to listen that way now too. It's harder than it sounds.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} mentioned they keep mental notes on everyone. Said if someone bothers telling you something, the least you can do is remember it.
statCheck.fail: I think they just have a sharp memory. Useful quality.

entries.intro: {npc_name} and {npc_name_2} compete over who goes to bed latest. Neither will be the first to turn in.
details: I watched them both yawning by the fire last night, wide awake out of pure stubbornness.
I went to sleep, woke up once in the night, and they were both still sitting there.
reactions: They looked terrible the next morning. Both claimed they slept perfectly.

entries.intro: {npc_name} always asks before touching your things. Always. Even in an emergency.
details: A pot was about to fall and they still asked 'can I grab that?' before catching it.
It sounds excessive until you realize how rare basic courtesy actually is.
reactions: I trust them around my things more than anyone else here. That's why.

entries.intro: {npc_name} makes plans and then changes them three times before anything starts.
details: We were going to start at dawn, then midmorning, then after lunch. By the time we began, the day was half over.
The final plan was somehow worse than the first one. I didn't have the energy to say so.
reactions: I've started adding half a day to anything {npc_name} organizes. Keeps expectations realistic.

entries.intro: {npc_name} tilts their head when they're thinking. Like a bird studying a worm.
details: The head goes to one side, the eyes narrow, and you know they're working something out.
I've started recognizing the tilt and giving them a moment before I continue talking.
reactions: It's one of those small things that makes someone unmistakably themselves.

entries.intro: {npc_name} is the first person I'd want beside me if things went wrong. No hesitation.
details: Not because they're the strongest or the smartest, but because they don't panic. Ever.
I've seen them face problems that would rattle anyone else and their reaction is always 'alright, let's sort this out.'
reactions: Steadiness under pressure is worth more than talent or strength.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} told me calmness was something they had to learn. Said they used to fall apart and decided they didn't want to be that person anymore.
statCheck.fail: I don't know if it's natural or practiced. Either way, it's dependable.

entries.intro: {npc_name} picks at their nails when bored. The clicking sound carries further than they think.
details: Three meetings in a row now. Click, click, click. It's like a tiny, relentless clock.
I've tried sitting farther away. The sound finds me.
reactions: It's not worth mentioning. But it's also not worth pretending I don't notice.

entries.intro: {npc_name} said something kind about me to someone else and it got back to me. Best thing I've heard all month.
details: They didn't know I'd find out. That's what made it real.
You can pay a compliment to someone's face and mean nothing by it. But behind their back, that's honest.
reactions: I'm carrying that one around for a while. Doesn't cost anything to hold onto something warm.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} doesn't know I found out. And I think the kindness would mean less to them if they knew I'd heard it.
statCheck.fail: I don't know the details. Just that someone repeated something nice.

entries.intro: {npc_name} took the last of the hot water this morning. Again.
details: There's a limited amount and they use it like it's infinite. I've been washing in cold water for three days.
I left earlier today to beat them to it. They'd already been and gone.
reactions: I'm getting up before dawn tomorrow. This ends.

entries.intro: {npc_name} scratches their chin before saying anything important. I've learned to read it.
details: Quick scratch: small observation. Long scratch: something they've been thinking about for a while.
I mentioned this to someone else and now they watch for it too. It's remarkably consistent.
reactions: People have patterns they don't see. It's interesting to be the one who does.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} told me once that the scratch gives them time to think before speaking. It's a deliberate pause disguised as a habit.
statCheck.fail: Could just be a nervous tick. Everyone has one.

entries.intro: {npc_name} included me in a decision that didn't require my input. Just because they valued it.
details: They could have just done it. Nobody would have questioned them. But they asked what I thought first.
My suggestion wasn't even that useful, but they listened like it was.
reactions: Being included when you don't have to be: that's how you make people feel they matter.

entries.intro: {npc_name} coughs pointedly when they disagree with something instead of speaking up.
details: It's a very specific cough. Not a sick cough. A deliberate, theatrical clearing of the throat that means 'I object.'
I've watched people change course based on a single cough from {npc_name}. That's influence without words.
reactions: I'd rather they just say what they mean. But the cough is effective, I'll give them that.

entries.intro: {npc_name} walks with the heaviest footsteps of anyone I've ever known.
details: You can track their location in the settlement by sound alone. They don't sneak anywhere.
I've never been surprised by them coming around a corner. I always hear them first.
reactions: There's a comfort in knowing exactly where people are. {npc_name} provides that unintentionally.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} told me they were taught that walking softly meant you were sneaking. They'd rather be loud and honest about where they are.
statCheck.fail: Some people just walk heavy. It's not intentional.

entries.intro: {npc_name} and {npc_name_2} make each other better. You can see it in how they work.
details: {npc_name} brings the ideas and {npc_name_2} brings the discipline. Together, things actually get done.
Alone, each of them is capable. Together, they're something else entirely.
reactions: Not every partnership works that way. When it does, you notice.

entries.intro: {npc_name} closes drawers loudly. Not slamming, just with more force than any drawer requires.
details: Every morning: thud, thud, thud. Like a three-beat alarm clock I didn't sign up for.
I've thought about padding the drawers somehow. But then they'd just push harder.
reactions: Some battles aren't worth fighting. This is one of them.

entries.intro: {npc_name} keeps a running tally of who owes what. Somehow it's always accurate.
details: I borrowed a nail three weeks ago and they reminded me yesterday. Exactly one nail.
It's not hostile, just precise. Like a ledger with legs.
reactions: I return things quickly now. The tally doesn't forget.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} admitted once that the tracking started because they kept getting taken advantage of. Now it's a reflex they can't turn off.
statCheck.fail: Maybe they just have a good memory. Some people are built that way.

entries.intro: {npc_name} is the kind of person who makes you want to be better. Not by saying anything, just by being.
details: I watch how they handle disagreements, how they treat people, how they do their work. It's all consistent.
There's no gap between who they are when people are watching and who they are when they're not.
reactions: Integrity doesn't announce itself. It just shows up, every day, in small ways.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} mentioned once that they try hard to be the same person all the time because they grew up around people who weren't.
statCheck.fail: I think it's just who they are. Naturally consistent.

entries.intro: {npc_name} grumbles through every task like the world is ending, but they always finish. Every time.
details: You'd think they hated the work from the complaining alone. But the quality is always solid.
I asked them once if they actually disliked the work and they said 'no, complaining just makes it go faster.'
reactions: I can't argue with the method. The work speaks for itself.

statCheck.pass: I think the grumbling is armor. {npc_name} cares more about doing a good job than they'd ever admit.
statCheck.fail: Some people grumble. Doesn't mean they're unhappy.

entries.intro: {npc_name} has this habit of humming while they work. It's quiet, but once you notice it, you can't un-notice it.
details: Same tune every day. I don't recognize it, but I could hum it back to you note for note at this point.
It's not unpleasant, just constant, like a second heartbeat in the background.
reactions: I catch myself humming it sometimes and that's when I know I've been around them too long.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} told me the tune was something their mother used to sing. They don't remember the words anymore, just the melody.
statCheck.fail: Probably just a habit. Everyone has something they do without thinking.

entries.intro: {npc_name} told me to my face that my cooking was bad. Not unkind about it, just honest.
details: Everyone else ate it and said it was fine. {npc_name} said 'this needs salt and another hour.'
I was annoyed for about a day. Then I tried the salt and the extra hour and they were right.
reactions: I'd rather hear the truth from one person than polite lies from ten.

entries.intro: {npc_name} never admits when they're wrong. The closest you get is a long silence and a subject change.
details: I proved them wrong on something simple last week. They blinked twice, paused, and asked what I had for breakfast.
It's almost impressive. The redirection is so smooth you forget you were right.
reactions: I don't fight it anymore. I just know, and that's enough.

statCheck.pass: I think admitting fault was never safe for them growing up. The stubbornness isn't pride: it's protection.
statCheck.fail: Some people just don't like being wrong. Nothing deeper than that.

entries.intro: {npc_name} and {npc_name_2} share meals together most evenings. It's become a quiet ritual.
details: Neither one makes a big show of it. One cooks, the other cleans, and they switch off without discussing it.
I've walked past and seen them eating in comfortable silence. Not every friendship needs words.
reactions: There's something steady about two people who just show up for each other day after day.

entries.intro: {npc_name} has started sighing before they speak. Every sentence begins with an exhale.
details: I asked them a yes-or-no question and got a sigh so deep I thought they were going to tell me someone had died.
The answer was 'yes.' Just 'yes.' But with a full lung evacuation first.
reactions: I've stopped taking the sighs personally. It's just how they breathe now.

entries.intro: {npc_name} left a bundle of kindling at my door without a note. I know it was them because nobody else stacks it that neatly.
details: They've done things like this before. Small, practical gifts that solve a problem you didn't mention.
I tried to thank them and they waved me off like I was being dramatic.
reactions: Thoughtfulness that refuses recognition is the most genuine kind.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} told me the kindling wasn't random. They'd noticed my supply was low and didn't want to embarrass me by saying so.
statCheck.fail: Probably just had extra and thought of me. Simple as that.

entries.intro: {npc_name} keeps volunteering me for things without asking first.
details: I found out I was helping with a project because someone thanked me for offering. I hadn't offered.
When I asked {npc_name} about it they said 'I knew you'd want to help.' They assumed correctly, but that's not the point.
reactions: I'd like to be asked, that's all: the answer would probably be yes.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} admitted they do it because saying yes is easier than explaining why they can't. They dread the conversation more than the extra work.
statCheck.fail: I think they just assume everyone wants to help. Maybe they would.

entries.intro: {npc_name} always smells faintly of woodsmoke. Even when there hasn't been a fire in hours.
details: I mentioned it once and they laughed. Said the smell followed them from their last home and never left.
It's become a comfort. If I catch woodsmoke on the air, I know {npc_name} was nearby recently.
reactions: Some people carry where they've been with them. {npc_name} carries it in the air around them.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} told me the woodsmoke is from their old home, where they built a fire every night for someone who's gone now.
statCheck.fail: Probably just lives near a fire pit. Smoke gets into everything.

entries.intro: {npc_name} tried to show me how to sharpen a blade properly. I didn't have the heart to tell them I already knew.
details: They were so earnest about it. Full demonstration, careful angles, even checked my grip.
I nodded along and let them teach me what I learned ten years ago. They seemed happy about it.
reactions: Sometimes letting someone teach you is a kindness you do for them, not yourself.

entries.intro: {npc_name} complains about everything but never does anything to change what bothers them.
details: The seat's uncomfortable, so they sit in it every day and grumble. I've suggested three different seats.
The food's too bland, but they eat it plain rather than add seasoning. It's like the complaint IS the meal.
reactions: I think they enjoy the complaining more than they'd enjoy the solution.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} once told me complaining is how they process the day. Fixing things would take away the outlet.
statCheck.fail: Maybe they just like to grumble. Plenty of people do.

entries.intro: {npc_name} and {npc_name_2} used to be close but barely talk now. Nobody knows what happened.
details: They're polite when they cross paths. The kind of polite that used to be warmth.
I've tried gently asking both of them about it. Each changes the subject in the same way.
reactions: I miss seeing them together. Whatever broke, I hope they find a way to fix it.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name_2} let slip that they had a disagreement about trust. One of them said something they can't take back.
statCheck.fail: I don't think either of them wants to talk about it. I've stopped asking.

entries.intro: {npc_name} gives the most direct compliments I've ever received. No preamble, no softening.
details: Last week they looked at something I'd built and said 'that's good work.' Three words. Meant more than a speech.
They don't hand them out often, so when one lands, you know they mean it.
reactions: I'd rather have one honest word from {npc_name} than a dozen vague ones from someone else.

entries.intro: {npc_name} keeps giving me advice I didn't ask for. Every conversation turns into a lesson.
details: I mentioned I was tired and got a ten-step plan for better sleep. I just wanted to say I was tired.
They mean well. But sometimes you want sympathy, not a strategy.
reactions: I've started keeping complaints to myself around them. Safer that way.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} told me the advice is how they show they care. They don't know how to just listen without trying to fix.
statCheck.fail: I think they just want to be helpful. Can't fault someone for that.

entries.intro: {npc_name} whistles through their teeth when they're concentrating. I find it oddly calming.
details: It's not a tune. Just a thin, steady stream of air that stops the moment they look up.
I've noticed I work better when I can hear it. It means someone nearby is focused.
reactions: We all have our noises. Theirs happens to be one I don't mind.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} told me the whistling is something they picked up from their father. He'd whistle while he worked and the sound meant everything was alright.
statCheck.fail: Just a habit. Everyone makes noise when they're focused.

entries.intro: {npc_name} noticed I was having a bad day and just sat beside me without saying a word.
details: No questions, no advice, just presence: they stayed until I felt like talking and then they listened.
I didn't realize how much I needed someone to just be there until they were.
reactions: The people who know when not to speak are rarer than the ones who always do.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} told me they do it because when they were at their lowest, all they wanted was company, not words.
statCheck.fail: They're just observant. Some people notice things others miss.

entries.intro: {npc_name} eats with their mouth open and somehow doesn't notice the looks they get.
details: I can hear every chew from across the table. It's a talent, in the worst way.
I've tried sitting further away but there are only so many seats.
reactions: I eat early now. Problem solved.

entries.intro: {npc_name} names their tools. Every one of them.
details: The good hammer is 'reliable.' The bad one is 'patience.' I asked why patience and they said 'because it tests mine.'
I thought it was strange until I caught myself talking to my own shovel last week.
reactions: Maybe naming things makes the work feel less lonely. I get it now.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} told me they name tools because they spent years working alone. The names were the only conversation they had.
statCheck.fail: Probably just a quirk. Everyone personalizes their belongings in some way.

entries.intro: {npc_name} covered for me when I was late without being asked. Just picked up where I should have been.
details: By the time I arrived, the work was halfway done. They acted like nothing had happened.
When I tried to apologize they cut me off and said 'you'd have done the same.'
reactions: I would have. And knowing {npc_name} believes that means something.

entries.intro: {npc_name} talks over people constantly. They don't even realize they're doing it.
details: Three times in one conversation yesterday: my sentences don't get endings anymore.
I've tried pausing to let them finish, but the pause just invites more interruption.
reactions: I hold up a finger now when I'm mid-sentence. It works about half the time.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} admitted once that they interrupt because they're afraid of forgetting what they want to say. The urgency is real to them, even when it shouldn't be.
statCheck.fail: Maybe they just get excited. Fast thinkers sometimes outrun their manners.

entries.intro: {npc_name} and {npc_name_2} finish each other's sentences. It's unsettling how often they get it right.
details: {npc_name} starts a thought, trails off, and {npc_name_2} completes it without missing a beat.
I tried guessing once to join in and got it completely wrong: it's a two-person trick.
reactions: That kind of shorthand only comes from spending a lot of time together.

entries.intro: {npc_name} walked me home when it was dark without me asking. Just fell into step beside me.
details: We didn't talk much, just walked, and they peeled off at my door with a nod.
The next morning they didn't mention it. Like it was just the obvious thing to do.
reactions: You learn who people are by what they do when there's nothing in it for them.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} told me they walk people home because a friend got hurt on a dark path once. They swore they'd never let that happen near them.
statCheck.fail: They're just thoughtful. Not everyone needs a reason to be kind.

entries.intro: {npc_name} told me a story about themselves that I'm pretty sure was mostly fiction.
details: The details shifted every time they told it. The fish was bigger, the distance was longer, the rain was harder.
I don't think they're lying on purpose. I think the story just grows in the telling.
reactions: I enjoy the versions. Each one is a little more entertaining than the last.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} admitted the stories grow because the truth is boring. The real versions are much smaller and sadder.
statCheck.fail: They just like telling a good tale. Can't blame someone for embellishing.

entries.intro: {npc_name} remembered my birthday when I'd forgotten it myself. Had a small something set aside.
details: Nothing fancy. Just a warm word and a gesture that said 'I keep track of you.'
I don't even know when their birthday is. That bothers me now.
reactions: I'm going to find out and return the favor. People who remember deserve to be remembered.

entries.intro: {npc_name} uses silence as a weapon. They just go quiet and let you fill the gap with your own guilt.
