details: I said something mildly inconsiderate and got thirty seconds of dead air. By the end I'd apologized twice.
They don't yell or argue, just wait: it's more effective than any shouting match.
reactions: I choose my words carefully around them now. The silence teaches you that.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} once admitted they learned it from a parent, said silence was the only power they had as a child.
statCheck.fail: Maybe they're just not the arguing type. Not everyone fights with words.

entries.intro: {npc_name} always saves me the seat I like without saying anything about it.
details: They get there first, set something on my spot to hold it, then move it when I arrive.
I noticed the pattern after the third time. It's been going on longer than that, I'm sure.
reactions: Small kindnesses that don't ask for credit. That's the best kind of neighbor.

entries.intro: {npc_name} and {npc_name_2} are competing over who can grow the better garden. Neither will admit it's a competition.
details: {npc_name} planted something new last week and {npc_name_2} planted something bigger the day after. Coincidence, they both say.
I've been asked to look at both gardens and comment. I refuse to pick sides.
reactions: The settlement is more colorful for it, so I'm not complaining.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} confided the garden started as a distraction from grief. The growing things helped them feel like something was moving forward.
statCheck.fail: They probably just enjoy gardening. Not everyone needs a competition to plant things.

entries.intro: {npc_name} snores and everyone knows it. Including people who live three doors down.
details: I brought it up gently and they denied it: 'I breathe heavily,' they said, as if that covers it.
The walls don't help, and I've tried putting cloth in my ears: the snoring gets through.
reactions: I've adjusted my schedule to fall asleep first. If I'm already out, it doesn't wake me.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} told me they snore because of a broken nose from years ago. They're self-conscious about it but can't afford to have it looked at.
statCheck.fail: Some people are heavy sleepers. Nothing to be done about it.

entries.intro: {npc_name} always has a warm drink ready for anyone who's been working outside in the cold.
details: You come in shivering and there's already a cup waiting. Timing that good can't be luck.
They watch the weather and start heating water before anyone's even come back yet.
reactions: It's a small thing. But on a cold day, a warm cup is everything.

entries.intro: {npc_name} asked to borrow a tool and returned it in better condition than when I lent it.
details: Cleaned, oiled, edge resharpened. I almost didn't recognize it as mine.
I should be grateful but now I feel bad about the state it was in when I handed it over.
reactions: I'll lend {npc_name} anything they want. The return alone is worth it.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} told me they return things better because someone once returned something of theirs ruined. They promised themselves they'd never do that to anyone.
statCheck.fail: They're just particular about how things are kept. Good quality in a neighbor.

entries.intro: {npc_name} takes everything personally. You can't say 'it's cold out' without them thinking you blame them for the weather.
details: I mentioned the path was muddy and they asked if I was criticizing how they maintain things. I was talking about mud.
Conversations require careful navigation. Every sentence is a potential mine.
reactions: I stick to compliments and weather around them. Even weather is risky, apparently.

statCheck.pass: Someone told me {npc_name} was criticized a lot when they were younger. The defensiveness isn't about now: it's about then.
statCheck.fail: Some people are just sensitive. You learn to tread lightly.

entries.intro: {npc_name} stretches the truth about how early they wake up. Claims to be up before dawn every day.
details: I'm actually up before dawn. I've never once seen them out that early.
I think 'before dawn' means 'earlier than I'd like' in their vocabulary.
reactions: I let them have it. Everyone needs a small fiction that makes them feel capable.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} told me they lie about it because they feel guilty for sleeping in. The early riser story is the person they wish they were.
statCheck.fail: Everyone stretches the truth about their habits. Harmless.

entries.intro: {npc_name} and {npc_name_2} disagree about everything but eat lunch together every day without fail.
details: They'll argue about the best way to stack firewood and then share bread like nothing happened.
I think the disagreements are how they talk. It's just their language.
reactions: Friendship is stranger than people give it credit for.

entries.intro: {npc_name} defended me in a conversation when I wasn't there. That means more than defending me to my face.
details: Someone was saying unkind things and {npc_name} shut it down. Flatly, clearly, without making a scene.
I heard about it secondhand. {npc_name} never mentioned it.
reactions: I won't forget that. The people who stand up for you behind your back are the real ones.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} told me they defend people because nobody defended them when they needed it. Said they'd rather overdo it than stand by.
statCheck.fail: They're just loyal. Some people look after their own without thinking about it.

entries.intro: {npc_name} has a way of making everything about themselves. Start any story and they'll redirect it.
details: I was telling someone about my morning and before I finished, {npc_name} was talking about their morning instead.
It's not malicious. They just orbit their own experience like it's the only reference point.
reactions: I keep conversations short with them. Easier than competing for the topic.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} told me they redirect because they panic when they're not the center of something. Silence around them feels like rejection.
statCheck.fail: Some people are just talkative. I don't think it's intentional.

entries.intro: {npc_name} nodded off during a conversation with me. Right in the middle of a sentence.
details: Their eyes closed and their chin dipped. I stopped talking and they jerked awake like nothing happened.
They said 'go on' with such confidence I almost believed they'd been listening.
reactions: I wasn't offended, just entertained, and a little worried they're not sleeping enough.

entries.intro: {npc_name} fixed something that was bothering the whole settlement and didn't wait for permission or credit.
details: It just appeared done one morning. The kind of task everyone complained about but nobody touched.
I found out it was {npc_name} because they had the right kind of dirt on their boots.
reactions: The world runs on people who do things without being asked. {npc_name} is one of them.

entries.intro: {npc_name} leaves their things in shared spaces like they live alone.
details: Coat on the bench, bag on the table, boots in the path. Every surface becomes their storage.
I've moved their things twice. Both times they were back in the same spots by evening.
reactions: I've accepted it: the bench is theirs now and I use a different one.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} admitted they grew up alone and never learned to account for others in shared spaces.
statCheck.fail: Maybe they just don't notice. Some people need more reminders than others.

entries.intro: {npc_name} wraps things carefully when they hand them over. Even a borrowed spoon comes back in cloth.
details: I lent them a simple cup and it came back cleaned and wrapped like a gift.
They don't make a point of it. It's just how they handle things that aren't theirs.
reactions: You can tell a lot about a person by how they treat what belongs to someone else.

entries.intro: {npc_name} stares when they're thinking and doesn't realize they're staring at a person.
details: I've been the target twice. You look up and they're locked on you with completely vacant eyes.
I waved once. They blinked, came back from wherever they were, and apologized.
reactions: It's harmless. Just startling if you're not expecting it.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} confessed the staring happens when they're worried about something. Their mind leaves and their eyes fix on whatever's in front of them.
statCheck.fail: They're just a daydreamer. Some people check out when they're thinking.

entries.intro: {npc_name} and {npc_name_2} have been teaching each other things, trading skills back and forth.
details: I've watched the exchange happen over weeks. Neither seems to keep score: they just share what they know.
The settlement benefits because both of them are more capable now than they were a month ago.
reactions: That's how a community is supposed to work. People making each other better.

entries.intro: {npc_name} chews loudly when they eat dried anything: jerky, nuts, bread crusts.
details: I've tried sitting across the room but the jaw has power I didn't expect.
They seem completely unaware. Once offered me some jerky mid-crunch and I declined out of self-preservation.
reactions: I eat soft things around them out of solidarity with my own ears.

entries.intro: You'd think the {npc_role} would be more organized. Every time I go there it's a different kind of chaos.
details: Last time there was a queue that went nowhere because nobody knew who was next.
I've given up expecting order. I just show up and wait.
reactions: As long as the work gets done, I suppose I can tolerate the mess.

statCheck.pass: I've heard the {npc_role} inherited the position and never got proper training. They're making it up as they go.
statCheck.fail: Maybe there's a system I'm not seeing. Though I doubt it.

entries.intro: {npc_name} has an opinion about how everyone else does their work. Always watching, always noting.
details: I caught them watching me from the corner of my eye and when I looked up they quickly looked away.
They never criticize directly, just offer 'suggestions' later, always phrased as gentle observations.
reactions: I'd rather they just said something outright. The polite hovering is worse.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} told me they were a perfectionist by nature and it extends to everyone around them. They know it's a problem.
statCheck.fail: I think they just pay close attention. Not everyone means harm by it.

entries.intro: {npc_name} told me something personal and trusted me to keep it. I will.
details: It wasn't dramatic or scandalous. Just something real they hadn't told anyone else.
The weight of being trusted with something like that settles on you. You carry it differently.
reactions: I'm glad they felt safe enough to say it. That's not nothing.

entries.intro: {npc_name} laughs at inappropriate times. Not at anything funny: just when things go wrong.
details: Something fell and broke yesterday and they burst out laughing while the rest of us groaned.
It's a nervous laugh, I think. But the timing makes it look like joy at misfortune.
reactions: I've gotten used to it. The laugh just means {npc_name} is processing.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} confessed the laughing is a reflex they hate. They can't control it and it's caused problems before.
statCheck.fail: Probably just a strange sense of humor. Not everyone laughs at the same things.

entries.intro: {npc_name} waited until I was done talking before responding, every time, with a full pause before their thoughts.
details: It's rare enough that I noticed. Most people jump in before you finish.
That pause made me feel like what I said actually mattered to them.
reactions: Listening without planning your reply is a skill. {npc_name} has it.

entries.intro: {npc_name} and {npc_name_2} can't agree on how to split chores and everyone else pays for it.
details: What should take a few minutes to sort out becomes a negotiation that drags on all morning.
The rest of us end up doing the chores ourselves while they're still debating.
reactions: I've stopped waiting for them to decide. If it needs doing, I just do it.

entries.intro: {npc_name} brought me extra food without me asking. Just appeared at my door with a plate.
details: I hadn't mentioned being short on anything. They just noticed I'd been busy and figured I might not have eaten.
The food was still warm. They'd come straight from their own table.
reactions: Being seen without asking to be seen. That's what good neighbors do.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} mentioned they went hungry once and swore nobody near them would again if they could help it. The food isn't charity: it's a promise they made.
statCheck.fail: They're just a generous cook. Some people show caring through food.

entries.intro: {npc_name} corrects people in front of others. Facts, pronunciation, anything that's slightly off.
details: I said something approximately right and got an immediate and very public correction. Felt about two inches tall.
It's always accurate, and that's the frustrating part: they're never wrong, just badly timed.
reactions: Being right isn't enough. You have to be right at the right moment, and that's a lesson they haven't learned.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} mentioned they were raised by someone who valued precision above all else. The correcting is inherited, not chosen.
statCheck.fail: Maybe they just can't let things slide. Some people are built to notice errors.

entries.intro: {npc_name} told me I looked well today, nothing more, and it changed my whole morning.
details: I didn't feel well. But after they said it, I stood a little straighter.
Sometimes the wrong compliment at the right time does more good than the right one.
reactions: I've been thinking about saying it to someone else. The words don't cost anything.

entries.intro: {npc_name} fidgets constantly: tapping, bouncing, shifting, like sitting still isn't in their nature.
details: If it's not a foot it's a finger. If it's not a finger it's something in their hands being turned over and over.
I sat next to them for an hour once. By the end I was fidgeting too.
reactions: It's just energy with no place to go. I get it, even if it rattles my nerves.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} told me the fidgeting started after a bad year. Their body carries the restlessness their mind can't settle.
statCheck.fail: Some people just have extra energy. Not everything has a story behind it.

entries.intro: {npc_name} greeted me by name this morning for the first time. We've lived here together for months.
details: I wasn't sure they even knew it until today. Turns out they're just slow to warm up.
The greeting was casual, like they'd been doing it every day. I didn't correct that impression.
reactions: Progress comes at different speeds. I'll take it.

entries.intro: {npc_name} and {npc_name_2} have started a running joke that only they understand.
details: One of them says a single word and the other cracks up. Nobody else gets it.
I asked once and they tried to explain. The explanation made less sense than the joke.
reactions: Shared nonsense is its own kind of bond. I'm happy for them even if I'm excluded.

entries.intro: {npc_name} showed me how to fix something I'd been struggling with for a week. Took them about a minute.
details: They didn't make me feel stupid about it. Just showed the trick and moved on.
I've used the fix six times since. Each time I think of them and feel a little grateful.
reactions: A minute of someone's skill can save you a week of frustration. I owe {npc_name} for that.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} told me they teach because they spent years figuring things out alone. They want to spare people the frustration they went through.
statCheck.fail: Maybe they just enjoy being helpful. Not everyone needs a reason.

entries.intro: {npc_name} keeps bringing up something I said weeks ago that I barely remember. Won't let it go.
details: I made an offhand comment that apparently landed badly. Now it comes back every other conversation.
I've apologized and clarified, but the comment still surfaces like it's on a rotation.
reactions: Some people hold onto words the way others hold onto debts. No amount of payment settles it.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} told me once that they replay conversations in their head at night. The ones that hurt get stuck on repeat.
statCheck.fail: Maybe they have a long memory. Not everyone moves on at the same speed.

entries.intro: {npc_name} said 'thank you' yesterday and meant it so deeply I almost didn't know how to respond.
details: It was for something small. But the way they said it carried the weight of something much larger.
I think they don't say it often. So when it comes, it arrives heavy.
reactions: There's a difference between the words and the feeling behind them. {npc_name} gave me the feeling.

entries.intro: {npc_name} takes forever to make decisions. Choosing what to eat takes longer than eating it.
details: I watched them deliberate over two options for so long the first one went cold.
They weigh everything like it matters equally. A rope or a meal: same level of consideration.
reactions: I've learned to decide for both of us when time is short. They don't seem to mind.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} confided that they deliberate because they've made rushed decisions that cost them. The slowness is a scar, not a preference.
statCheck.fail: Maybe they're just careful. Worse things than thinking twice.

entries.intro: {npc_name} apologized for something so small I hadn't even noticed it.
details: They said sorry for bumping my cup slightly while passing. The cup didn't even move.
The apology was longer than the offense. I spent more time saying 'it's fine' than they spent bumping the cup.
reactions: Overly polite people are exhausting in a gentle, endearing way.

entries.intro: {npc_name} shared something with me that they clearly hadn't told anyone else. I could hear it in their voice.
details: They spoke lower, slower, and kept glancing around. It wasn't a big thing: just a private one.
I'm holding onto it. Not because anyone asked me to, but because it deserves to stay where it was put.
reactions: Trust is the only gift you can't ask for. You either earn it or you don't.

entries.intro: {npc_name} drops hints when they want something instead of just asking.
details: 'Sure is cold without a warm drink.' That means they want me to make tea. I've learned the code.
The hints are so transparent they barely qualify as hints. But asking directly seems impossible for them.
reactions: I make the tea. It's faster than decoding another hint.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} admitted they hint because asking directly makes them feel like a burden. They'd rather be ignored than feel like they're imposing.
statCheck.fail: Some people are just indirect. Not everyone can ask outright.

entries.intro: {npc_name} and {npc_name_2} went through a rough patch but came out of it stronger. You can tell.
details: There was a stretch where they could barely be in the same room. Now they check on each other first thing.
Whatever they worked through, they did it quietly, without dragging anyone else in.
reactions: People who survive disagreement and stay close are more solid than people who've never been tested.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} told me the rough patch was about expectations. They expected too much from each other and had to learn what was fair.
statCheck.fail: I don't know the details. Just that things are better now.

entries.intro: {npc_name} clears their throat before every sentence like they're about to give a speech.
details: Even 'pass the salt' gets a preparatory ahem. The salt does not require ceremony.
I've started counting: seven throat-clearings in one conversation, a new record.
reactions: It's harmless. I just wish my brain would stop noticing it.

entries.intro: {npc_name} stood up for someone who wasn't popular and caught grief for it. Didn't back down.
details: The easy thing would have been to stay quiet. They chose the hard thing and took the looks.
A few people came around afterward, others haven't, and {npc_name} doesn't seem bothered either way.
reactions: Courage isn't always dramatic. Sometimes it's just saying what's fair when it costs you.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} told me they stood up because nobody did it for them once. The silence around injustice is what they remember most from their past.
statCheck.fail: They're just principled. Some people can't stay quiet when things aren't fair.

entries.intro: {npc_name} borrows things and returns them slightly worse. Just enough to notice.
details: A bowl came back with a chip, a cloth came back stained: nothing ruined, just diminished.
I can't prove it's carelessness because none of it is bad enough to mention. Which is the problem.
reactions: I lend them things I don't care about now. The good stuff stays hidden.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} told me they handle borrowed things poorly because they don't value objects the way other people do. Things are just things to them.
statCheck.fail: Maybe they're just careless with small stuff. Not everyone pays attention to wear and tear.

entries.intro: {npc_name} always asks about my family when we talk. Remembers names, remembers details.
details: They follow up on things I mentioned weeks ago. 'How's your brother's knee?' That kind of care.
I don't even remember telling them about my brother's knee. But they remember.
reactions: Attention like that is a form of respect. I try to return it.

entries.intro: {npc_name} talks about people behind their backs but is sweet as honey to their faces.
details: I've heard them say unkind things about someone and then greet that person with a smile ten minutes later.
It makes me wonder what they say about me when I'm not around.
reactions: I keep them at a distance. Kindness that only shows when you're watching isn't kindness.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} admitted to me that they vent behind people's backs because they're afraid of confrontation. The gossip is pressure relief, not cruelty.
statCheck.fail: I don't know what drives it. Maybe they just like talking.

entries.intro: {npc_name} checks on me after bad weather, every time, without fail.
details: Wind, rain, cold snap: they're at my door the next morning asking if everything held together.
I've told them I'm fine and they still check. It's not about whether I need help: it's about showing up.
reactions: Consistency is how you know someone actually cares. {npc_name} is consistent.

entries.intro: {npc_name} always has to have the last word. Every conversation ends on their terms.
details: I tried once to end on my point, but they came back with one more sentence, and it went on for twenty minutes.
I've started just walking away mid-sentence. They still call out a final comment to my back.
reactions: I let them have it now. The peace of ending the conversation is worth more than winning it.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} confided they need the last word because growing up, being interrupted meant being dismissed. They can't let a conversation end on someone else's terms.
statCheck.fail: Some people just like talking. It's not always about power.

entries.intro: {npc_name} and {npc_name_2} have started looking out for each other in ways they didn't used to.
details: One saves food for the other if they're late. The other checks in when something seems off.
Small things. But small things done consistently add up to something real.
reactions: It's nice to watch two people figure out how to care about each other.

entries.intro: {npc_name} walks so quietly you don't hear them until they're right behind you.
details: I've been startled at least four times this week. They appear like a shadow with opinions.
I asked them to cough or something when they approach. They tried, then forgot by the next day.
reactions: My nerves will recover. Eventually.

entries.intro: {npc_name} sat with me when I got bad news and didn't try to fix it.
details: No advice, no silver lining, no 'it'll be fine.' Just sat there and let me be upset.
When I was ready to talk, they listened. When I wasn't, they stayed quiet.
reactions: That kind of patience with someone else's pain is rare. I won't forget it.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} told me later they'd been through something similar and the worst part was people trying to cheer them up before they were ready.
statCheck.fail: I think they just know how to be present. Not everyone does.

entries.intro: {npc_name} points out problems but never offers solutions. Just the problem, delivered and left on your plate.
details: 'The water's low.' Yes, I can see that. 'The path needs work.' Thank you for the update.
They identify issues with precision and then walk away like observation is enough.
reactions: I've started responding with 'and what do you suggest?' It's usually met with silence.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} said pointing out problems makes them feel useful. Without something to criticize, they don't know what to contribute.
statCheck.fail: Maybe they just notice things. Not everyone thinks to offer solutions.

entries.intro: {npc_name} carves little shapes from scraps of wood during idle moments. Leaves them around the settlement.
details: I found one on a post near my place, small and rough, clearly done in a few minutes: a bird, I think.
I've seen others pick them up and look around to see who left them. Nobody claims it.
reactions: There's something lovely about anonymous art. The settlement is dotted with little moments of someone's hands.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} told me they started carving to keep their hands busy when they felt anxious. The leaving-them-around part was an accident that stuck.
statCheck.fail: Probably just a hobby. Some people can't sit still without making something.

entries.intro: {npc_name} and {npc_name_2} avoid each other at meals. Nobody says anything but everyone sees it.
details: If one sits down, the other eats later. The timing is too consistent to be coincidence.
They're civil when forced to interact. But the chill is obvious.
reactions: Whatever happened between them, it's not my business. But it makes communal meals awkward.

statCheck.pass: I heard from someone close to both that a confidence was broken. The kind of thing that damages trust for a long time.
statCheck.fail: I don't know what caused it. Could be something minor that just festered.

entries.intro: {npc_name} remembered something I'd said I wanted to try and set things up for me to do it.
details: I'd mentioned it in passing, the kind of thing you say and forget. They didn't forget.
When the chance came, they'd already handled the hard part. Just said 'go ahead, it's ready.'
reactions: Being heard is one thing. Being acted on is another level entirely.

entries.intro: {npc_name} makes excuses for everything. Nothing is ever their fault: always circumstances.
details: The task wasn't done because the tools weren't right, and the tools weren't right because someone moved them, and so on.
You follow the chain of blame long enough and it loops back to the start without touching {npc_name}.
reactions: I've stopped asking why things aren't done. I just note that they aren't.

statCheck.pass: I think accountability scares them: {npc_name} told me once they grew up being blamed for everything, and now they defend against it before it starts.
statCheck.fail: Maybe they just see things differently. Everyone has a version of events.

entries.intro: {npc_name} catches your eye across a room and gives you a nod that says more than a conversation would.
details: It's a 'we're fine, you're fine, everything's handled' kind of nod. Efficient communication.
I don't know how a head movement can carry that much meaning, but with {npc_name} it does.
reactions: Some people need fewer words than others. I respect that.

entries.intro: {npc_name} thanked me for something I did months ago. Out of nowhere, completely unprompted.
details: I had forgotten doing it, but they hadn't: the delay made the thanks land harder.
They said they'd been meaning to say something and finally did. That's not late gratitude: that's the real kind.
reactions: Late thanks are sometimes the truest kind. The feeling stuck with them long enough to speak.

entries.intro: {npc_name} whistles the same three notes every time they pass my place. I can time my day by it.
details: Morning: three notes going out, evening: three notes coming back, rain or shine.
I've started looking forward to it. If the whistle's late, something feels off about the whole day.
reactions: Funny how a stranger's habit can become part of your routine.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} told me the three notes are from a song they used to share with someone. It's the only part they still whistle.
statCheck.fail: Probably just a walking habit. Some people whistle without thinking.

entries.intro: {npc_name} lent me their coat when I was cold without me saying I was cold.
details: They just draped it over my shoulders mid-conversation and kept talking. Like it was nothing.
I tried to give it back and they waved me off. 'I run warm,' they said.
reactions: Generosity that arrives before you ask for it: that's the kind that stays with you.

entries.intro: {npc_name} agreed with me to my face and then did the opposite. I saw the results.
details: We had a clear plan with nodding and agreement, then they went and did what they wanted anyway.
When I asked about it they acted confused. As if the conversation never happened.
reactions: I write things down now when {npc_name} is involved. Memory is convenient when it's selective.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} told me later they agreed because they didn't want conflict. The doing-their-own-thing was the real answer they couldn't say out loud.
statCheck.fail: Maybe they just changed their mind. People do that.

entries.intro: {npc_name} and {npc_name_2} bicker like siblings but would defend each other against anyone else.
details: I said something mildly critical of {npc_name_2} and {npc_name} shut me down immediately. Same person who was complaining about them an hour earlier.
There's a line between 'my people' and 'everyone else.' With those two, the line is very clear.
reactions: I've learned not to agree when either of them complains about the other. That invitation is a trap.

entries.intro: {npc_name} brought back something useful from a trip and gave it away before anyone knew they had it.
details: I only found out because the person who received it mentioned where it came from.
They didn't announce it, didn't wait for thanks. Just handed it over and moved on.
reactions: Generosity done quietly speaks louder than the kind that wants an audience.

entries.intro: {npc_name} says 'interesting' when they mean 'I disagree.' I've cracked the code.
details: The tone is flat, the eyebrow lifts slightly, and you know you've said something they'd push back on if they bothered.
I tested it once by saying something obviously wrong. 'Interesting.' Right on cue.
reactions: I'd prefer honest disagreement, but at least I know what 'interesting' means now.

entries.intro: {npc_name} pulled me aside and said something encouraging when no one else was around.
details: Not a public show, just a quiet word between two people: it hit different because it was private.
They could have said nothing and no one would have known. That's what makes it matter.
reactions: Private encouragement is the honest kind. No audience to perform for.

entries.intro: {npc_name} asks to borrow things and then acts like they're doing you a favor by using them.
details: 'I'll put your bucket to good use.' Thank you for honoring my bucket with your attention.
The gratitude runs backward with them. Somehow I end up feeling thanked for being borrowed from.
reactions: I've stopped trying to understand it. The bucket always comes back, so I let the logic go.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} told me the borrowing thing is a power move they learned young. Taking small liberties without asking makes them feel in control.
statCheck.fail: I think they just don't see food as individual property. Different upbringing maybe.

entries.intro: {npc_name} squints at people when they're talking. Not suspiciously: more like they're reading your face very carefully.
details: It's disconcerting at first. Feels like they're looking for something behind your words.
After a while you realize they're just paying very close attention. The squint is just how they focus.
reactions: I'd rather someone listen too hard than not listen at all.

entries.intro: {npc_name} made something for me with their own hands. Didn't cost them money: just time and thought.
details: It's not perfect. The edges are rough and one side is slightly uneven.
But someone sat down, thought about what I needed, and made it. I wouldn't trade it for a perfect version.
reactions: Handmade things carry the maker with them. I look at it and think of {npc_name}.

entries.intro: {npc_name} complains about the noise but they're the loudest person here.
details: Slamming doors, shouting across spaces, dropping things heavily. Then someone else makes a sound and it's a problem.
I pointed it out once. They looked at me like I'd invented the concept of hypocrisy on the spot.
reactions: I don't bring it up anymore. The irony is entertainment enough.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} admitted once that they don't hear their own noise. When I described what they sound like, they were genuinely surprised.
statCheck.fail: Maybe they just lack self-awareness about it. Wouldn't be the first.

entries.intro: {npc_name} and {npc_name_2} were assigned the same task and somehow both did it. Twice the work, nobody coordinated.
details: We had two of everything done and neither knew the other was working on it.
They blamed each other for not checking. I blame them both equally.
reactions: At least the task is thoroughly done. Silver linings.

entries.intro: {npc_name} noticed I'd been quiet and checked in without making a fuss.
details: Just a 'you alright?' as they passed by, low voice, no audience: perfect delivery.
I wasn't entirely alright. But knowing someone noticed helped more than they'll ever know.
reactions: Two words: that's all it took, and some people know exactly how much is enough.

entries.intro: {npc_name} insists on doing things the hard way and acts proud of the struggle.
details: I showed them an easier method and they waved it off. 'That's not how it should be done.'
The harder way took three times as long and the result was the same. But they were satisfied.
reactions: I admire the principle. I just don't share it.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} told me they believe the effort is part of the result. That something done easily carries less weight.
statCheck.fail: Maybe they just prefer the familiar way. Change is hard for some.

entries.intro: {npc_name} gave me the last of something without telling me it was the last.
details: I found out later they went without because of it. They never mentioned it.
When I tried to repay them they brushed it off and said 'I had plenty,' but they didn't.
reactions: Quiet sacrifice is the hardest kind to repay because you're not supposed to know about it.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} confessed they'd rather go without than see someone else do the same: it's not heroism, it's compulsion.
statCheck.fail: I think they're just generous. Some people give without keeping count.

entries.intro: {npc_name} tells the same stories over and over. I could recite them from memory.
details: The punchlines and setup haven't changed, but {npc_name} delivers them each time like they're brand new.
I've heard the one about the barrel at least five times. I still smile because the enthusiasm is real.
reactions: I don't have the heart to stop them. The joy in the telling matters more than the content.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} confided the stories are all they have from before they came here. Telling them keeps the past alive in a way nothing else does.
statCheck.fail: Some people just like their own stories. Nothing wrong with a well-worn tale.

entries.intro: {npc_name} offered to take my shift when they could see I was struggling. I didn't even have to ask.
details: They just said 'go rest, I've got this' and started working before I could argue.
I don't think I looked that bad. But apparently bad enough for {npc_name} to step in.
reactions: That kind of attentiveness only comes from someone who's been in the same spot before.

entries.intro: {npc_name} never contributes ideas but always has objections to everyone else's.
details: I proposed something at the last gathering and got three reasons why it wouldn't work. No alternatives offered.
They sit back, wait for someone to suggest something, and then explain why it's flawed.
reactions: Criticism without contribution is just noise. I've started tuning it out.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} told me once that they're afraid of proposing something and being wrong. It's easier to find flaws than risk having your own ideas picked apart.
statCheck.fail: Maybe they're just cautious by nature. Some people see problems before possibilities.

entries.intro: {npc_name} and {npc_name_2} always walk together in the morning. Same pace, same direction, like a routine neither planned.
details: I asked if they coordinate and both said no. They just end up in step.
It's the kind of thing that just happens between people who are comfortable with each other.
reactions: Some friendships don't need planning. They just settle into a rhythm.

entries.intro: {npc_name} shakes your hand like they're trying to detach it. Every time.
details: I've braced for it and still gotten jolted. The grip is industrial.
I saw someone new meet them for the first time and watched the shock travel up their arm.
reactions: I offer a wave now. My shoulder thanks me.

entries.intro: {npc_name} helped me with something and refused anything in return. Not even thanks.
details: I tried to give them something for the trouble and they actually seemed offended.
They said helping was the default and that I didn't owe them for doing what anyone should.
reactions: I respect that. But I'm going to help them back anyway, whether they like it or not.

entries.intro: {npc_name} watches people leave conversations and mutters comments under their breath after.
details: I turned around once and caught the tail end of something. They froze like I'd caught them stealing.
I don't know if the muttering is critical or just processing. But it feels pointed.
reactions: I walk away a little faster now. What I don't hear can't bother me.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} admitted the muttering is how they decompress. Said they think out loud and don't always realize they're doing it.
statCheck.fail: Could be nothing. People talk to themselves all the time.

entries.intro: {npc_name} taps their foot when they're waiting. Not impatiently: rhythmically, like there's music only they hear.
details: It's a steady beat, same tempo every time: I've started associating it with {npc_name} the way you'd associate a song with a place.
I asked what they're tapping to. They looked at their foot, surprised, and said 'I didn't know I was.'
reactions: We're all made of habits we can't see. At least theirs has rhythm.

entries.intro: {npc_name} picked up a skill I thought was hard and learned it in half the time it took me.
details: I spent weeks struggling with it. They watched once, tried twice, and had it.
I wasn't jealous: well, maybe a little, but mostly impressed.
reactions: Some people just learn fast. It's not about effort: it's wiring.

entries.intro: {npc_name} and {npc_name_2} swapped responsibilities for a week and both admitted the other's job was harder.
details: {npc_name} was exhausted by day three. {npc_name_2} lasted four before asking to switch back.
The mutual respect after that swap was visible. Neither has complained about the other's work since.
reactions: Everyone should try someone else's work for a week. It changes what you take for granted.

entries.intro: {npc_name} leaves conversations without saying goodbye. Just turns and walks off mid-topic.
details: I was still talking when they started walking away. Not rude exactly: just done.
I've learned to watch for the signs. When their weight shifts to one foot, the departure is imminent.
reactions: I've started doing the same thing. It's oddly liberating.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} told me they leave without goodbye because farewells feel heavy to them. They'd rather just go than make ending a thing.
statCheck.fail: Maybe they're just socially abrupt. Not everyone follows the same script.

entries.intro: {npc_name} came to me with an apology I didn't think I needed. But hearing it, I realized I did.
details: The thing they apologized for was small. But it had been sitting in the back of my mind without me noticing.
The apology cleared something between us. Like opening a window I forgot was shut.
reactions: An apology you didn't ask for is sometimes the one that matters most.

entries.intro: {npc_name} said they'd help and then forgot. Not once: three separate times.
details: Each time they seemed genuinely surprised I reminded them, always an 'oh, right, I meant to' with no follow-through.
I've stopped asking and started handling things myself. Faster than waiting.
reactions: I don't hold it against them. But I don't rely on them either.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} admitted they forget because they overcommit. They say yes to too many people and then can't keep track.
statCheck.fail: Maybe they just have a bad memory. Some people do.

entries.intro: {npc_name} counts things under their breath. Steps, items on a table, people in a room.
details: I watched them count the number of posts along a fence once, for no reason, and they just counted.
They looked satisfied at the end, like knowing the number completed something.
reactions: I've started counting things too: it's calming, and I understand the appeal now.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} told me counting is how they keep their mind steady, and without it, thoughts scatter: the numbers are an anchor.
statCheck.fail: Some people just like numbers. Not everything has a deep reason.

entries.intro: {npc_name} asked me how my day was and actually waited through my long answer. The whole thing.
details: Most people ask out of habit and are already walking away by the third word. {npc_name} stood still.
I went on too long and they still didn't interrupt. I almost apologized for talking so much.
reactions: I didn't need anyone to solve my day. I just needed someone to hear it.

entries.intro: {npc_name} takes compliments like they're accusations. Every nice thing I say gets deflected.
details: 'You did good work.' 'It was nothing.' 'You look rested.' 'I barely slept.' It's a pattern.
I've tried different angles: direct, indirect, casual, and all bounced back.
reactions: I keep saying the kind things anyway. Maybe one will land eventually.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} told me they don't trust compliments because too many came with strings attached. The deflection is armor.
statCheck.fail: Maybe they're just modest. Some people don't know how to take a nice word.

entries.intro: {npc_name} put in a good word for me when it counted. I found out after the fact.
details: Someone told me {npc_name} had spoken up on my behalf. Clearly, specifically, without being asked.
They never mentioned it to me. I only know because the person they spoke to told me later.
reactions: I owe them. And the best way to repay it is to do the same for someone else.

entries.intro: {npc_name} 'borrows' food off other people's plates. A little bite here, a quick grab there.
details: They reach across without asking, take something, and eat it before you've processed what happened.
I've started eating with one arm around my plate like a barrier. It works about half the time.
reactions: I don't think they see it as stealing: they see it as communal, but the problem is nobody else does.

entries.intro: {npc_name} and {npc_name_2} had a misunderstanding that blew over faster than anyone expected.
details: One morning they weren't speaking. By afternoon they were sharing a bench like nothing happened.
I don't know who apologized first. I suspect it was both, at the same time.
reactions: That's how it should work: disagreement, resolution, move on, no lingering.

entries.intro: {npc_name} sniffs loudly and constantly. Not sick: just always sniffing.
details: It's a sharp, rhythmic sniff every few seconds. I've tried to time my breathing to it and can't.
I offered them a cloth once. They looked at it, confused, and said 'what for?'
reactions: Some things you just learn to live with. This is one of them.

entries.intro: {npc_name} gives advice that sounds simple but turns out to be exactly right.
details: They said 'start smaller' about something I was overcomplicating. I ignored it, failed, then tried it their way.
The advice never sounds impressive when they say it. It only sounds impressive when it works.
reactions: I've started asking {npc_name} first before I overthink things.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} told me they learned by making every mistake first. The simple advice comes from having done it the hard way.
statCheck.fail: Maybe they just have good instincts. Some people see straight to the point.

entries.intro: {npc_name} paces when they're thinking. Back and forth, same path, wearing a line in the ground.
details: You can tell when a big decision is coming because the pacing intensifies. Faster steps, tighter turns.
Once they stop and stand still, they've decided. The announcement follows within seconds.
reactions: I've learned to wait out the pacing. The conclusion is usually worth the walking.

entries.intro: {npc_name} smiles when they greet you. Not the polite kind: the kind that reaches the eyes.
details: It's warm every time, like they're genuinely glad to see you. Even if you just saw them an hour ago.
I've started smiling back before I even think about it. It's involuntary at this point.
reactions: People who make you feel welcome without trying are the backbone of any place.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} told me the smile is something they choose to do every morning. They decided long ago to greet the world warmly even when they don't feel it.
statCheck.fail: Some people are just warm by nature. No trick to it.

entries.intro: {npc_name} takes the best seat every time and somehow nobody challenges them on it.
details: They arrive, go straight to it, and sit down with the authority of someone who built the bench.
I sat in it once before they arrived. The look I got when they found it occupied could curdle milk.
reactions: It's their seat now. I've made peace with that.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} told me the seat was the first thing they claimed when they arrived here. It was the only thing that felt like theirs in a new place.
statCheck.fail: Maybe they're just territorial. Everyone needs a spot.

entries.intro: {npc_name} and {npc_name_2} are so different I can't figure out how they get along. But they do.
details: One's quiet, the other never stops talking. One plans everything, the other improvises.
On paper it shouldn't work. In practice, they balance each other perfectly.
reactions: Friendship doesn't follow rules. The strangest pairings are sometimes the strongest.

entries.intro: {npc_name} waved at me from across the way today: small thing, but it made my whole morning better.
details: Just a hand up and a quick nod. Took maybe two seconds of their day.
I spent the rest of the morning in a noticeably better mood and traced it back to that wave.
reactions: Two seconds of someone's attention can carry you through hours. Strange how that works.

entries.intro: {npc_name} always seems to be chewing on something: a twig, a bit of grass, a straw.
details: I asked what they were chewing once and they pulled out a stick and looked at it like they'd forgotten it was there.
It doesn't bother anyone. It's just a detail you notice and can never un-notice.
reactions: We all have our things. Theirs happens to involve their mouth and nearby plant life.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} told me the chewing calms their nerves. Without something in their mouth, their jaw tenses and they get headaches.
statCheck.fail: Just an old habit. Probably started young and never stopped.

entries.intro: The {npc_role} has been doing well lately. Things run smoother when someone actually cares about the work.
details: Jobs get done on time, fairly, without the usual complaints. People have noticed.
I don't know if anything changed or if they just found their stride. Either way, it's welcome.
reactions: Competence shouldn't be surprising, but after what we've had before, I appreciate it.

entries.intro: {npc_name} asks for opinions and then ignores them completely. Every single time.
details: I tell them what I think, they nod, and then they do exactly what they were going to do before asking.
I've tested it by giving absurd suggestions. Same nod, same result.
reactions: The asking is a ritual, not a consultation. I've accepted my role as audience.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} confessed they ask for validation, not input. The decision is already made: the asking is about feeling less alone in it.
statCheck.fail: Maybe they just process decisions by talking them through. The outcome was never in doubt.

entries.intro: {npc_name} does a thing with their eyebrows when they're amused. Both go up and stay there.
details: The eyebrows rise and hold position while they process the humor. Then slowly lower as the smile arrives.
I've started watching for it. The eyebrow phase is funnier than whatever caused it.
reactions: People's faces tell stories their mouths haven't started yet.

entries.intro: {npc_name} told me they appreciated something I do that I didn't think anyone noticed.
details: It was such a small habit I'd stopped thinking of it as a choice. Just something I did.
Hearing that it mattered to someone made me want to keep doing it. Deliberately now, not by default.
reactions: Noticing what's invisible is its own kind of gift.

entries.intro: {npc_name} and {npc_name_2} keep score on who's done more work this week. The tally is public and contested.
details: {npc_name} marked something done and {npc_name_2} challenged whether it counted. Twenty minutes of debate.
The rest of us have been outproducing both of them while they argue about the score.
reactions: Competition is only useful when it produces results. This one produces noise.

entries.intro: {npc_name} leans against things: walls, posts, fences, people, always leaning.
details: I'm not sure I've seen them stand unsupported. There's always something nearby taking part of their weight.
They leaned on me once during a conversation, literally just slowly shifted over until I was bearing load.
reactions: I stepped sideways, they found a post, and the cycle continues.

entries.intro: {npc_name} is the kind of person who makes hard work look easy. It isn't: I've tried.
details: I watched them do in one afternoon what would take me two days. Same tools, same conditions.
It's not speed, it's efficiency: every movement counts and nothing's wasted.
reactions: I'd be jealous if I wasn't so busy being impressed.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} told me it took years of doing it badly before they got smooth. The effortlessness cost them a lot of effort.
statCheck.fail: Maybe they're just naturally good with their hands. Some people are.

entries.intro: {npc_name} and {npc_name_2} always take the same side in any discussion. Doesn't matter what it's about.
details: If {npc_name} says left, {npc_name_2} says left. I've tested this with topics neither cares about.
I think it's loyalty rather than agreement. They back each other on principle.
reactions: It must be nice to have someone in your corner by default. I'd take that.

entries.intro: {npc_name} uses too many words when one would do. Everything is a speech.
details: I asked if they were hungry and got a five-sentence response about the nature of appetite.
By the end I'd forgotten the question. I think the answer was yes.
reactions: I've learned to listen for the conclusion and skip the journey.

entries.intro: {npc_name} stayed behind to clean up after everyone left. Didn't need to.
details: The rest of us went home and they stayed, putting things back, sweeping up, making things right.
I came back for something I'd forgotten and found them still at it. They looked content.
reactions: People who take care of things after the work is done are the ones keeping everything from falling apart.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} told me they clean up last because it's when they feel most useful. The quiet after everyone leaves is where they find their purpose.
statCheck.fail: Some people just can't leave a mess. It's a reflex.

entries.intro: {npc_name} said something I've been thinking about for three days. Can't get it out of my head.
details: It wasn't meant to be profound, just an observation about how people change, but it stuck.
I keep turning it over, finding new angles. They'd probably be surprised it landed this hard.
reactions: The things that stay with you are rarely the ones people planned.

entries.intro: {npc_name} is generous with their time but stingy with their opinions. Getting a real answer takes patience.
details: They'll help you all day without complaint, but ask what they think about something and you get a shrug.
I've learned that the real opinions come out late, when they've forgotten to guard them.
reactions: I don't push. The honest thoughts arrive when they're ready.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} told me opinions got them in trouble before. So they keep them quiet until they know who they're talking to.
statCheck.fail: Maybe they just don't think their opinion matters. Some people undervalue their own thoughts.

entries.intro: {npc_name} included me in something when they didn't have to. Went out of their way.
details: They walked across the settlement to tell me about something they thought I'd enjoy. Took effort.
Nobody asked them to: they just thought of me, and that's worth more than most gestures.
reactions: Being thought of without being present: that's how you know you matter to someone.

entries.intro: {npc_name} only talks about themselves. I know their entire life story and they've never asked about mine.
details: I tried redirecting once and started a sentence about my own day, but they pivoted it back to theirs within three words.
It's a skill, honestly. An annoying, perfectly executed skill.
reactions: I listen because I'm polite. But the traffic only goes one way.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} confided that talking about themselves is how they keep people from asking questions they don't want to answer. The chatter is a shield.
statCheck.fail: Some people are just self-centered. It's not always complicated.

entries.intro: {npc_name} rubbed their eyes when I told them something kind. I think it caught them off guard.
details: They played it off, but there was a pause, a softening, just for a second.
I don't think they hear kind things often enough. That bothers me.
reactions: I'm going to say kind things more. To everyone, but especially to them.

entries.intro: {npc_name} and {npc_name_2} have private conversations they stop the moment someone walks up.
details: I approached and the talking switched to weather so fast I nearly got whiplash.
I'm not offended: everyone needs private words, but the speed of the switch is comedic.
reactions: Whatever they discuss, it's not my business. The weather updates are getting creative though.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} told me they talk about personal things and don't want the whole settlement weighing in. Fair enough.
statCheck.fail: Probably just a private conversation. Not everything is a secret.

entries.intro: {npc_name} has never once asked me for anything, not once, in all the time we've been neighbors.
details: Everyone else has needed a hand, a tool, a favor. {npc_name} manages everything alone.
I've offered and been turned down so gently it didn't even sting.
reactions: Independence is admirable. But I wish they'd let someone in once in a while.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} told me that asking for help feels like failing, and they know it's not rational but the feeling is real.
statCheck.fail: Maybe they just don't need much. Some people are more self-sufficient than others.

entries.intro: {npc_name} puts other people's tools back in the wrong place and never realizes.
details: My things end up in {npc_name}'s spot, their things end up in mine. It's a slow migration.
I've reorganized three times. It drifts back within a week.
reactions: I'm considering just labeling everything. But knowing {npc_name}, they'd reorganize the labels too.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} admitted they have trouble remembering which things belong where because everything looks the same to them. They genuinely try.
statCheck.fail: Maybe they're just disorganized. Not everyone has a system.

entries.intro: {npc_name} looked tired today and I told them to take a break. They actually listened.
details: Normally they push through everything. The fact they sat down tells me they really needed it.
I brought them water and we sat in quiet for a while. They looked better after.
reactions: Sometimes people need permission to rest. I'm glad I gave it.

entries.intro: {npc_name} makes promises in the moment that they can't keep. Heart's in the right place, calendar isn't.
details: They say yes to everything and then scramble when three commitments overlap.
I've watched them run between obligations looking panicked while insisting everything is fine.
reactions: I appreciate the eagerness. I just wish they'd check with reality first.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} told me they can't say no because being needed is the only thing that makes them feel valuable. The overcommitting is the price.
statCheck.fail: They're just eager. Some people want to help with everything.

entries.intro: {npc_name} nods along enthusiastically when they agree with you. The whole upper body gets involved.
details: It's like they're agreeing with their entire skeleton. Head, shoulders, sometimes the hands join in.
It's encouraging in a way that makes you want to keep talking just to see the nodding continue.
reactions: Everyone deserves someone who agrees that hard. It makes you feel like your thoughts have weight.

entries.intro: {npc_name} caught me struggling and jumped in without a word. Just started working next to me.
details: No 'do you need help?' No assessment. Just hands on the work.
We finished in half the time and they went back to what they were doing like a detour in a river.
reactions: The people who help without asking are the ones who've needed help and didn't get it.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} told me they can't walk past someone struggling. Said it makes their skin crawl to see work going wrong when they have hands.
statCheck.fail: They're just a helpful person. The world needs more of those.

entries.intro: {npc_name} one-ups every story: tell them about your bad day and theirs was worse, always.
details: I mentioned I barely slept and they launched into a tale of three sleepless nights. Immediately.
It's not competition in their mind, I think: it's how they try to relate, but it doesn't feel that way.
reactions: I've started keeping things vague around them. Less to one-up.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} admitted the one-upping is how they try to connect, thinking sharing a similar experience shows understanding, but it doesn't land that way.
statCheck.fail: Maybe they just relate through comparison. Awkward but not mean.

entries.intro: {npc_name} and {npc_name_2} trust each other with things they wouldn't tell anyone else. You can see it.
details: The way they lean in when they talk. The way one glances around before the other starts speaking.
That kind of closeness is earned, not given. Whatever they've been through built something solid.
reactions: Real trust between two people makes a whole settlement feel safer somehow.

entries.intro: {npc_name} and {npc_name_2} talk at the same time and neither backs down. Conversations with both are exhausting.
details: Two simultaneous monologues running in parallel. You pick one to follow and lose the other.
I asked them to take turns once. They agreed, waited exactly one sentence, and overlapped again.
reactions: I talk to them separately now. It's the only way to actually hear what either is saying.

entries.intro: {npc_name} cracks their knuckles one at a time, slowly, like a countdown.
details: Ten pops, spaced out. You hear it before you see them sometimes.
I've asked if it hurts and they said no; I've asked if it could stop and they said probably not.
reactions: I've made peace with the popping. It's just part of the ambient sound here now.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} told me the knuckle-cracking started when they were young and scared. It was a way to feel something when everything else felt numb.
statCheck.fail: It's just a habit. Everyone fidgets in some way.

entries.intro: {npc_name} told me something encouraging at a time when I needed it badly. I don't think they knew.
details: It was offhand for them. A passing remark about something I'd been doing right.
For me it was the thing that kept me going that week. They'll never know the scale of it.
reactions: We don't always know which of our words land. That's why it matters to say the kind ones.

entries.intro: You'd think the {npc_role} would set a better example. But I suppose even the position doesn't change the person.
details: Same habits, same shortcuts. The title changed but the work ethic didn't.
Other folk have started to notice. Nobody's said anything yet, but the grumbling is building.
reactions: I keep my head down and do my own work. That's all I can control.

statCheck.pass: I've heard the {npc_role} didn't want the position, and was given it because nobody else would take it: hard to blame someone for being average at something they never chose.
statCheck.fail: Maybe I expect too much. Not everyone grows into a role.

entries.intro: {npc_name} keeps a consistent mood. Good days, bad days: same steady presence.
details: You never have to guess which version of them you're going to get. It's always the same one.
In a place where everyone's mood swings with the weather, that steadiness is an anchor.
reactions: Reliability in temperament is underrated. I seek them out on the hard days because of it.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} told me the evenness is work. They feel the swings like anyone else: they just don't show them.
statCheck.fail: Some people are just built steady. It's a gift.

entries.intro: {npc_name} and {npc_name_2} share tools without tracking who owns what anymore. It just works.
details: A saw moves between them as needed: neither asks, neither thanks, it's just there when it's needed.
I tried joining the arrangement and immediately got confused about whose hammer I had.
reactions: It only works between people who trust each other completely. I'm not there yet.

entries.intro: {npc_name} walked past me this morning like I wasn't there. Eyes forward, no greeting, nothing.
details: I was standing right in the path. Not hidden, not off to the side: right there.
Other people have mentioned the same thing. It's not personal, apparently: they do it to everyone.
reactions: I've stopped expecting acknowledgment. Makes the rare nod feel like a holiday.

entries.intro: {npc_name} brought me something to eat without being asked. Just showed up with a plate.
details: I hadn't mentioned being hungry. They just noticed I'd been working through the afternoon.
It was simple but warm, and the timing was perfect. I nearly thanked them twice.
reactions: Kindness that arrives unannounced is the best kind.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} told me they bring food because they know what it's like to be too busy to stop. They've been there.
statCheck.fail: They're just considerate. Some people pay attention to what others need.

entries.intro: {npc_name} has a habit of finishing other people's sentences. Never gets them right either.
details: I started saying 'I was thinking about...' and they jumped in with something completely wrong.
The confidence with which they guess wrong is almost impressive. No hesitation whatsoever.
reactions: I've learned to talk faster. It's the only defense.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} told me they finish sentences because their mind races ahead of conversation and they can't stop it. It's not impatience: it's overflow.
statCheck.fail: Maybe they just get excited. Some people's mouths outrun the room.

entries.intro: I don't see {npc_name} often, but when I do, they always remember what we last talked about.
details: We spoke weeks ago about something minor and they picked it right back up like no time had passed.
It's a small thing, but it makes you feel like the conversation mattered to them.
reactions: I've started trying to do the same for others. It's harder than it looks.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} told me they keep a mental record of conversations because forgetting what someone said feels disrespectful to them.
statCheck.fail: They just have a good memory. Some people retain things better than others.

entries.intro: {npc_name} and {npc_name_2} had an argument over something so small I can't even remember what it was.
details: Voices went up, hands started gesturing, and the rest of us found somewhere else to be.
By evening they were fine. Whatever it was, it burned hot and fast and left no mark.
reactions: Some pairs just run hot. Doesn't mean anything's broken.

entries.intro: {npc_name} talks to animals like they're people. Full conversations, complete with pauses for replies.
details: I overheard them asking a bird about its morning. The bird left and they looked mildly offended.
They're aware they do it and don't see the problem. 'Better listeners than most,' they said.
reactions: I can't argue with the logic. The bird did seem attentive.

entries.intro: {npc_name} gave me advice last week that saved me half a day's work. Just a few words.
details: I was overcomplicating things and they said 'try it the other way round.' That was it.
I tried it, and they were right. Half the effort, same result.
reactions: Experience talks in short sentences. I should listen more.

entries.intro: {npc_name} eats with their mouth open. I can hear it from three seats away.
details: It's rhythmic: chew, smack, chew, smack. Like a percussion section nobody wanted.
I've tried sitting further away but the sound carries. It has range.
reactions: I time my meals differently now. Cowardly, but effective.

entries.intro: {npc_name} remembers birthdays. Not just mine: everyone's.
details: I don't know how they keep track. Nobody tells them, they just know.
A quiet word in the morning, maybe something small they made. Never a fuss, just recognition.
reactions: Being remembered on a day that matters to you: that's worth more than most gifts.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} told me they keep a list because forgetting someone's day once made them feel terrible. The list started with one name and grew.
statCheck.fail: They just have a good memory for dates. Some people are wired for it.

entries.intro: {npc_name} never admits they're wrong. Even when the proof is standing right in front of them.
details: I showed them exactly where they'd made the mistake and they found a way to blame the materials.
It's not even stubbornness at this point: it's a reflex. The word 'sorry' simply doesn't exist in their vocabulary.
reactions: I've stopped correcting them. The argument costs more than the mistake.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} told me admitting fault feels like losing ground and they've lost too much ground already. The defensiveness is survival, not arrogance.
statCheck.fail: Some people just can't bend. That's all there is to it.

entries.intro: {npc_name} hums while they work. Same tune, every day, never gets old somehow.
details: I don't know the song and I've never asked. It's become part of the background here.
When they're not around, the silence where the humming should be is noticeable.
reactions: Some people fill a space just by being in it. {npc_name} fills it with sound.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} told me the tune is one their mother hummed. They don't remember the words, just the melody.
statCheck.fail: Probably just a song they like. Not everything has a story behind it.

entries.intro: {npc_name} and {npc_name_2} split a task between them so cleanly it looked rehearsed.
details: No discussion, no negotiation: {npc_name} started on one end, {npc_name_2} on the other, met in the middle.
The rest of us were still figuring out who does what while they were already halfway done.
reactions: That kind of coordination only comes from knowing someone well. It's earned, not planned.

entries.intro: {npc_name} stares when they're thinking. Not at anything: through you.
details: I waved a hand in front of their face once and got no response for a full three seconds.
When they came back they had the answer to something nobody had asked yet.
reactions: I've stopped being unnerved by it. The staring means the thinking is deep.

entries.intro: {npc_name} defended me in a conversation I wasn't part of. I heard about it later.
details: Someone was talking about me unfairly and {npc_name} shut it down. Firmly, but not meanly.
They never mentioned it. I only know because the person they corrected told me.
reactions: Having someone stand up for you when you're not in the room: that's real.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} told me they defend people because nobody defended them when they needed it. The correction is for the past as much as the present.
statCheck.fail: They're just fair-minded. Some people won't let nonsense stand.

entries.intro: {npc_name} borrows things and returns them in worse condition than they left. Every time.
details: Lent them a bucket last week. Came back with a crack and dirt inside that wasn't there before.
They hand it back with a smile like they've done me a favor. The audacity is almost endearing.
reactions: I've started keeping a 'lending set' of things I don't care about. Problem solved.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} admitted they don't notice the damage because they treat everything like they'd treat their own, and their own things are in similar shape.
statCheck.fail: They're probably just rough with things. Not everyone handles objects with care.

entries.intro: {npc_name} and {npc_name_2} avoid each other and everyone pretends not to notice.
details: One walks in, the other finds a reason to leave. The timing is too consistent to be coincidence.
Nobody knows what happened between them, or if they do, nobody's talking about it.
reactions: It's their business. As long as it stays quiet, the rest of us stay out of it.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} told me it was a disagreement about something personal that can't be undone. They're not angry, just done.
statCheck.fail: People drift apart. It doesn't always need a dramatic reason.

entries.intro: {npc_name} is the first person I'd go to in an emergency. No question.
details: They're calm when things go sideways. While everyone else panics, they start thinking.
I've seen them handle pressure that would flatten most people and come out steady.
reactions: Knowing someone like that is around makes the hard days feel manageable.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} told me they stay calm because panicking once cost them dearly. The steadiness is a lesson they paid for.
statCheck.fail: Some people are just unshakeable. Built for pressure.

entries.intro: {npc_name} whistles the same three notes whenever they pass by. I hear it in my sleep now.
details: Three notes, always the same order, always the same pitch. It's embedded in my skull.
I whistled it back once and they looked at me like I'd learned their language.
reactions: I can't unhear it. That melody is part of this place for me now.

entries.intro: {npc_name} lent me a hand when nobody else would. I won't forget that.
details: It was a thankless job and everyone had excuses. {npc_name} just showed up and got to work.
Didn't ask for anything in return. Didn't even bring it up after.
reactions: When you find out who's reliable, you hold onto that information.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} told me they help with the thankless jobs because those are the ones that taught them who their real friends were.
statCheck.fail: They're just reliable. Not everyone needs a reason to show up.

entries.intro: {npc_name} sighs before every response. Like speaking is a great burden they've been asked to bear.
details: You ask a question, get the sigh, then the answer. It's a three-part process.
I've started counting the sighs per conversation. My record is eleven.
reactions: I don't take it personally anymore. The sigh is just punctuation for them.

entries.intro: {npc_name} sat with me when I was having a rough day. Didn't say much, just sat.
details: No advice, no questions, no 'it'll be fine.' Just presence.
After a while I felt better, not because anything changed but because someone was there.
reactions: Silence from the right person is worth more than words from anyone else.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} told me they sat with me because someone once sat with them during a bad time and it changed everything. They're passing it on.
statCheck.fail: Some people just know when to be present. It's instinct.

entries.intro: {npc_name} and {npc_name_2} have been bickering about the same thing for as long as I can remember.
details: I don't even think they know what the original disagreement was. It's just tradition at this point.
The rest of us have stopped intervening. It's like weather: predictable and unavoidable.
reactions: Honestly, I think they'd miss the arguing if it stopped.

entries.intro: I asked {npc_name} how they were doing and got an honest answer for once. That's rare around here.
details: No 'fine,' no deflection. Just a real answer about how they actually felt.
It caught me off guard. I'd been expecting the usual polite nothing.
reactions: Honesty like that makes me want to be more honest too.

entries.intro: {npc_name} interrupts constantly. I haven't finished a thought around them in weeks.
details: I get maybe four words in before they launch off on whatever my sentence reminded them of.
It's not hostile: they're just bursting with thoughts and mine trigger theirs.
reactions: I've taken to holding up a finger when I'm mid-sentence. It works about half the time.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} admitted the interrupting comes from fear of forgetting what they want to say. If they don't say it immediately, it's gone.
statCheck.fail: They're just an eager talker. Some minds run faster than the conversation.

entries.intro: {npc_name} keeps their word without exception. If they say they'll do something, it's done.
details: I've tested this without meaning to. Offhand mentions, half-serious requests: all followed through.
It's not showy about it either: no announcements, just quiet delivery.
reactions: Reliability shouldn't be this unusual. But it is, and I value it.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} told me they keep every promise because someone broke too many to them and they swore they'd never be that person.
statCheck.fail: They're just dependable. Not everyone needs a reason for it.

entries.intro: {npc_name} squints at you when you talk, like they're reading something on your face that you didn't write.
details: It's an intense squint. You feel examined, like your words are being weighed against your expression.
I caught myself adjusting my face to look more convincing mid-sentence. That's not normal.
reactions: Talking to them is like a performance I didn't audition for.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} told me they squint because they're hard of hearing and read lips to fill in the gaps. The intensity isn't judgment: it's concentration.
statCheck.fail: Maybe they're just an intense listener. Not everyone has a casual face.

entries.intro: {npc_name} shared something personal with me and I'm not sure what to do with it.
details: It came out of nowhere during a normal conversation. A real thing, not small talk.
I think they needed someone to know and I happened to be there.
reactions: I'll keep it to myself. That's the only right thing to do.

entries.intro: {npc_name} takes everything personally. You can't give feedback without it becoming a wound.
details: I mentioned something could be done differently and they went quiet for the rest of the day.
Everyone walks on eggshells around them now. It's exhausting for everybody.
reactions: I feel bad about it, but I've started just saying 'looks good' regardless.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} told me they take things hard because they were told they were never good enough growing up. Every critique echoes.
statCheck.fail: Some people are just sensitive. It's not always about something deeper.

entries.intro: {npc_name} noticed I was struggling before I did. Offered help before I knew I needed it.
details: I was getting frustrated and hadn't said a word, but they read it on me somehow.
A quiet 'want a hand?' at exactly the right moment. Not too early, not too late.
reactions: That kind of attentiveness is rare. Most people don't look closely enough to see.

entries.intro: {npc_name} and {npc_name_2} laugh at jokes nobody else finds funny. They have their own humor.
details: One of them says something perfectly ordinary and the other cracks up. The rest of us just stare.
I've asked what was funny and gotten explanations that make it less funny somehow.
reactions: Private jokes are a sign of real closeness. I'm not offended, just bewildered.

entries.intro: {npc_name} has a voice that carries. Every conversation they have is a public event.
details: I can hear them from across the settlement when the wind is right. Every word, clearly.
They don't realize how loud they are. I've seen people flinch when they start talking nearby.
reactions: You never have to wonder where {npc_name} is. You can triangulate by sound.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} admitted they don't realize how loud they are. Their family was loud and anything below a shout felt like a whisper growing up.
statCheck.fail: They just have a naturally loud voice. Not everyone can control their volume.

entries.intro: {npc_name} makes things by hand and gives them away. Never keeps what they make.
details: Small things: a carved spoon, a braided cord, a mended strap. All given to whoever needs them.
I got one last month and it's better made than anything I could buy.
reactions: Generosity that takes real effort is the kind worth remembering.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} told me making things for others keeps their hands busy and their mind quiet. It's as much for them as for anyone else.
statCheck.fail: They just like making things. Some people show care through craft.

entries.intro: {npc_name} has strong, unasked-for opinions about how I do my work.
details: I was doing perfectly fine until they stopped by to suggest a 'better' approach that wasn't.
The worst part is they circle back to check if I've adopted their method. I haven't.
reactions: I smile and nod and keep doing it my way. Peace preserved.

entries.intro: {npc_name} told me I looked tired and meant it as genuine concern, not an insult.
details: It was the way they said it: quiet, without judgment, and followed by an offer to help.
Most people who say 'you look tired' are telling you to fix your face. This was different.
reactions: Concern that comes without critique is a gift. I needed it that day.

entries.intro: {npc_name} and {npc_name_2} disagree about everything but do it so cheerfully it doesn't seem to matter.
