details: They argue like it's a sport. Points scored, comebacks delivered, both grinning the whole time.
I tried to mediate once and they both turned on me. Apparently the arguing is the fun part.
reactions: If both sides are enjoying it, maybe it's not really an argument.

entries.intro: You'd think the {npc_role} would be easier to talk to. Getting a straight answer is like pulling teeth.
details: I asked a simple question and got redirected twice, deferred once, and eventually forgotten.
Other folk have had the same experience. We've started going to each other instead.
reactions: I don't need charm, just clarity. That shouldn't be too much to ask.

statCheck.pass: I heard the {npc_role} avoids direct answers because they're terrified of committing to something and getting it wrong.
statCheck.fail: Maybe they're just cautious by nature. Some people hedge everything.

entries.intro: {npc_name} smells like woodsmoke all the time. Even in the heat of summer.
details: You can tell where they've been by the trail of smoke scent. It lingers in a room after they leave.
I asked about it once and they said they keep a fire going year-round for comfort.
reactions: It's not unpleasant, just distinctive: I'd know them blindfolded by smell alone.

entries.intro: {npc_name} apologized for something they did months ago. Out of the blue, no prompting.
details: I'd already moved past it, but hearing them bring it up unprompted told me they hadn't.
It was a small thing, but the fact they carried it this long says something about their character.
reactions: Late apologies are still good apologies. The carrying is the hard part.

entries.intro: {npc_name} gossips. And not the harmless kind: the kind that changes how people see each other.
details: They told me something about someone else that I wish I didn't know. True or not, it's in my head now.
I've noticed they always start with 'don't tell anyone, but...' which means everyone already knows.
reactions: I've started treating everything they say about others as unreliable. Saves me trouble.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} admitted the gossip is how they stay relevant. If people come to them for information, they feel needed.
statCheck.fail: Maybe they just like talking. Some people process the world out loud.

entries.intro: {npc_name} checked on me after something went wrong. Nobody else did.
details: A quiet visit, nothing dramatic. 'How are you holding up?' and then actually listening to the answer.
Everyone else assumed I was fine because I looked fine. {npc_name} looked closer.
reactions: The people who check on you after are the people who matter.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} told me they check on people because being overlooked during a hard time once left a mark they never want anyone else to carry.
statCheck.fail: They're just attentive. Some people follow up naturally.

entries.intro: {npc_name} walks too close when they talk to you. Right up in your space.
details: I take a step back, they take a step forward. We've covered entire lengths of path this way.
I don't think they notice. Their sense of personal distance is just calibrated differently.
reactions: I've started talking to them with a fence between us. Natural barrier.

entries.intro: {npc_name} taught me something without making me feel stupid for not already knowing it.
details: No condescension, no 'well obviously.' Just a patient explanation at the right level.
I've been taught things badly enough times to recognize when someone does it well.
reactions: Good teachers don't just know things: they know how to share them.

entries.intro: {npc_name} and {npc_name_2} pass each other without a word most days. Not cold: just parallel.
details: They nod if their eyes meet, but they don't seek each other out. Two lives running side by side.
I've never seen them argue or laugh together. Just a quiet, mutual acknowledgment.
reactions: Not every relationship needs to be close. Some people just coexist peacefully.

entries.intro: {npc_name} says 'we'll see' to everything. Commitments are not their strong suit.
details: 'Can you help tomorrow?' 'We'll see.' 'Will you be there?' 'We'll see.' It's a wall made of two words.
The answer is usually no, dressed up as maybe.
reactions: I've started treating 'we'll see' as 'no' and planning accordingly. Less disappointment that way.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} told me 'we'll see' is how they avoid saying no directly because turning people down makes them feel guilty.
statCheck.fail: They might just be indecisive. Not everyone can commit quickly.

entries.intro: {npc_name} always speaks well of people who aren't present. Every time, without exception.
details: Even when someone's given them good reason to complain, they find the kind angle.
I've tried to get them to grumble with me about someone. They just won't.
reactions: It's made me trust them more. If they speak well of others to me, they probably speak well of me to others.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} told me they speak well of others because gossip destroyed a friendship they valued. They swore off it completely.
statCheck.fail: They're just a kind person. Not everyone needs a reason to be decent.

entries.intro: {npc_name} leaves early from everything. The moment something's winding down, they're gone.
details: I turned around to say something and they'd already left. No goodbye, no excuse.
Someone said they've always been like that. First to arrive, first to leave.
reactions: I've given up saving my best conversation for the end. They won't be there for it.

entries.intro: {npc_name} and {npc_name_2} cook for each other on alternate days. It's a good system.
details: One cooks while the other rests, then they swap. Neither seems to mind the other's cooking.
I ate with them once: the food was plain but the company was warm.
reactions: Simple arrangements like that make living here easier for everyone involved.

entries.intro: {npc_name} has a way of making small problems sound enormous. A splinter becomes a tragedy.
details: I heard them describe a stubbed toe with the gravity of a natural disaster.
It's hard to know when something's actually serious because everything gets the same weight.
reactions: I listen for the rare moment they're calm. That's when I know it's really bad.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} admitted the exaggeration is fear. If they make everything sound big enough, maybe someone will take them seriously before it's dismissed.
statCheck.fail: Some people are just dramatic. It's a personality, not a crisis.

entries.intro: {npc_name} waited for me so we could walk together. I didn't expect that.
details: I came out late and they were leaning against a post, in no hurry, like they had all morning.
We walked in comfortable silence most of the way. They didn't need conversation: just company.
reactions: Small gestures like that remind me I'm not just a neighbor: I'm a friend.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} told me they waited because they noticed I'd been walking alone too much lately and wanted to change that.
statCheck.fail: They were probably just heading the same way and thought they'd wait.

entries.intro: {npc_name} criticizes everything but offers nothing better. All complaint, no solution.
details: I fixed something the best I could and got told three things wrong with it and zero suggestions for improvement.
They see flaws the way a hawk sees movement: instantly and from a distance.
reactions: I've started asking 'so what would you do?' The silence that follows is informative.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} confided that they criticize because they notice things others miss, and it frustrates them when they can't articulate what would be better.
statCheck.fail: Maybe they're just a perfectionist without the skills to match. It happens.

entries.intro: {npc_name} laughs at their own jokes before anyone else can. It's infectious despite itself.
details: The joke lands and they're already wheezing. You laugh because they're laughing, not because the joke was good.
I've tried keeping a straight face, but it's impossible: the laughter is too honest.
reactions: People who enjoy their own humor are easier to be around than people who don't enjoy anything.

entries.intro: {npc_name} stopped to fix something that wasn't their responsibility. Nobody asked.
details: A loose board that everyone walks past every day: {npc_name} just bent down and fixed it.
Took them five minutes. The rest of us had been stepping over it for weeks.
reactions: Fixing things that aren't yours to fix is a quiet kind of decency.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} told me they fix things nobody notices because the unfixed things bother them physically. The loose board was louder to them than it was to anyone else.
statCheck.fail: They're just handy. Some people see a broken thing and can't walk past it.

entries.intro: {npc_name} has never once said 'thank you.' Not once that I've heard.
details: I did them a solid last week and got a nod: that's it, just a nod.
Other people have noticed the same thing. It's not hostility: the gratitude just doesn't have words.
reactions: I still help them because it's the right thing to do. But I won't pretend the silence isn't noticeable.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} told me they struggle to express gratitude because where they come from, thanking someone was considered a sign of weakness.
statCheck.fail: Some people show appreciation differently. Or not at all.

entries.intro: {npc_name} and {npc_name_2} look out for each other in small ways that add up.
details: One saves a seat, the other brings extra water: little things, done without discussion.
It's not dramatic loyalty: it's the daily kind, the kind that actually keeps people close.
reactions: Grand gestures get the attention, but the small ones hold the weight.

entries.intro: {npc_name} is the slowest walker I've ever met. I've aged visibly keeping pace with them.
details: Every step is deliberate and measured, like they're counting each one.
I tried matching their pace once and nearly lost my balance from going too slow.
reactions: I've learned to either go ahead and wait or find a reason to be patient.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} told me the slow pace is deliberate. They decided a long time ago that rushing cost them more than it saved.
statCheck.fail: Maybe they just move slowly. Not everyone's in a hurry.

entries.intro: {npc_name} called me by my name in a way that made me feel known. Not recognized: known.
details: It wasn't the name itself: everyone uses my name. It was the weight they put behind it.
Like it meant something specific to them, not just a label they'd memorized.
reactions: A name said right by the right person can feel like a handshake from the inside.

entries.intro: {npc_name} talks behind people's backs and then acts sweet to their faces. I've seen both sides.
details: They were complaining about someone and the moment that person appeared, all smiles and warmth.
The switch was instant: no transition, no hesitation. Like flipping a coin.
reactions: I wonder what they say when I'm the one who's not in the room.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} admitted the venting is how they process frustration, and being kind to someone's face is how they keep the peace. They see no contradiction.
statCheck.fail: Everyone vents sometimes. Not everyone gets caught doing it.

entries.intro: {npc_name} has a dry sense of humor that sneaks up on you. Half the time I don't realize I've been had until later.
details: They deliver a joke with the same flat tone as a fact. The pause before you get it is the best part.
I've laughed out loud an hour after a conversation ended because something they said finally clicked.
reactions: Humor that works on a delay is an underrated skill.

entries.intro: {npc_name} and {npc_name_2} have been giving each other the silent treatment for days.
details: Nobody knows what started it and neither is volunteering the information.
The rest of us are caught in the middle, trying not to pick sides in a war with no declared cause.
reactions: Silence between two people makes noise for everyone around them.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} told me it was something small that got too big to walk back. Now they both want the other to go first.
statCheck.fail: Probably just a spat. These things tend to resolve on their own.

entries.intro: {npc_name} always looks like they just thought of something funny but decided not to share it.
details: A quick smile that appears and gets tucked away. Happens several times a conversation.
I've asked 'what?' and they always say 'nothing.' The nothing is clearly something.
reactions: I'd give a lot to hear the thoughts they keep. The leaked smiles are promising.

entries.intro: {npc_name} covered for me when I made a mistake. Didn't even blink.
details: I messed up something obvious and before anyone noticed, they'd quietly fixed it.
They gave me a look that said 'we never speak of this' and I've honored that.
reactions: The people who cover for you without being asked are the ones you owe the most.

entries.intro: {npc_name} keeps asking how things should be done instead of trying. Every single task.
details: I explained it once, then again, then a third time, and eventually just did it myself.
The questions aren't about learning: they're about avoiding the risk of doing it wrong.
reactions: I have patience for most things, but this one is wearing thin.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} told me the constant asking comes from being harshly corrected too many times. They'd rather ask twice than be told they failed.
statCheck.fail: Maybe they just lack confidence. Not everyone trusts their own hands.

entries.intro: {npc_name} complimented my work in front of other people. I didn't know how much I needed that.
details: It wasn't grand: just a matter-of-fact 'good job' said where others could hear it.
The public part mattered. Private praise is nice but public recognition changes how people see you.
reactions: I'm going to do that for someone else the first chance I get.

entries.intro: {npc_name} fidgets with anything within reach. Buttons, straps, loose threads: nothing is safe.
details: I watched them unravel the edge of a cloth without looking at it while we talked. Completely unconscious.
They left and I found a pile of tiny torn bits where they'd been sitting.
reactions: I've started moving fragile things out of arm's reach before they sit down.

entries.intro: {npc_name} told me something I'd been afraid to admit to myself. Gently, but directly.
details: I didn't ask for it and I'm not sure I wanted it, but they were right.
The way they said it made it possible to hear. Anyone else and I'd have pushed back.
reactions: Truth from someone who cares about you is the only kind that doesn't cut.

entries.intro: {npc_name} and {npc_name_2} finish each other's thoughts in a way that's equal parts impressive and unsettling.
details: One starts a sentence, the other completes it. Seamlessly, without pause or signal.
I tried to jump in once with my own ending and they both looked at me like I'd interrupted a duet.
reactions: That level of synchronization either means deep understanding or too much time together. Possibly both.

entries.intro: {npc_name} is quick to blame others when things go wrong. Never themselves.
details: Something broke while they were handling it and within seconds it was somehow my fault.
The explanation was so convoluted I nearly believed it. Nearly.
reactions: I've started keeping my distance during anything that might go sideways.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} admitted the blame-shifting is panic, not malice. When something goes wrong, the fear of being at fault overrides everything.
statCheck.fail: Some people just point fingers. It's reflex, not strategy.

entries.intro: {npc_name} has a way of making you feel like the only person who matters when they're listening.
details: Everything else drops away. Their focus is complete: no glancing around, no half-attention.
I talked to them for ten minutes and felt like I'd been heard for the first time in weeks.
reactions: Attention like that is rare enough to be a gift. I seek them out when I need to think out loud.

entries.intro: {npc_name} tells the same stories over and over. I could recite them word for word at this point.
details: I've heard the one about the fence post at least four times. The punchline hasn't changed.
I nod along like it's fresh every time. It seems kinder than pointing it out.
reactions: Repetition means a story matters to someone. I can spare the patience for that.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} told me the stories repeat because telling them is how they keep certain memories alive. If they stop telling, they're afraid of forgetting.
statCheck.fail: They probably just don't realize they've told it before. Happens to everyone.

entries.intro: {npc_name} gave credit to someone else for work they did themselves. I saw it happen.
details: They did the entire thing and when asked about it, pointed to someone else and said 'their idea.'
The other person looked confused. {npc_name} just shrugged and moved on.
reactions: Humility at that level is either saintly or stubborn. Either way, I respect it.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} told me attention makes them deeply uncomfortable. Deflecting credit is how they stay invisible, and invisible is where they feel safe.
statCheck.fail: They're just modest. Not everyone wants the spotlight.

entries.intro: {npc_name} clears their throat before saying anything. It's the prelude to every statement.
details: I hear the throat-clear and brace for whatever follows. It's become a signal, like a bell before a door opens.
I asked if they were feeling alright once. They said 'perfectly fine,' preceded, naturally, by a throat-clear.
reactions: I've accepted it as part of the vocabulary. The throat-clear is sentence zero.

entries.intro: {npc_name} and {npc_name_2} support each other publicly even when I know they disagree privately.
details: I've heard {npc_name} voice doubts to me about something {npc_name_2} decided. But in front of everyone, full support.
It's loyalty over correctness. I'm not sure I agree with the approach, but I admire the solidarity.
reactions: Having someone who backs you even when they have reservations: that's a bond worth keeping.

entries.intro: {npc_name} said something sharp to me and I don't think they realized it stung.
details: It was an offhand remark, probably meant as a joke. Didn't land that way.
I didn't say anything about it because bringing it up would make it into a thing, and I don't want a thing.
reactions: Most small wounds heal if you let them. I'm letting this one go.

entries.intro: {npc_name} brings calm to tense situations without raising their voice or taking charge.
details: I've seen tempers flare and then {npc_name} says one quiet thing and the room settles.
It's not authority: it's presence. They don't lead the room, they steady it.
reactions: Some people defuse things just by being there. That's a talent you can't teach.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} told me they learned to be the calm one because nobody was calm for them when they needed it most.
statCheck.fail: Some people are just naturally even-tempered. It's a disposition, not a story.

entries.intro: {npc_name} cracks jokes at the worst moments. Something serious happens and out comes a quip.
details: We were dealing with a real problem and they made a remark that had half the group laughing and half glaring.
I don't think it's disrespect. I think it's how they handle being uncomfortable.
reactions: Bad timing doesn't mean bad intentions. I remind myself of that.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} admitted the jokes are a shield. When things get heavy, humor is the only response that doesn't feel overwhelming.
statCheck.fail: Maybe they just have no filter. Some people's mouths work faster than their judgment.

entries.intro: {npc_name} watches sunsets. Every evening, without fail, they stop what they're doing and look west.
details: I asked if they were waiting for something and they said 'no, just watching.' That was it.
There's something steady about a person with a daily ritual that asks nothing of anyone.
reactions: I watched one with them once: neither of us spoke and it was a good evening.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} told me watching sunsets is the only time their mind goes quiet. The rest of the day, it never stops.
statCheck.fail: Maybe they just like the colors. Not every ritual needs a reason.

entries.intro: {npc_name} and {npc_name_2} work together like they've been doing it for years.
details: No wasted motion, no repeated instructions. One moves and the other adjusts.
I tried working with them once and felt like a third wheel on a cart that only needs two.
reactions: Some partnerships just click. I'm better off admiring it than trying to join it.

entries.intro: The {npc_role} has been more approachable lately. I almost thought I was talking to a different person.
details: Used to be all business, no warmth. Now there's the occasional smile and an actual greeting.
I'm not sure what changed, but I'll take it.
reactions: People grow at their own pace. Better late than never.

entries.intro: {npc_name} sneezes like a thunderclap. The first time it happened I thought something collapsed.
details: It's a single, explosive sneeze that echoes. Birds leave the area.
They follow it with a quiet 'excuse me' that's absurd given the volume of what preceded it.
reactions: I flinch every time and I've accepted that I always will.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} told me they've tried to sneeze quietly and it makes it worse. The body wants what the body wants.
statCheck.fail: Some people just sneeze loud. It's not a choice.

entries.intro: {npc_name} went out of their way to include someone who was being left out. I saw it.
details: Someone was standing on the edge of a group, not quite part of it. {npc_name} opened a space and pulled them in.
It was done so naturally that if you weren't watching closely you'd think it happened by itself.
reactions: People who widen the circle without being asked are the ones who make a place feel like home.

entries.intro: {npc_name} and {npc_name_2} blame each other for everything. Nothing is ever either one's fault.
details: One says it was the other's idea. The other says they were just following along.
The rest of us have stopped trying to sort out responsibility. The blame just circles forever.
reactions: Accountability between those two is a ball that never stops bouncing.

entries.intro: {npc_name} is impossible to read. Happy, angry, tired: same face for all of it.
details: I've seen them receive good news and bad news with identical expressions. Completely neutral.
The only tell is a slight pause before they respond. The longer the pause, the bigger the feeling.
reactions: I've learned to read the pauses. It's like a second language made of silence.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} told me the flat expression is deliberate. They decided young that showing feelings gave people too much power over them.
statCheck.fail: Some people are just reserved. Not every calm face hides a storm.

entries.intro: {npc_name} remembered a promise from weeks ago and followed through today. I'd completely forgotten.
details: They mentioned it like it was obvious: 'I said I'd take care of it, so I did.'
I was the one who forgot the promise. They were the one who kept it.
reactions: That kind of follow-through is rare and I'm taking note.

entries.intro: {npc_name} chews their fingernails when they're nervous. You can always tell when something's on their mind.
details: The sound is quiet but persistent, like a mouse working on something in the walls.
I offered them a task to keep their hands busy once. It worked until the task ended.
reactions: Everyone has a tell. Theirs just happens to be audible.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} told me the nail-biting started during a period when they had nothing to do with their hands and the idle energy turned inward.
statCheck.fail: It's just a nervous habit. Most people have one.

entries.intro: {npc_name} stood up for what they believed in even when it wasn't the popular view.
details: Everyone was leaning one way and {npc_name} said what they actually thought. Clearly, calmly.
It wasn't aggressive. Just honest in a room that was leaning toward comfortable agreement.
reactions: Courage isn't always loud. Sometimes it's just one voice that doesn't bend.

entries.intro: {npc_name} takes credit for ideas that weren't theirs. Smoothly, too: you almost don't notice.
details: Someone suggested something yesterday and today {npc_name} presented it as their own thought.
The original person was right there. The look on their face was a study in restraint.
reactions: I've started stating my ideas more firmly. Harder to claim what's clearly marked.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} confessed they don't realize they're doing it. The idea enters their head and after a day it feels like it was always theirs.
statCheck.fail: Maybe they just have a loose sense of ownership. Generous interpretation, but possible.

entries.intro: {npc_name} and {npc_name_2} cover for each other so seamlessly I can never tell who actually did what.
details: If one makes a mistake, the other's already patching it before anyone looks.
It's not dishonest: it's partnership. The work gets done and the seams don't show.
reactions: I wish I had someone I could work that smoothly with.

entries.intro: {npc_name} always has to have the last word. Every conversation is a competition to them.
details: I said goodbye once and they said it back, then I waved, then they waved: it went on.
If you stop engaging, they'll wait a beat and then add one more thing. Guaranteed.
reactions: I've learned to just walk away. They get their last word and I get my peace.

entries.intro: {npc_name} offered me something they clearly valued. I tried to refuse and they insisted.
details: It wasn't big, but I could tell it mattered to them. That's what made the giving meaningful.
The way they pressed it into my hands: firmly, no room for argument. This was happening.
reactions: Accepting a gift gracefully is its own kindness. I kept it.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} told me the giving is selfish in a way: holding onto things they care about makes them anxious, and letting go brings relief.
statCheck.fail: They're just generous. Some people show care through objects.

entries.intro: {npc_name} plays favorites and doesn't bother hiding it.
details: Some people get the smile and the conversation. The rest of us get the nod.
I'm not in the favored group and it's made clear in a hundred small ways every week.
reactions: I don't need to be everyone's favorite. But the transparency of it does sting.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} admitted they gravitate toward people who remind them of someone they used to know. The favoritism isn't about worth: it's about memory.
statCheck.fail: Some people just click with certain folk and not others. It's human nature.

entries.intro: {npc_name} left a kind note where I'd find it. No name on it, but I recognized the handwriting.
details: It said something simple about appreciating having me around. Unsigned but unmistakable.
I think they wanted it to be anonymous. That makes it better, somehow.
reactions: Kindness that doesn't need to be acknowledged is the purest kind.

entries.intro: {npc_name} is the kind of person who points out problems but never pitches in to solve them.
details: 'That's going to fall over.' Yes, thank you: would you like to help hold it?
They stand back, arms crossed, full of observations and empty of assistance.
reactions: I've started responding with 'then grab that end.' It shuts down the commentary fast.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} confessed they point things out because they want to contribute but physical help is harder for them than they let on.
statCheck.fail: Maybe they just prefer observing to doing. Everyone has their comfort zone.

entries.intro: {npc_name} and {npc_name_2} went through a rough patch and came out closer than before.
details: For a while nobody thought they'd speak again. Now they're practically inseparable.
Whatever was said between them to fix things, it must have been real. The repair is solid.
reactions: Relationships that survive a fracture are stronger at the break. Like a bone.

entries.intro: {npc_name} stretches before every task like they're preparing for battle. Even the small ones.
details: Picking up a crate: stretch; walking somewhere: stretch; conversation: oddly, no stretch.
It's methodical: arms, back, neck, always in the same order.
reactions: I tried their routine once and pulled something. They make it look easier than it is.

entries.intro: {npc_name} believed in something I was trying to do when nobody else did. That kept me going.
details: Everyone else was skeptical or politely silent. {npc_name} said 'I think you're onto something.'
Five words. That's all it took to make the difference between quitting and continuing.
reactions: Belief from one person at the right time outweighs doubt from a dozen.

entries.intro: {npc_name} has a way of agreeing with you that somehow feels like they're disagreeing.
details: 'You're right.' The way they say it makes 'right' sound like a concession, not an agreement.
I've walked away from conversations feeling wrong even though they said I was correct.
reactions: It's a talent: agreement that undermines. I still can't explain how they do it.

entries.intro: {npc_name} and {npc_name_2} both insist they owe the other a favor. Neither will let it go.
details: Who helped first depends on who you ask: {npc_name} says it was them, {npc_name_2} says otherwise.
They've been trading favors back and forth trying to even the score. The score will never be even.
reactions: It's the friendliest debt cycle I've ever seen. Nobody loses.

entries.intro: {npc_name} scratches the back of their head before saying anything uncertain. Dead giveaway.
details: If the hand goes to the head, whatever follows is a guess dressed as knowledge.
I've started watching for the scratch before asking important questions. Saves me time.
reactions: Everyone broadcasts their uncertainty somehow. {npc_name}'s broadcast just happens to be visible.

entries.intro: {npc_name} treated me fairly when they had every reason not to.
details: We had a disagreement before and I expected resentment. Got straightforward decency instead.
They handled it like the past was the past and the present deserved its own rules.
reactions: Fairness from someone you've wronged is harder to earn than praise from a friend.

entries.intro: {npc_name} makes everything into a competition. Who worked harder, who finished first, who carried more.
details: I was just doing a task and suddenly it was a race I didn't sign up for.
They keep score in their head and announce the results to nobody's interest.
reactions: I stopped competing and their interest evaporated. Turns out the game needs two players.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} confided that competing is the only way they know how to connect. Everything was a contest growing up and the habit stuck.
statCheck.fail: Some people are just competitive by nature. It's not always deeper than that.

entries.intro: {npc_name} asked me a question about myself that nobody else has thought to ask.
details: Not personal in a prying way: personal in a 'you see me as a person, not just a neighbor' way.
I answered more honestly than I expected to. The question earned it.
reactions: Good questions are rarer than good answers. I'm still thinking about mine.

entries.intro: {npc_name} leaves things half-done everywhere. Unfinished tasks scattered like a trail.
details: A pile here, a half-mended thing there, something leaning against a wall waiting for a conclusion that never arrives.
I've accidentally finished two of their projects just by tidying up.
reactions: I've accepted the landscape. {npc_name}'s wake is part of the scenery now.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} admitted the unfinished tasks come from losing interest the moment they figure out how to do something. The solving is the part they enjoy, not the finishing.
statCheck.fail: They might just get distracted easily. Not everyone follows through naturally.

entries.intro: {npc_name} told me to take care of myself. Simple words, but from them it hit differently.
details: They said it at the end of a conversation, casually, like it was nothing.
But the way they held my gaze for a beat too long turned it from pleasantry to genuine concern.
reactions: Some people mean things more than they say them. {npc_name} is one of those people.

entries.intro: {npc_name} slams things down when they're upset. Cups, tools, doors: all victims.
details: You can track their mood by the volume of their handling. Gentle means good: percussive means bad.
A cup hit a table so hard yesterday that everyone in earshot stopped mid-sentence.
reactions: I've learned to give wide berth when the slamming starts. The storm passes faster without an audience.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} told me the slamming is because they can't find the words. The frustration has to go somewhere and objects are easier to hit than feelings.
statCheck.fail: Some people are just physical with their emotions. Loud doesn't always mean dangerous.

entries.intro: {npc_name} walks their own pace through life. Doesn't rush, doesn't drag: just moves at their own rhythm.
details: Everyone around them is hurrying or dawdling and there's {npc_name}, untouched by the current.
I've tried to match their pace. It's surprisingly hard to move at a speed that isn't yours.
reactions: There's something calming about being near someone who isn't in a hurry.

entries.intro: {npc_name} and {npc_name_2} tease each other constantly. To an outsider it sounds mean, but they're both grinning.
details: The insults are creative and obviously practiced. They've been sharpening these on each other for a while.
I made the mistake of joining in once and both turned on me immediately. United front.
reactions: Their version of affection is just louder and sharper than most people's.

entries.intro: {npc_name} showed me how to do something I've been doing wrong for months. No judgment in the correction.
details: They watched me struggle, waited until I paused, then showed me the right way like it was nothing.
I felt foolish for about two seconds, then grateful for the rest of the day.
reactions: A correction delivered kindly is more useful than a hundred politely ignored mistakes.

entries.intro: {npc_name} talks about themselves like they're not worth much. I disagree strongly.
details: Little comments: 'I'm not good at that,' 'you don't want my help,' 'anyone else would be better.'
Every one of those statements is wrong, but saying so doesn't seem to change the script.
reactions: I keep correcting them anyway. Repetition has to count for something eventually.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} told me the self-deprecation started as deflection and became habit. They know it's not all true, but the pattern is deep.
statCheck.fail: Some people just see themselves harshly. You can't always talk someone out of it.

entries.intro: {npc_name} brought up something I said a long time ago as if it mattered. Because to them, it did.
details: I barely remembered saying it. They remembered it exactly: words, tone, the day.
It changed something for them, and I had no idea until they told me.
reactions: We throw words into the world without knowing where they land. That's terrifying and wonderful.

entries.intro: {npc_name} always looks slightly annoyed. Even when they're having a good time.
details: I saw them laughing at something genuinely funny and still their resting expression defaulted back to irritated within seconds.
It's misleading. The face says 'leave me alone' and the person says 'stay and talk.'
reactions: I've learned to read the words, not the face. The face lies.

entries.intro: {npc_name} made me feel welcome when I first arrived here. I haven't forgotten.
details: Everyone else was polite but distant. {npc_name} was the one who actually talked to me like a person.
First impressions of a place are usually about one person. For me, that person was {npc_name}.
reactions: A settlement is just buildings until someone makes you feel like you belong in them.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} told me they made a point of welcoming newcomers because arriving somewhere new and being ignored was the loneliest thing they'd ever felt.
statCheck.fail: They're just friendly by nature. Some people make it easy to settle in.

entries.intro: {npc_name} drops hints instead of asking directly. Everything is an implication.
details: 'It sure would be nice if someone helped with that pile.' They're looking at me. The pile is next to them.
I've started responding to the hint like I didn't understand it. The resulting directness is refreshing.
reactions: Asking plainly would save us both the dance. But the dance is all they know.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} told me asking outright feels vulnerable. Hints let them pretend they didn't ask if the answer is no.
statCheck.fail: Maybe they're just indirect by nature. Some people communicate sideways.

entries.intro: {npc_name} and {npc_name_2} look after each other in ways neither acknowledges out loud.
details: One makes sure there's always enough for two. The other checks that nobody overworks.
Neither says 'I'm looking after you.' They just do it, quietly, daily.
reactions: Care that doesn't announce itself is the kind that lasts.

entries.intro: {npc_name} rubs their temples when they're thinking hard. Makes them look permanently stressed.
details: I've asked if they have a headache and gotten a puzzled look. They don't even know they do it.
The temple-rubbing is just part of how they process things. The hands think alongside the head.
reactions: Once you know what it means, it's reassuring. The rubbing means they're taking something seriously.

entries.intro: {npc_name} is generous when it costs them something. That's the part that matters.
details: Anyone can give what they don't need. {npc_name} gives what they do need, without hesitation.
I've seen them go without so someone else could have enough. They never mention it.
reactions: Real generosity is invisible because it doesn't want credit. I try to repay it when I can.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} told me giving things away calms something restless in them. They hold onto too much otherwise and it weighs on them.
statCheck.fail: They're just generous. Some people give because it's in their nature.

entries.intro: {npc_name} corrects people in public, loudly, without considering how it lands.
details: Someone misspoke yesterday and {npc_name} corrected them in front of everyone with the subtlety of a falling tree.
The person being corrected looked like they wanted the ground to swallow them.
reactions: Being right and being kind aren't mutually exclusive. Someone should tell {npc_name} that.

entries.intro: {npc_name} noticed I'd changed something small about how I do things. Nobody else saw it.
details: It was a tiny adjustment, barely visible. They mentioned it like it was obvious.
I didn't think anyone was paying that much attention. Apparently {npc_name} was.
reactions: Being noticed by someone who pays attention feels different from being noticed by everyone else.

entries.intro: You'd think the {npc_role} would take more pride in the work. The sloppiness is starting to show.
details: Things that used to be done properly are now done just well enough to pass. The standard has dropped.
I mentioned it once and got a dismissive wave. That told me everything I needed to know.
reactions: I've lowered my expectations accordingly. Less disappointment that way.

statCheck.pass: I heard the {npc_role} is overwhelmed and too proud to ask for help. The sloppiness is exhaustion, not laziness.
statCheck.fail: Maybe the job is just too much for one person. Standards slip when energy runs out.

entries.intro: {npc_name} always tilts their head to one side when listening. Like a curious bird.
details: The tilt is consistent: always the same side, always at the same angle.
It makes you feel heard, oddly. Something about the posture says 'I'm giving this my full attention.'
reactions: It's a small thing. But the people who make you feel listened to change the texture of a day.

entries.intro: {npc_name} trusted me with something fragile and I didn't break it. Felt like passing a test.
details: They handed it over without instructions, just a steady look that said 'be careful with this.'
The weight of being trusted with something someone values: that's its own kind of responsibility.
reactions: Trust given freely is the kind worth protecting.

entries.intro: {npc_name} complains about everything: the weather, the food, the work, all of it.
details: I've never heard them say a positive thing about anything. Not once.
Even good news gets reframed: 'nice day today' becomes 'won't last' within seconds.
reactions: Being around that level of negativity is tiring. I limit my exposure.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} told me complaining is how they process the world. If they say something negative about it, they've dealt with it and can move on.
statCheck.fail: Maybe they're just unhappy. Some people carry a cloud with them everywhere.

entries.intro: {npc_name} and {npc_name_2} share silences comfortably. No need to fill the air.
details: I've seen them sit together for long stretches without a word exchanged. Both perfectly content.
Most people panic in silence and rush to fill it. Those two let it breathe.
reactions: Comfortable silence between two people tells you more about their bond than any conversation could.

entries.intro: {npc_name} stands with their arms crossed most of the time. Makes them look closed off even when they're not.
details: I used to think they were angry. Turns out it's just how they stand: comfortable, not defensive.
The arms uncross when they laugh, briefly, and then fold right back.
reactions: Bodies say things people don't mean. I've learned to listen to the words instead.

entries.intro: {npc_name} said something kind about me to someone else. Word got back.
details: It was specific: not vague pleasantries but something about a quality of mine they'd noticed.
The person who told me seemed almost jealous. That's how genuine the praise was.
reactions: Kind words travel. I'm glad {npc_name}'s are the kind that reach the right ears.

entries.intro: {npc_name} makes plans and then cancels at the last moment. Every single time.
details: I've stopped rearranging my day for their commitments. The pattern is too reliable.
The excuse is always different but the result is always the same: no show.
reactions: I like them well enough. I just don't count on them for anything time-sensitive.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} admitted the canceling is anxiety. They want to go, they plan to go, and then something in them locks up when the time comes.
statCheck.fail: Some people just overcommit. Planning is easier than following through.

entries.intro: {npc_name} keeps a steady rhythm to their days. Same routines, same pace, same calm.
details: You could set a clock by their habits. Morning walk, midday rest, evening work: never varies.
There's a reassurance in watching someone live so deliberately. It makes the days feel ordered.
reactions: In a world where things shift constantly, {npc_name}'s consistency is a comfort.

entries.intro: {npc_name} shared their lunch with me when mine fell in the dirt. Without a moment's hesitation.
details: They split it right down the middle, handed me the bigger half, and carried on eating.
I offered to repay them and they looked at me like the suggestion was absurd.
reactions: Sharing what you have without thinking about it: that's character showing itself.

entries.intro: {npc_name} gives unsolicited, detailed updates about their day to anyone within earshot.
details: I know what they ate, how they slept, what bothered them, and what they plan to do next. I didn't ask for any of it.
They treat every passerby as a captive audience and every event as worth reporting.
reactions: I've developed a polite exit strategy. A nod, a step back, and then a brisk walk.

entries.intro: {npc_name} and {npc_name_2} give each other a look before either speaks in a group. A quick check.
details: It's subtle: a glance, a tiny nod or shake, and then one of them talks. The other waits.
I don't think they're aware they do it. It's a coordination that runs below the surface.
reactions: Two people with a private channel in a public room: that's deep familiarity.

entries.intro: {npc_name} said 'I appreciate you' and it didn't sound rehearsed. That's what got me.
details: Not 'I appreciate what you do.' I appreciate *you*. The whole person, not just the utility.
I carried that sentence around for days like a coin in my pocket.
reactions: Three words. That's all it takes when someone means them.

entries.intro: {npc_name} does the bare minimum and acts like they deserve a celebration for it.
details: They completed a basic task yesterday and talked about it for the rest of the afternoon.
The gap between effort and self-congratulation is staggering.
reactions: I've stopped expecting more. Disappointment was my fault for hoping.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} confided that doing even the minimum takes them more effort than it looks. What seems basic to everyone else is a real accomplishment for them.
statCheck.fail: Some people just have different standards. Low effort, high self-regard.

entries.intro: {npc_name} picks up on moods without being told. They knew I was off before I'd said a word.
details: I walked in and they just handed me something warm and said 'rough morning?'
I hadn't told anyone. Didn't even think I was showing it.
reactions: People who read others that well are either very attentive or have needed to be. Maybe both.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} told me they learned to read moods because missing someone's shift used to have consequences. It's not intuition: it's survival turned skill.
statCheck.fail: They just pay attention. Not everyone looks, but the ones who do see a lot.

entries.intro: {npc_name} and {npc_name_2} tried working together and it was a disaster. But a funny one.
details: Two completely different approaches collided and nothing got done, but both were laughing by the end.
They've agreed to work separately from now on. The decision was mutual and immediate.
reactions: Knowing when you don't work well together is its own kind of wisdom.

entries.intro: {npc_name} mutters when they disagree instead of speaking up. Little comments under their breath.
details: You can hear just enough to know they're unhappy but not enough to address it directly.
I've asked 'did you say something?' and gotten a 'no, nothing' that was clearly something.
reactions: I'd rather hear the objection out loud than decode the mumbling. But I'll take what I get.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} told me the muttering is the only form of disagreement that feels safe to them. Speaking up directly was punished when they were younger.
statCheck.fail: Some people just think out loud quietly. Not every mutter is a message.

entries.intro: {npc_name} works quietly and asks for nothing. I wish more people operated that way.
details: No complaints, no drama, no 'look at what I did.' Just steady, silent contribution.
Half the improvements around here were done by {npc_name} and nobody knows because they don't say.
reactions: The world runs on people who work without applause. {npc_name} is one of them.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} told me they stay quiet because attention brought scrutiny in the past and scrutiny brought trouble. Silence is where they do their best work.
statCheck.fail: They're just private by nature. Not everyone needs to be seen.

entries.intro: {npc_name} nods like they understand and then asks the same question five minutes later.
details: The nod is convincing. Full engagement, eye contact: you'd swear the information landed.
Five minutes later: 'wait, what did you say about that?' Every time.
reactions: I've started asking 'does that make sense?' after key points: it helps, a little.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} told me the nodding is genuine but the information doesn't stick because they're focused on making the other person feel heard, not on retaining the words.
statCheck.fail: Maybe they just have a short memory for details. It's not uncommon.

entries.intro: {npc_name} handles disagreements with a directness I wish I had.
details: No passive-aggression, no lingering resentment. Just 'I disagree, here's why,' and then it's done.
The conversation stays clean: no personal attacks, no dragging in old grievances.
reactions: Honest disagreement is refreshing when so many people choose polite dishonesty instead.

entries.intro: {npc_name} and {npc_name_2} compete over who gives more to the settlement. It's gotten ridiculous.
details: One fixed something and by noon the other had fixed two things, then three, then four.
The settlement benefits enormously from the rivalry but neither will admit it's a competition.
reactions: If pettiness produces results, I won't complain.

entries.intro: {npc_name} scratches something on a post every morning. I think they're counting the days.
details: One mark per morning, a long row of tiny scratches stretching back further than I want to count.
I don't know what they're counting to or from. The marks just keep appearing.
reactions: Everyone measures time their own way. Theirs just happens to leave marks.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} told me the marks track the days since they left somewhere they cared about. Each scratch is distance measured in time.
statCheck.fail: Maybe it's just a habit. Some people like keeping track of things.

entries.intro: {npc_name} thanked me for something I did so long ago I'd forgotten. They hadn't.
details: It was a favor I barely remember giving. They brought it up like it happened yesterday.
The delay made the thanks heavier, not lighter. They'd been carrying gratitude all that time.
reactions: Late thanks from someone who means it is better than prompt thanks from someone who doesn't.

entries.intro: {npc_name} dominates every conversation. By the end you've learned everything about them and shared nothing.
details: I opened my mouth to contribute and they steamrolled right through without a breath.
It's like talking to a river. You can stand in it but you can't change the direction.
reactions: I've started just letting them talk. It's easier than fighting the current.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} admitted they dominate conversations because silence makes them deeply uncomfortable. The talking fills a space they're afraid of.
statCheck.fail: Some people just have a lot to say. It's not always about something deeper.

entries.intro: {npc_name} winks when they're making a joke. It's the only warning you get.
details: The deadpan delivery is perfect and without the wink I'd never know they weren't serious.
I've started watching for it. The wink is the punctuation: everything before it is the setup.
reactions: People with a tell for their humor are doing you a favor. {npc_name}'s tell is well-timed.

entries.intro: {npc_name} did something thoughtful and played it off like it was nothing. But it wasn't nothing.
details: They took care of a problem I mentioned once, offhandedly, in a conversation I thought they'd forgotten.
When I thanked them they said 'oh, that' like it was a minor detour, not an act of kindness.
reactions: The kindness people minimize is often the kindness that matters most.

entries.intro: {npc_name} and {npc_name_2} have an inside reference for everything. The rest of us are lost.
details: Half their sentences are callbacks to something only they remember. The other half are setting up new ones.
I sat through a whole exchange that sounded like a foreign language made entirely of shared experience.
reactions: It's endearing and maddening in equal measure.

entries.intro: {npc_name} closes their eyes when they're concentrating. Full commitment to the internal world.
details: I thought they'd fallen asleep standing up until they opened their eyes and gave a perfectly clear answer.
The closing lasts just a few seconds, but in those seconds they're completely elsewhere.
reactions: I've learned to wait out the eye-close. Whatever comes after is usually worth the pause.

entries.intro: {npc_name} laughed at something I said and it was the most genuine sound I've heard all week.
details: Not the polite laugh people give to be nice. A real one, unexpected and uncontrolled.
I don't even remember what I said that was funny. The laugh eclipsed the joke.
reactions: Making someone laugh without trying is one of the quiet pleasures.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} told me they laugh that freely because they spent too long not laughing and decided never to hold one back again.
statCheck.fail: They're just easy to amuse. Everyone has a different threshold for humor.

entries.intro: {npc_name} takes forever to make a decision. By the time they've chosen, the moment has passed.
details: I waited for an answer about something simple for so long that I made the decision myself.
They weigh every option like it carries equal consequence, whether it's important or trivial.
reactions: Patience is a virtue, but mine has limits.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} told me the indecision comes from making a bad choice once that they've never forgiven themselves for. Every decision since carries that weight.
statCheck.fail: Some people are just deliberate. They'll get there: slowly.

entries.intro: {npc_name} and {npc_name_2} brought food to a family that was struggling. Didn't make a scene about it.
details: Dropped it off early morning, no note, no mention of it to anyone. I only saw because I was up early.
Neither has said a word about it. The family doesn't know who left it.
reactions: Anonymous kindness between neighbors: that's what makes a place worth living in.

entries.intro: {npc_name} frowns when they're concentrating. Deep, committed frowning that looks like anger but isn't.
details: I asked if something was wrong and they said 'no, just thinking about grain yields,' with the face of someone planning revenge.
The disconnect between expression and intent is consistent. Scary face, mundane thoughts.
reactions: I've stopped asking if they're alright during tasks. The frown is just their working face.

entries.intro: {npc_name} made time for me when they didn't have any. I could tell it cost them.
details: They were clearly busy, had a dozen things waiting, but they stopped and listened.
Not a rushed listen either: a proper, full-attention listen. Their tasks could wait.
reactions: Time is the most honest gift. When someone gives it knowing they'll pay for it later, that's real.

entries.intro: {npc_name} remembers things I wish they'd forget. Old mistakes I've moved past.
details: I tripped over my own words once and they still bring it up like it happened this morning.
The memory is impressive. The deployment of it is less so.
reactions: I've accepted that my past lives rent-free in {npc_name}'s head.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} told me they bring up old things because those moments feel vivid and real. They don't mean it unkindly: they just can't tell which memories hurt.
statCheck.fail: Maybe they just have a good memory and a poor filter.

entries.intro: {npc_name} helped me fix something I'd been putting off for weeks. Took them half an hour.
details: I'd been dreading the job and they made it look effortless. Almost insulting how quick it was.
They didn't even ask for anything. Just saw the problem and solved it.
reactions: Some people make the hard things look easy and the easy things look like favors.

entries.intro: {npc_name} doesn't gossip. I've tried: they just change the subject.
details: Everyone else has opinions about everyone. {npc_name} keeps theirs locked up tight.
I respect it. I also find it slightly frustrating when I really want to vent.
reactions: The people who don't gossip are the ones you can actually trust with something real.

entries.intro: {npc_name} borrowed something of mine and returned it in better condition than I lent it.
details: Cleaned, sharpened, and neatly wrapped. I almost didn't recognize it.
I've started lending them things on purpose just to get the upgrade.
reactions: That's the kind of borrower you want: the kind that improves what they touch.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} told me they fix things they borrow because returning something worse than you found it is a debt they can't stand carrying.
statCheck.fail: They're just meticulous. Some people treat borrowed things like their own.

entries.intro: {npc_name} talks about people behind their backs but is all smiles to their faces.
details: I've heard them say things about someone and then greet that person warmly an hour later.
It makes me wonder what they say about me when I'm not around.
reactions: I keep my guard up. The smile is real, but so are the words I'm not supposed to hear.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} admitted the two-faced thing isn't intentional. They say things in frustration they don't fully mean, and the friendliness is genuine too.
statCheck.fail: Some people just need to vent. Doesn't mean they're being dishonest exactly.

entries.intro: {npc_name} and {npc_name_2} finish each other's sentences. It's equal parts impressive and unsettling.
details: One starts a thought and the other completes it without hesitation, like reading from the same page.
I tried to keep up once. I couldn't even predict where a sentence was going.
reactions: That level of sync only comes from years. You can't fake it.

entries.intro: {npc_name} never sits still. Always fidgeting, tapping, shifting weight from foot to foot.
details: Having a conversation with them is like talking to someone who's perpetually about to leave.
The energy is constant. Even standing in one place, they're somehow in motion.
reactions: It's not rude: it's just how they're built. The stillness gene passed them by.

entries.intro: {npc_name} told me something honest about myself that nobody else would say. It stung, but it helped.
details: Not cruel, not gentle either. Just direct, like pulling a splinter: quick and necessary.
I spent two days annoyed and then realized they were right.
reactions: Honest friends are more valuable than kind ones. {npc_name} is both, in the right order.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} told me they say the hard things because nobody did that for them when they needed it. The honesty is repayment for something they never received.
statCheck.fail: Some people are just blunt by nature. It's not always deeper than that.

entries.intro: {npc_name} hums when they work. The same tune, over and over, all day long.
details: I don't think they know they're doing it. The humming starts the moment their hands get busy.
It's actually pleasant if you're far enough away. Up close, the repetition grinds.
reactions: I've caught myself humming it at home. The tune is contagious, annoyingly.

entries.intro: {npc_name} always offers to carry things for people. Doesn't matter how heavy or how far.
details: I've seen them loaded down with other people's burdens, cheerful about it, arms full.
Nobody asks: they just offer before you can manage it yourself.
reactions: Kindness like that either comes from deep generosity or deep need to be useful. Maybe both.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} told me carrying things makes them feel needed. The weight in their arms is simpler than the weight in their head.
statCheck.fail: They're just helpful. Some people are happiest when they're doing something for someone else.

entries.intro: {npc_name} eats louder than anyone I've ever met. You can hear every bite.
details: Crunching, smacking, the occasional contented sigh between bites. It's a full performance.
I've timed my meals to avoid overlap. It's the only strategy that works.
reactions: I don't think they can help it. But knowing that doesn't make the chewing quieter.

entries.intro: {npc_name} and {npc_name_2} argue about the smallest things like they're life-or-death decisions.
details: Yesterday it was about the right way to stack firewood. It went on for twenty minutes.
The volume and intensity never match the stakes. Every disagreement is at full throttle.
reactions: I've stopped trying to mediate. They enjoy the argument more than the resolution.

entries.intro: {npc_name} gave me advice I didn't ask for, and it turned out to be exactly what I needed.
details: Unsolicited wisdom is usually annoying. This time it landed perfectly.
I was too stubborn to see the obvious and {npc_name} wasn't too polite to point it out.
reactions: I thanked them later. They shrugged like it was nothing, but it wasn't nothing.

entries.intro: {npc_name} promises things and then conveniently forgets. The pattern is getting old.
details: Three times this month alone. Each promise delivered with full sincerity: each one forgotten by the next day.
I've started saying 'I'll believe it when I see it' and they look hurt every time.
reactions: The intentions are probably real. The follow-through just isn't.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} admitted they promise in the moment because saying no feels wrong. They know they overcommit and hate themselves for it.
statCheck.fail: People who mean well but deliver nothing: I know the type.

entries.intro: {npc_name} checks on me when I've had a bad day. Just shows up, no fuss.
details: They don't pry or push. Just a 'how are you holding up' and a willingness to sit there if I need it.
The checking-in is never performative. They actually want to know.
reactions: Having someone who notices when you're low and cares enough to show up: that's not common.

entries.intro: {npc_name} leaves conversations mid-sentence and walks away. No warning, no excuse.
details: You'll be talking and then they're just gone. Walking off like the conversation ended ten words ago.
I've started keeping my points short. The window of attention is narrow.
reactions: I don't think it's rudeness exactly. Their mind just moves on before their manners catch up.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} told me they leave because they've processed what they need and staying longer feels like pretending. Efficiency, not disrespect.
statCheck.fail: They might just have a short attention span. Not everyone is built for long conversations.

entries.intro: {npc_name} remembered my preference for something I mentioned once, months ago.
details: I didn't even remember telling them. But there it was: exactly right, without me having to say a word.
That kind of attention feels deliberate. You don't remember small things about people you don't care about.
reactions: Being remembered in the small details: that's how you know someone's paying attention.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} told me they remember small things about people because being forgotten is the thing they fear most.
statCheck.fail: Some people just have sharp memories for details. A gift, not a choice.

entries.intro: {npc_name} sighs constantly. Loud, theatrical sighs at everything, all day.
details: Morning sigh, midday sigh, evening sigh: each one heavy enough to carry the weight of the world.
I asked once if something was wrong and they said 'no, why?' then immediately sighed again.
reactions: I've made peace with the soundtrack. It's just how {npc_name} breathes, apparently.

entries.intro: {npc_name} and {npc_name_2} walk everywhere together. I don't think I've seen one without the other.
details: Morning errands, evening strolls, even just standing around: always side by side.
It's not clingy. It's just a preference so strong it looks like a rule.
reactions: Some people are better in pairs. Those two are proof of it.

entries.intro: {npc_name} lent me something when I had nothing to offer in return. No strings.
details: I tried to insist on some kind of arrangement and they waved me off.
The generosity wasn't about getting something back. It was just about the giving.
reactions: I'll repay it when I can. Not because they expect it, but because they deserve it.

entries.intro: {npc_name} takes credit for group efforts. Every time.
details: We all worked on something together and by the end of the day {npc_name} was the hero of the story.
Nobody else gets mentioned. The narrative reshapes itself around {npc_name} every time.
reactions: The people who were there know the truth. That has to be enough.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} told me they highlight their own contributions because they're afraid of being overlooked. Being invisible is worse than being seen as self-serving.
statCheck.fail: Some people just need the credit. It's not a great trait, but it's a common one.

entries.intro: {npc_name} stood up for me when nobody else would. That kind of thing stays with you.
details: The situation was uncomfortable and everyone else looked at their feet. {npc_name} spoke up.
It cost them nothing practical and everything socially, and they did it anyway.
reactions: You learn who people are when there's something to lose. {npc_name} showed me exactly who they are.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} told me they spoke up because silence in that moment would have been the same as agreement. They couldn't live with that.
statCheck.fail: Some people are brave in the moment without thinking about it. Instinct, not calculation.

entries.intro: {npc_name} has a way of making everything about themselves. You share something and they redirect.
details: I told them about my week and within two sentences we were back to their problems.
The pivot is seamless. You don't even notice it until you realize you're listening instead of talking.
reactions: I've stopped sharing things that matter with them. The space for it just isn't there.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} admitted they redirect because other people's struggles trigger their own. It's not dismissal: it's overflow.
statCheck.fail: They might not realize they do it. Self-awareness isn't everyone's strong suit.

entries.intro: {npc_name} always has a kind word ready. Even when things are hard, they find something warm to say.
details: It's never forced or hollow. The warmth is natural, like heat from a hearth.
I've leaned on those words more than once without telling them.
reactions: People who light rooms up when they walk in don't always know they do it.

entries.intro: {npc_name} and {npc_name_2} can't agree on anything, but they keep trying. I'll give them that.
details: Every conversation is a negotiation. Neither gives ground easily.
They always reach some kind of compromise, eventually, both looking equally unsatisfied.
reactions: Stubborn people who keep talking to each other are better than stubborn people who stop.

entries.intro: {npc_name} squints when they think you're wrong but won't say it outright.
details: The squint is unmistakable. One eye narrows just slightly and the nod becomes slower.
I've tested this: I'll say something deliberately wrong and watch the squint arrive on cue.
reactions: I'd prefer the direct correction, but the squint is honest in its own way.

entries.intro: {npc_name} waits for everyone to finish eating before they start. Old-fashioned, but it means something.
details: Their plate sits untouched while others are halfway done. They don't make a point of it: they just wait.
It's the kind of small courtesy that says a lot about how someone was raised.
reactions: Manners like that don't show up by accident. Somebody taught them well.

entries.intro: {npc_name} interrupts. Every conversation, without fail, they cut in before you're done.
details: I'll be mid-thought and there they are, talking over me like I'd already finished.
The worst part is they usually have something worth saying. The delivery just ruins it.
reactions: I've started holding up a finger when they try. It works about half the time.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} told me they interrupt because the thought vanishes if they don't say it immediately. It's a race between their mouth and their memory.
statCheck.fail: Probably just impatient. Some people can't wait their turn.

entries.intro: {npc_name} apologized for something small and really meant it. You could hear it in their voice.
details: It wasn't a big deal, honestly. But they treated it like it mattered, which made it matter.
A sincere apology is rare enough that you notice when you get one.
reactions: I'd rather hear a real apology for something small than a hollow one for something big.

entries.intro: {npc_name} picks favorites and doesn't try to hide it. You're either in or you're out.
details: Some people get the warm greeting and the easy smile. The rest of us get a nod at best.
I've never figured out what the criteria is. The favorites don't seem to have much in common.
reactions: I stopped trying to earn a spot. The nod is fine.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} told me the favoritism is instinctive, not deliberate. Some people put them at ease and others don't, and they can't explain why.
statCheck.fail: Everyone plays favorites. {npc_name} is just more obvious about it than most.

entries.intro: {npc_name} and {npc_name_2} cook for each other without being asked. There's always an extra portion.
details: One makes something in the morning: the other shows up with something in the evening. Unplanned, consistent.
It's not a trade. It's just two people who think of each other around mealtime.
reactions: Feeding someone is its own language. Those two are fluent.

entries.intro: {npc_name} talks about themselves in a way that makes me feel bad for them without them asking me to.
details: Little mentions: 'I'm used to it' or 'nobody expects much from me.' Dropped casually into conversation.
It's not a plea. It's more like a fact they've accepted and I haven't.
reactions: I want to argue with the way they see themselves, but they'd just shrug it off.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} told me the self-minimizing is armor. If they say it first, nobody else's low opinion can surprise them.
statCheck.fail: Some people just carry a dim view of themselves. It's hard to watch but harder to change.

entries.intro: {npc_name} brought me something I needed without me mentioning it. Just knew.
details: I hadn't said a word about needing it. They saw the problem before I described it.
The timing was too perfect to be coincidence. They were paying attention.
reactions: Anticipating what someone needs before they ask: that takes real care.

entries.intro: {npc_name} cracks their knuckles constantly. In every conversation, at every pause.
details: The sound punctuates their sentences like a period. Sentence, crack, sentence, crack.
I've cringed so many times that my cringe reflex is getting tired.
reactions: It's their hands, not mine. I just wish the sound wasn't quite so loud.

entries.intro: You'd think the {npc_role} would be more organized. Things fall through the cracks constantly.
details: I've reminded them about the same thing three times this week. Each time they look surprised.
The work gets done, eventually, but the path from start to finish is anything but straight.
reactions: Maybe the job needs a different kind of person. Or maybe the person needs a different kind of system.

statCheck.pass: The {npc_role} told me they're barely keeping up. The work grew faster than the help did and they're too stubborn to say so out loud.
statCheck.fail: Could be they're just stretched thin. One person can only handle so much.

entries.intro: {npc_name} walked me home when it was late and I didn't ask. Just fell into step beside me.
details: We didn't talk much. The company was the point, not the conversation.
They peeled off at their own door with a wave, like it was the most natural thing.
reactions: Small acts of looking out for someone: that's the backbone of being neighbors.

entries.intro: {npc_name} and {npc_name_2} have been avoiding each other. It's obvious to everyone.
details: They take different paths, eat at different times, find reasons to be wherever the other isn't.
Neither has said anything about it. The silence around the subject is louder than the avoidance.
reactions: Whatever happened, they'll sort it out or they won't. Not my business to push.

statCheck.pass: I heard it started over something one of them said that the other wasn't meant to hear. Words spoken carelessly landing heavily.
statCheck.fail: People drift apart sometimes. It might blow over on its own.

entries.intro: {npc_name} keeps their workspace immaculate. Everything has a place and nothing wanders.
details: I looked at their setup once and felt immediate shame about my own.
The order isn't rigid: it's comfortable. Like someone who genuinely thinks in straight lines.
reactions: I tried to copy the system. Lasted three days before chaos reclaimed its territory.

entries.intro: {npc_name} tells the same stories over and over. Word for word, same punchline.
details: I've heard the one about the broken fence at least six times. I could tell it myself at this point.
The enthusiasm never dims. They deliver each repeat like it's opening night.
reactions: I laugh every time. Not at the story, but at the joy they take in telling it.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} told me the stories are anchors. Retelling them keeps certain moments alive when everything else fades.
statCheck.fail: Some people just enjoy the telling. The audience might change, but the story doesn't need to.

entries.intro: {npc_name} makes eye contact that's a little too intense. Like they're reading your thoughts.
details: Most people glance away now and then. {npc_name} locks on and holds.
It makes simple conversations feel like confessions.
reactions: I've mostly gotten used to it, though the first few seconds still catch me off guard.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} told me they hold eye contact because looking away feels like dismissal. The intensity is respect, not aggression.
statCheck.fail: Some people just look at you when they talk. It's unnerving, but probably harmless.

entries.intro: {npc_name} defended my cooking in front of a group. Didn't have to, but they did.
details: Someone made a comment and {npc_name} shut it down with a tone that left no room for reply.
The defense was more passionate than my cooking deserved, honestly.
reactions: Having someone stand on your side without being asked: that's loyalty showing itself.

entries.intro: {npc_name} borrows things and returns them late. Every single time.
details: I lend something expecting a day and get it back after a week, sometimes two.
The item always comes back fine. It's just the timeline that's fictional.
reactions: I've adjusted my lending expectations accordingly. 'A day' means 'a week' in {npc_name} time.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} told me the lateness with returns isn't forgetfulness: they keep things because having something of someone else's feels like a connection they're not ready to let go of.
statCheck.fail: Probably just disorganized. Some people lose track of borrowed things.

entries.intro: {npc_name} and {npc_name_2} trade favors without keeping score. One gives, the other gives back, naturally.
details: There's no ledger, no 'you owe me.' The balance maintains itself through goodwill.
I've watched this cycle for months. It never tips, never sours.
reactions: That's what trust looks like in practice: generosity without arithmetic.

entries.intro: {npc_name} scratches behind their ear when they're about to say something they're not sure about.
details: The ear scratch is the tell. Everything after it is guesswork dressed up as opinion.
I've started waiting for the scratch before deciding how much weight to give what follows.
reactions: Once you know someone's tells, conversations become more honest, even if they don't know you're reading them.

entries.intro: {npc_name} laughs at their own jokes before anyone else can. The punchline arrives pre-appreciated.
details: They start giggling halfway through the setup and by the end they're the only one laughing.
It's contagious though. You end up laughing at them laughing, which is almost the same thing.
reactions: Self-amusement is underrated. {npc_name} is their own best audience.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} told me they laugh first because they grew up telling jokes to empty rooms. Being their own audience became habit before anyone else showed up.
statCheck.fail: Self-amusement is just a personality trait. Some people entertain themselves first.

entries.intro: {npc_name} is the first person I'd go to in a pinch. Reliable down to the bone.
details: No hesitation, no conditions. You say you need help and they're already moving.
I've never been let down. Not once.
reactions: Knowing someone you can count on absolutely changes how you walk through the day.

entries.intro: {npc_name} has a habit of one-upping every story. You climbed a hill: they climbed a mountain.
details: I shared something I was proud of and before I finished they'd already topped it with their version.
The competition isn't hostile, but it makes you stop wanting to share.
reactions: I've learned to keep my small victories to myself around them.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} told me the one-upping is how they relate: they share something similar to show they understand, not to compete. The effect is the opposite of the intent.
statCheck.fail: Some people just can't let someone else have the spotlight. It's reflex, not malice.

entries.intro: {npc_name} gave me a compliment that was so specific it had to be true.
details: Not a vague 'good job.' They named the exact thing I'd done well and why it mattered.
Generic praise slides off, but specific praise sticks: that one stuck.
reactions: I'm still thinking about it days later. {npc_name} probably has no idea.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} told me they give specific compliments because vague ones meant nothing to them growing up. They swore they'd never hand out words that didn't land.
statCheck.fail: Some people are just observant. They notice what others miss.

entries.intro: {npc_name} shows up late to everything. Not a little late: consistently, reliably late.
details: I've started telling them things start earlier than they do. The buffer helps, sometimes.
They always have a reason, and the reason is always different, and it's always delivered with genuine surprise that time passed.
reactions: I don't think time works the same way for them as it does for the rest of us.

entries.intro: {npc_name} and {npc_name_2} have their own way of greeting each other. A little gesture nobody else uses.
details: It's quick: a hand motion and a nod. Over in half a second, but it carries weight.
I asked about it and they both laughed without explaining. Some things are private.
reactions: Shared rituals between two people are a language with an audience of two.

entries.intro: {npc_name} catches small mistakes before they become big ones. Saves everyone trouble.
details: They spotted a problem I'd missed and mentioned it quietly, no fuss.
The correction saved me half a day's work at least.
reactions: Attention to detail like that is a gift to everyone around them.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} told me they catch mistakes early because a small error cost someone they cared about dearly once. The vigilance is prevention dressed as habit.
statCheck.fail: Detail-oriented people exist. Not everything careful has a sad origin.

entries.intro: {npc_name} gives directions that make no sense. 'Turn at the thing, past the place, you'll see it.'
details: I followed their directions once and ended up completely turned around.
They were baffled when I couldn't find it. In their head, the directions were crystal clear.
