reactions: I've learned to ask someone else or just figure it out myself.

entries.intro: {npc_name} listened to me complain for a long time without trying to fix anything. Just listened.
details: No advice, no solutions, no 'have you tried.' Just a nod and silence in the right places.
By the time I was done talking, the problem felt smaller. They didn't do anything except be there.
reactions: A good listener is worth more than ten good advisors.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} told me they listen without fixing because they learned that most people don't want solutions. They want proof that someone heard them.
statCheck.fail: Some people are just patient. Good listeners don't always have a method.

entries.intro: {npc_name} clicks their tongue when they disapprove. One sharp click and you know exactly where you stand.
details: It's more effective than words. The click carries a full paragraph of judgment in a single sound.
I've started modifying my behavior preemptively to avoid the click. That's how powerful it is.
reactions: Communication doesn't need to be verbal to be perfectly clear.

entries.intro: I trust {npc_name}'s judgment about people more than my own. They read others better than I do.
details: Every time they've told me to be careful around someone, they turned out to be right.
It's not gossip: it's observation. They watch and they remember and they draw conclusions that hold up.
reactions: Having a good judge of character nearby is like having a compass you didn't bring yourself.

entries.intro: {npc_name} says 'no offense' before every offensive thing they say. The warning doesn't help.
details: The two words have become a siren. I hear 'no offense' and brace for impact.
I've pointed out the pattern and they said 'no offense, but you're too sensitive.'
reactions: I've accepted the package deal. The honesty and the sandpaper delivery come together.

entries.intro: {npc_name} and {npc_name_2} disagree about almost everything but respect each other through it.
details: The arguments are genuine and neither holds back, but it never turns personal.
They can disagree loudly over a meal and share a laugh before it's over.
reactions: Disagreement without disrespect is a skill most people never learn.

entries.intro: {npc_name} has a laugh that fills a room. You hear it from three streets away.
details: It's big and sudden and impossible to ignore. People turn their heads every time.
The laugh doesn't match their quiet speaking voice at all. It's like two different people.
reactions: Honestly, it improves every gathering. A big laugh makes other people braver about their own.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} told me they used to be embarrassed by the laugh. Now they see it as a gift: people know when the joy is real.
statCheck.fail: Big laughs just happen to some people. Not much philosophy behind it.

entries.intro: {npc_name} snaps when they're stressed and apologizes after. Every time, same cycle.
details: The snap is sharp and unexpected: a harsh word out of nowhere over nothing.
Then an hour later, a quiet 'I'm sorry, I didn't mean that.' And they mean the apology.
reactions: The apology is genuine. I just wish it came before the snap, not after.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} told me they know the pattern and hate it. The stress builds until something minor cracks the surface and whoever is nearby catches it.
statCheck.fail: Stress gets to everyone differently. Some people just run hot.

entries.intro: {npc_name} teaches without making you feel stupid. That's rarer than it should be.
details: They explain things patiently, at your pace, without the sighs or the condescension.
I asked a question I thought was embarrassingly basic and they treated it with total seriousness.
reactions: Good teachers make you feel smarter for asking. Bad ones make you wish you hadn't.

entries.intro: {npc_name} has opinions about how everyone else should do their work. Loudly.
details: I was handling something my way and they appeared with a commentary that nobody requested.
The opinions aren't always wrong. They're just always uninvited.
reactions: If I want a supervisor, I'll find one. Until then, I'll manage.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} admitted the unsolicited advice comes from anxiety. Seeing things done differently makes them uneasy, so they try to steer everyone toward what feels safe.
statCheck.fail: Some people just think they know better. Sometimes they do, but the delivery still matters.

entries.intro: {npc_name} and {npc_name_2} started working on something together and it's going surprisingly well.
details: I wouldn't have paired them myself. Their styles are completely different.
But the differences are complementary, apparently. What one misses, the other catches.
reactions: Some partnerships only make sense from the outside after they're already working.

entries.intro: {npc_name} keeps to themselves so completely that I forget they're here sometimes.
details: Days go by without a sighting. Then I'll catch a glimpse and remember: right, they live here.
It's not unfriendly. It's more like they occupy a different version of the same place.
reactions: Some people just need less of other people. I try not to take it personally.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} told me the isolation isn't preference: it's protection. Being around people drains something they can't replenish quickly.
statCheck.fail: Private people are private. Not much more to it than that.

entries.intro: {npc_name} took my side in a disagreement even though they probably agreed with the other person.
details: I could tell they weren't fully convinced, but they backed me anyway. Loyalty over accuracy.
That's a choice not everyone makes. Most people side with whoever's right, not whoever's theirs.
reactions: I'd do the same for them. That's the deal, whether it's spoken or not.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} told me loyalty to people matters more than being right. They'd rather be wrong beside you than correct against you.
statCheck.fail: Some people just pick a side and stay there. Loyalty over logic.

entries.intro: {npc_name} taps the table when they're impatient. The rhythm gets faster the longer you talk.
details: I've tried speeding up my point, but the tapping always outpaces me.
By the end of a long explanation, the table sounds like a drum roll.
reactions: I keep my points short now. The table thanks me.

entries.intro: {npc_name} makes everyone feel included. Nobody sits alone when they're around.
details: I've watched them notice someone on the edge of a group and pull them in without making it obvious.
The skill is in the subtlety. You don't see the work: you just see the result.
reactions: People like that change the shape of a room just by being in it.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} told me they include people deliberately because they know exactly how it feels to be the one standing at the edge, uninvited.
statCheck.fail: They're just socially aware. Not everyone has a wound behind the kindness.

entries.intro: {npc_name} leaves messes for others to clean. Not their messes: shared spaces.
details: They use something, set it down wherever, and walk away like gravity will sort it out.
I've cleaned up after them more times than I can count. They've never noticed.
reactions: I've stopped expecting change and started working around it.

entries.intro: {npc_name} and {npc_name_2} protect each other's reputations behind closed doors. I've overheard it.
details: Someone said something unkind about one of them, and the other shut it down immediately, firmly.
They don't report it to each other. The defense is for its own sake, not for credit.
reactions: Defending someone who isn't there to hear it: that's loyalty without an audience.

entries.intro: {npc_name} has an answer for everything. Right or wrong, they never hesitate.
details: The confidence is total, even when the information is questionable.
I've fact-checked them silently and the accuracy rate is maybe half. But the delivery is always a ten.
reactions: Confidence without knowledge is entertaining until you rely on it.

entries.intro: {npc_name} admits when they're wrong. Fully, immediately, without excuses.
details: No qualifying, no 'but,' no saving face. Just 'I was wrong' and a genuine correction.
It's disarming. You prepare for an argument and get honesty instead.
reactions: People who own their mistakes are people I want in my corner.

entries.intro: {npc_name} compares everything to how it was before. Nothing is ever as good as it used to be.
details: The food was better, the weather was milder, the people were friendlier: all in some golden past.
I've lived here the same amount of time. I don't remember this paradise they keep describing.
reactions: Nostalgia is a lens that flatters the past and dims the present.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} admitted the past they describe isn't entirely real. It's more how they wish things had been, polished by distance and need.
statCheck.fail: Some people just live looking backward. The view is always kinder from there.

entries.intro: {npc_name} puts food out for the animals. Not pets: just whatever comes by.
details: A little bowl near the door, filled each morning, empty each evening.
They never talk about it. I only know because I've seen them refill it.
reactions: Kindness that extends past people says something about the person.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} told me they feed the animals because something that small depending on them gives the day a purpose even when nothing else does.
statCheck.fail: Some people just like animals. A bowl of scraps doesn't need a reason.

entries.intro: {npc_name} speaks in riddles when a straight answer would do. Everything is a metaphor.
details: I asked a yes-or-no question and got a parable about seasons.
The metaphors aren't bad. They're just deployed when clarity would serve better.
reactions: I've started asking 'but what do you actually mean' after every response. It speeds things up.

entries.intro: {npc_name} and {npc_name_2} balance each other out. One's too cautious, the other's too bold, and together they land somewhere sensible.
details: Alone, either one would be too much in one direction. Together, they're steady.
I've watched the push and pull play out over small decisions and it always ends at a good middle.
reactions: Not every pair needs to agree. Sometimes the tension itself is the strength.

entries.intro: {npc_name} chews with their mouth open and I've run out of polite ways to not notice.
details: Mealtimes are a performance of sound and spectacle. I've changed where I sit.
They're completely unaware. The enjoyment is genuine: the volume is a side effect.
reactions: Small things shouldn't bother me this much. And yet.

entries.intro: {npc_name} works hard without expecting thanks. The work is the point, not the recognition.
details: I thanked them once and they looked confused, like the concept of gratitude for effort didn't compute.
The work gets done because it needs to: that's the whole motivation.
reactions: More people should have that attitude. Less noise, more results.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} told me they avoid thanks because accepting gratitude makes the act feel less clean. They want the work to exist without the weight of being owed something.
statCheck.fail: Private people just prefer to work without fuss. Not everyone wants attention.

entries.intro: {npc_name} volunteers information nobody asked for. Constantly.
details: I mentioned the weather and got a lecture on cloud patterns. I was making small talk.
The knowledge is impressive. The instinct to share all of it at once is less so.
reactions: I've developed a patient nod for these moments. It's getting a lot of practice.

entries.intro: {npc_name} smiles at people like they actually mean it. Not the polite kind: the real kind.
details: You can see it in the eyes. The mouth follows, but the eyes start it.
I catch myself smiling back before I've even decided to.
reactions: A genuine smile is free and changes someone's whole morning. {npc_name} gives them out freely.

entries.intro: {npc_name} never admits they don't know something. The guessing is presented as fact.
details: I've asked them things nobody could possibly know and received confident, immediate answers.
The answers are wrong about as often as they're right, but the delivery never wavers.
reactions: I've learned to verify independently. {npc_name}'s confidence is not correlated with accuracy.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} told me saying 'I don't know' feels like failing a test. The guessing is preferable to the admission, even when the guess is worse.
statCheck.fail: Some people just hate uncertainty. Filling the gap with a guess feels better than leaving it empty.

entries.intro: You'd think the {npc_role} would be more approachable. The job requires it.
details: Every interaction feels like I'm bothering them. The sighs when I approach are audible.
The work gets done, but the attitude makes you think twice before asking.
reactions: I'd be more forgiving if the position didn't come with an expectation of patience.

statCheck.pass: The {npc_role} told me they're burned out on people asking the same things repeatedly. The impatience isn't personal: it's cumulative.
statCheck.fail: Maybe they're just not suited to dealing with people. Some roles don't match the person.

entries.intro: {npc_name} and {npc_name_2} had a quiet conversation that left both of them looking lighter afterward.
details: I couldn't hear what was said, but the body language changed: shoulders dropped, pace slowed.
Whatever words passed between them, they were the right ones at the right time.
reactions: Sometimes all you need is one conversation with the right person.

entries.intro: {npc_name} raises their voice when they disagree, like volume makes an argument stronger.
details: The point might be valid, but it arrives wrapped in enough noise that the content gets lost.
I've noticed people agree with them faster just to bring the volume down.
reactions: Agreement reached through fatigue isn't really agreement. But it's quieter.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} told me the volume is unintentional: when they care about something, the heat rises before they realize it. They wish they could modulate better.
statCheck.fail: Loud people don't always know they're loud. It's just their normal.

entries.intro: {npc_name} makes time to talk even when they're clearly busy. That choice says something.
details: I can see the pile of things waiting for them, and they still stop to give me their full attention.
The unhurried conversation in the middle of a hurried day: that's a gift of time they don't have.
reactions: People who prioritize other people over their tasks are the ones worth knowing.

entries.intro: {npc_name} manages to look busy while accomplishing very little. The performance is flawless.
details: Always moving, always carrying something, always looking purposeful. The output doesn't match the activity.
I watched for an hour once. A lot of motion, very few results.
reactions: Energy without direction is just exercise.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} admitted the busy performance is habit from a time when looking idle meant getting more work piled on. The act outlasted the reason.
statCheck.fail: Maybe they're just not very efficient. Movement and progress aren't the same thing.

entries.intro: {npc_name} has this way of saying exactly one sentence that reframes everything you were thinking.
details: I was stuck on a problem and they said something simple that flipped my whole perspective.
The insight arrived like it was obvious, but I'd been circling it for days without seeing it.
reactions: Some people think in shortcuts. {npc_name} finds the path that saves everyone else the long way around.

entries.intro: {npc_name} hovers when they want something but won't ask. Just lingers nearby, waiting.
details: They stand close enough to be noticed but far enough to pretend they're not there for a reason.
I've started asking 'what do you need' the moment the hovering begins. Cuts the dance short.
reactions: Asking directly would be faster for both of us, but that's not how they're built.

entries.intro: {npc_name} has steady hands and a steadier temperament. Nothing rattles them.
details: I've seen situations that would fray anyone else and {npc_name} barely blinks.
The calm isn't apathy: it's control. There's a difference.
reactions: Being near someone unshakable makes the shakable things feel smaller.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} told me the calm is practiced, not natural. They decided a long time ago that visible panic helps no one, and trained themselves to hold still.
statCheck.fail: Some people are just naturally even-keeled. Lucky for the rest of us.

entries.intro: {npc_name} uses guilt like a tool. 'After everything I've done for you': that kind of thing.
details: The ledger of past favors gets opened whenever they need something. Every kindness was apparently a loan.
I've started declining favors from them. The interest rate is too high.
reactions: Generosity with strings attached isn't generosity. It's an investment.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} told me the guilt tactic is something they learned growing up. Love and obligation were the same thing in their household.
statCheck.fail: Some people keep score. It changes the nature of everything they give.

entries.intro: {npc_name} and {npc_name_2} tell completely different versions of the same events. Both absolutely certain.
details: I heard one version, then the other, and couldn't reconcile a single detail.
The truth is probably somewhere between them. But neither would accept that.
reactions: Memory is personal. Two honest people can remember the same thing entirely differently.

entries.intro: {npc_name} is funny in a quiet way. Not loud jokes: just observations that catch you off guard.
details: They'll say something under their breath that's funnier than anything said out loud all day.
The humor requires proximity: from a distance they look serious, but up close they're hilarious.
reactions: Quiet humor is the best kind. It rewards attention.

entries.intro: {npc_name} agrees with whoever they're talking to. Every time, no matter the topic.
details: I said something I don't even believe, just to test it, and got full agreement.
The nodding is automatic. I don't think they process the content before endorsing it.
reactions: Agreement without substance is just noise shaped like approval.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} told me they agree with everyone because disagreement cost them relationships before. Saying yes keeps the peace, even when it's hollow.
statCheck.fail: Maybe they're just easygoing. Not everyone needs to push back on every point.

entries.intro: {npc_name} treats strangers with the same warmth they give people they've known for years.
details: I've watched them welcome newcomers with genuine openness: no sizing up, no holding back.
It's not naivety. It's a choice to assume the best until proven otherwise.
reactions: The world is kinder when people like {npc_name} are in it.

entries.intro: {npc_name} and {npc_name_2} try to outdo each other in generosity. It's escalated into a quiet war of kindness.
details: One brought extra food for a neighbor: the other brought twice as much the next day.
Neither will admit they're competing. Both insist they're just being thoughtful.
reactions: If all rivalries looked like this, the world would be a better place.

entries.intro: {npc_name} whistles while they walk. Every day, every path, same habit.
details: You can track their location by the sound. The whistling precedes them like a herald.
Some days the tune is cheerful, some days it's not. The mood is in the melody.
reactions: It's become part of the background. I notice when it's missing more than when it's there.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} told me the whistling keeps thoughts at bay. When the tune is playing, the quiet parts of their mind stay quiet.
statCheck.fail: Probably just a lifelong habit. People whistle for all kinds of reasons.

entries.intro: {npc_name} always points out what's wrong before noticing what's right. The lens only faces one way.
details: I showed them something I'd finished and the first words were about the flaws, not the work.
The criticism is technically accurate and emotionally tone-deaf.
reactions: I've stopped sharing things I'm proud of with them. The pride doesn't survive the review.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} told me they criticize first because that's how they were taught: fix the problem, then appreciate the rest. They genuinely don't see it as unkind.
statCheck.fail: Some people are wired to find fault first. It's just their default setting.

entries.intro: {npc_name} asked how I was and then waited for a real answer instead of a polite one.
details: The pause after the question was deliberate. They weren't moving on: they were making space.
I gave the real answer for once. The space they made demanded honesty.
reactions: Most people ask 'how are you' as a greeting. {npc_name} asks it as a question.

entries.intro: {npc_name} drops everything when something more interesting comes along. Projects die mid-stroke.
details: I've seen three half-started things abandoned in a week. Each one replaced by the next enthusiasm.
The energy for new things is boundless. The endurance for finishing them is nonexistent.
reactions: I've accepted the landscape of half-done work. It's just how they operate.

entries.intro: The {npc_role} has been doing a solid job. Steady, no surprises, no complaints.
details: Things run smoothly and nobody thinks about why. That's the mark of good work.
The best sign is that there's nothing to talk about. Quiet competence.
reactions: I'd rather have reliable and unremarkable than flashy and inconsistent.

entries.intro: {npc_name} talks to everyone the same way: direct, plain, no adjustment for audience.
details: Whether it's me or a stranger, the tone doesn't shift. The words are the same.
Some find it rude, but I find it refreshing: you always know exactly what you're getting.
reactions: Consistency of character is underrated. {npc_name} is the same person in every room.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} told me they talk to everyone the same because code-switching exhausted them. Being one person in every room is the only version they can sustain.
statCheck.fail: Consistency might just be how they're built. Not everyone adjusts.

entries.intro: {npc_name} cried in front of me once. It changed how I see them.
details: Not dramatically: just quietly, unexpectedly, like a wall came down for a moment.
They composed themselves quickly and we never spoke of it. But I remember.
reactions: Seeing someone's guard drop, even briefly, is a kind of trust: whether they meant to give it or not.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} told me that moment slipped through a crack they usually keep sealed. They weren't ready for me to see it, but they don't regret it.
statCheck.fail: Everyone has a moment. Most people move past it quickly.

entries.intro: {npc_name} and {npc_name_2} always sit next to each other. Not across: beside.
details: The seating is automatic. Neither checks: they just end up side by side.
I sat in one of their usual spots once and the confusion was genuine. I'd disrupted a natural law.
reactions: Habits between two people say more about a bond than any declaration could.

entries.intro: {npc_name} holds doors, carries loads, offers seats. The small courtesies that most people forget.
details: It's automatic. They don't think about it: the helping is as natural as breathing.
I've watched people walk past the same opportunities without blinking. {npc_name} never does.
reactions: The world is held together by small gestures from people who bother.

entries.intro: {npc_name} sulks when things don't go their way. The mood fills the room.
details: Silence, heavy sighing, a cloud of displeasure that makes everyone around them uncomfortable.
Nobody addresses it because addressing it makes it worse.
reactions: I give the mood space to pass. It always does: it just takes its time.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} told me the sulking is the only way they know how to process disappointment. Talking about it makes the feeling bigger, not smaller.
statCheck.fail: Some people wear their displeasure like a coat. You see it before you hear it.

entries.intro: {npc_name} brought up a conversation we had weeks ago and built on it. They'd been thinking about it the whole time.
details: I'd forgotten what I'd said. They hadn't: they'd taken it seriously and followed the thought.
The continuation was better than the original. They'd added depth I hadn't considered.
reactions: Being taken seriously like that: it makes you want to bring better thoughts next time.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} told me they hold onto conversations because most people don't take theirs seriously. When someone does, the thought gets carried carefully.
statCheck.fail: Some people just have long attention spans for ideas. A good quality.

entries.intro: {npc_name} leaves in the middle of group conversations without saying goodbye. Just vanishes.
details: You turn around and they're gone. No exit, no wave, no 'I have to go.'
I've started checking for their presence every few minutes. The disappearances are sudden.
reactions: The abruptness is jarring, but the absence is always temporary. They'll be back.

entries.intro: {npc_name} gives their full attention when you're speaking. No wandering gaze, no distractions.
details: The focus is complete and slightly intimidating. You can feel your words being weighed.
It makes you speak more carefully because you know everything is landing.
reactions: Full attention from another person is a gift most of us don't give often enough.

entries.intro: {npc_name} stirs trouble and then steps back to watch the result. Every single time.
details: A pointed comment here, a repeated rumor there, and then they're nowhere to be found when the consequences arrive.
The hands are clean. The fingerprints are on everything.
reactions: I've learned to trace arguments back to their source. The trail often leads to the same door.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} told me they stir things because stillness makes them restless. Drama is entertainment when you're bored with peace.
statCheck.fail: Maybe they just talk without thinking about where words end up.

entries.intro: {npc_name} and {npc_name_2} have a habit of bringing each other small things. Nothing valuable: just tokens.
details: A smooth stone, an interesting leaf, something found and thought worth sharing.
The objects aren't the point. The 'I thought of you' is the point.
reactions: Little gifts with no occasion behind them: that's affection in its simplest form.

entries.intro: {npc_name} stretches the truth so often I've stopped knowing where the line is.
details: Small exaggerations mostly: a fish gets bigger, a walk gets longer, a wait gets more dramatic.
None of it matters much, but the habit makes you question the parts that do.
reactions: I divide everything they say by two. The adjusted version is usually closer to reality.

entries.intro: {npc_name} is the kind of person who notices when something's different and says so.
details: A haircut, a change of pace, a quiet mood: they see it and name it without making it awkward.
Being observed by someone who mentions it gently is different from being watched.
reactions: Observation paired with kindness: that's the combination that makes people feel seen.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} told me they name changes out loud because they spent a long time being invisible. Seeing others clearly is the opposite of what they experienced.
statCheck.fail: Observant people notice things. Some of them are kind enough to say so.

entries.intro: {npc_name} mutters complaints about their food while eating every bite. The bowl is always empty.
details: I've never seen them leave anything, no matter how vocally they disapprove.
The criticism and the consumption proceed in perfect parallel. Neither pauses for the other.
reactions: If the food were really that bad, the bowl wouldn't be that clean.

entries.intro: {npc_name} called me by a nickname I didn't know I had. Apparently everyone uses it.
details: They said it so casually I almost didn't notice. Then I realized: that's what they call me when I'm not around.
It's not unkind. Actually, I sort of like it.
reactions: Having a nickname means people talk about you enough to need a shorter version.

entries.intro: {npc_name} and {npc_name_2} share tools without needing to ask. One puts something down: the other picks it up.
details: The handoff is seamless, like a practiced routine that neither remembers learning.
I tried to borrow one of those tools once and got confused looks from both, as if the tool was in perpetual shared use.
reactions: That level of casual sharing means the boundaries between 'mine' and 'yours' dissolved a long time ago.

entries.intro: {npc_name} sat with me during a hard day and didn't say a word. Didn't need to.
details: They just showed up, sat down, and stayed. The silence was companionable, not empty.
When they left, the hard day was still hard, but I wasn't carrying it alone anymore.
reactions: Presence without words is sometimes the most useful thing a person can offer.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} told me they sat with me because silence saved them once when words couldn't. They were passing along something they received.
statCheck.fail: Some people just know when to be quiet. It's a rare and useful instinct.

entries.intro: {npc_name} always has to have the last word. Every conversation ends with them.
details: I tried once to just stop talking, and they waited before adding one final sentence with a look of satisfaction.
The need is so consistent I've made a game of it. How many rounds before they get the final one.
reactions: The last word is important to them. I've decided it's not important to me.

entries.intro: {npc_name} went out of their way to introduce me to people when I was new here.
details: Not just names: proper introductions with context. 'You'd get along' and 'you should talk about' this or that.
It saved me weeks of awkward first conversations. {npc_name} bridged every gap.
reactions: Connectors make a place feel small in the best way.

entries.intro: {npc_name} takes everything personally. Even things that have nothing to do with them.
details: I made a general comment about something and they assumed I was talking about them specifically.
The defensiveness comes before the understanding. The order never changes.
reactions: I've started qualifying everything with 'not about you' before I say it. It helps, a little.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} told me they take things personally because they were often the target when people spoke generally. The reflex doesn't know the difference.
statCheck.fail: Sensitive people hear criticism everywhere. It must be exhausting.

entries.intro: {npc_name} shares food without being asked. If they're eating and you're near, you're eating too.
details: A torn piece of bread appears in your hand before you've decided if you're hungry.
Refusing isn't really an option. The food is there and the expectation is that you take it.
reactions: Feeding people is {npc_name}'s way of saying hello. I've learned to arrive hungry.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} told me sharing food is how they were raised. Eating alone when someone nearby isn't eating feels wrong in their bones.
statCheck.fail: Generous people share. The impulse doesn't always need an explanation.

entries.intro: {npc_name} nods along to things they clearly disagree with. You can see the disagreement sitting behind the nod.
details: The mouth says 'mm-hm.' The eyes say 'absolutely not.'
I'd respect the disagreement more if they'd voice it. The nodding-while-disagreeing is unsettling.
reactions: A polite lie is still a lie. I'd take honest friction over smooth dishonesty.

entries.intro: {npc_name} can read a room better than anyone I've met. They know when to speak and when to be still.
details: I've watched them adjust their approach based on who's listening and what the mood is.
It's not manipulation. It's awareness: knowing that different moments need different things.
reactions: Social skill at that level is invisible. You only notice it when it's absent.

entries.intro: {npc_name} and {npc_name_2} don't talk to each other much, but there's no hostility. Just distance.
details: They occupy the same spaces without interacting. A polite nod at most.
It's not cold. It's more like two people who simply never found a reason to be close.
reactions: Not every pair of people becomes friends. Sometimes you just coexist.

entries.intro: {npc_name} rearranges things when they're anxious. Everything gets moved and then moved back.
details: I can tell how their day is going by whether the same objects are in the same place twice.
The rearranging produces no improvement. It's motion disguised as purpose.
reactions: Everyone has their coping mechanism. {npc_name}'s just happens to be visible.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} told me the rearranging gives their hands something to do while their mind won't settle. The objects don't matter: the movement does.
statCheck.fail: Maybe they're just restless. Idle hands and all that.

entries.intro: {npc_name} trusts people too easily. It worries me sometimes.
details: They take everyone at face value with no hesitation. The benefit of the doubt is infinite.
One day that trust will cost them something, and the lesson will be hard.
reactions: I keep an eye out without telling them. Someone has to.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} told me they'd rather be wrong about someone good than right about someone bad. The trust is a deliberate gamble, not naivety.
statCheck.fail: Some people are just trusting by nature. It's admirable until it isn't.

entries.intro: {npc_name} says 'I'll think about it' and then actually does. Comes back with a considered answer.
details: Most people say that to end a conversation. {npc_name} says it to start a process.
The delay between question and answer might be days, but the answer is always worth the wait.
reactions: Deliberate thought is rare. I've learned to wait for their answers.

entries.intro: {npc_name} blames the weather, the timing, the tools: anything but themselves when things go wrong.
details: I've heard a different excuse for every failure and none of them start with 'I.'
The creativity of the blame is almost impressive. The sources are endless: the responsibility is always elsewhere.
reactions: Everybody stumbles. The difference is who owns the stumble and who points at the ground.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} told me admitting fault feels like proving everyone's worst assumptions right. The excuses protect something fragile underneath.
statCheck.fail: Some people just can't take the blame. It's uncomfortable for everyone around them.

entries.intro: {npc_name} keeps promises like they're carved in stone. If they say it, it happens.
details: I've never once been let down. The reliability is almost unnerving because it's so consistent.
Other people's promises are hopes. {npc_name}'s promises are facts.
reactions: Trust that solid doesn't come from nowhere. It's built one kept promise at a time.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} told me they keep promises fiercely because a broken promise once broke something in them. They rebuilt around the commitment to never do that to someone else.
statCheck.fail: Reliable people are reliable. It's the kind of trait you don't question.

entries.intro: {npc_name} and {npc_name_2} were close once. Something shifted and now there's a careful politeness between them.
details: They still talk, but the warmth is gone. Replaced by courtesy: correct but thin.
I don't know what happened and neither seems willing to explain.
reactions: The gap between how people used to be and how they are now is sometimes the saddest distance.

statCheck.pass: I heard one of them said something during a hard time that the other couldn't forgive. Not cruelty: just the wrong words at the wrong moment.
statCheck.fail: People grow apart sometimes. Not every distance has a story behind it.

entries.intro: {npc_name} straightens their clothes before talking to anyone. A quick adjustment, then they're ready.
details: It's unconscious: a tug at the collar, a smoothing of the front. The preparation takes two seconds.
I've started noticing it every time now. The tiny ritual before every social encounter.
reactions: We all have our armor. {npc_name}'s is just more visible.

entries.intro: {npc_name} found me struggling and helped without making me feel small about it.
details: The help arrived sideways: 'mind if I give you a hand with that?' not 'you're doing it wrong.'
By the time it was done, I felt capable rather than rescued.
reactions: Help that preserves dignity is the highest form of kindness.

entries.intro: {npc_name} changes their opinion depending on who they're talking to. The position rotates.
details: I heard them agree with one thing in the morning and the opposite by evening with a different audience.
The shifts are smooth enough that you'd miss them if you weren't paying attention.
reactions: I've stopped trying to pin down what they actually think. The real opinion might not exist.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} told me they mirror the people around them because disagreement costs more energy than they have. The shifting isn't deception: it's exhaustion.
statCheck.fail: Some people just bend with the wind. You learn not to build on their words.

entries.intro: {npc_name} has a calm way of settling arguments between other people. Two sentences and it's done.
details: They find the thing both sides agree on and put it in the middle of the table. Simple.
The skill is in making both people feel heard before offering the resolution.
reactions: Peacemakers who don't take sides are worth their weight in gold.

entries.intro: {npc_name} holds grudges quietly. No outward anger: just a long, cold patience.
details: They'll be polite to your face while the debt sits in a ledger you can't see.
I wronged them once, small thing, and the temperature between us has never fully recovered.
reactions: I'd prefer the argument. At least arguments end.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} told me the grudges aren't deliberate: they just can't let certain things go, even when they want to. The feeling outlasts the reason.
statCheck.fail: Some people just have long memories for slights. Time doesn't soften everything.

entries.intro: {npc_name} and {npc_name_2} laugh at things nobody else finds funny. Their own frequency.
details: The joke lands for two people in the room and those two are already doubled over.
I've tried to understand the humor but I can't: it's a shared wavelength I'm not on.
reactions: Inside humor is impenetrable and honestly a little enviable.

entries.intro: {npc_name} does kind things and then denies doing them. The modesty is stubborn.
details: I thanked them for something specific and they said 'wasn't me' with a straight face.
Everyone knows it was them. The denial just makes the kindness slightly funnier.
reactions: Anonymous generosity from someone whose identity is an open secret: that's {npc_name} all over.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} told me the denial protects the act. Once something is acknowledged, it becomes an expectation, and expectations turn kindness into obligation.
statCheck.fail: Modest people deflect praise. It's awkward but harmless.

entries.intro: {npc_name} tells you what you want to hear and it's obvious every time.
details: The flattery is layered on so thick that it insults both of us: me for hearing it, them for saying it.
I'd rather hear a hard truth than a comfortable fiction.
reactions: Pleasant dishonesty is still dishonesty. I factor in the bias.

entries.intro: {npc_name} pats people on the shoulder when they're encouraging. It's a small thing that carries weight.
details: One firm pat and a nod. The gesture says 'you've got this' without needing the words.
I've received that pat on hard days and it did more than a long conversation would have.
reactions: Physical encouragement from the right person at the right time: there's no substitute.

entries.intro: {npc_name} wakes everyone else up when they're up early. Banging around, no awareness of volume.
details: The morning routine is percussive. Doors, boots, something falling over: every day.
I've asked them to be quieter and they tried for exactly one morning.
reactions: I go to sleep earlier now. Adjusting to {npc_name}'s schedule was easier than changing it.

entries.intro: I've been {self_role} long enough to know most people, but {npc_name} still surprises me.
details: Just when I think I've figured them out, they do something I wouldn't have predicted.
The unpredictability isn't chaos. It's depth: there's always another layer.
reactions: People who can still surprise you after years are the interesting ones.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} told me the surprises are deliberate. They like being unpredictable because it means nobody can box them in.
statCheck.fail: Complex people are hard to read. Maybe that's all there is to it.

entries.intro: {npc_name} covered for me when I made a mistake. Quietly, without anyone else knowing.
details: I found out later what they'd done. The fix was clean and nobody was the wiser.
They didn't ask for gratitude or even mention it. I only know because I traced the correction.
reactions: That kind of silent support: you don't forget it.

entries.intro: {npc_name} tracks favors like a merchant tracks debts. Nothing given is truly free.
details: They'll reference a small kindness from months ago as if it comes with interest.
I accepted a favor once and regretted it within the week.
reactions: I pay my own way around {npc_name} now. The cost of free things is too high.

entries.intro: {npc_name} and {npc_name_2} have been talking more recently. Good to see.
details: They weren't unfriendly before: just distant. Something bridged the gap.
Small conversations at first, now they linger. The warmth is growing visibly.
reactions: Watching two people find their way toward each other is quietly satisfying.

entries.intro: {npc_name} clears their throat before making a point. The throat-clear is the announcement.
details: You hear the 'ahem' and know something declarative is coming. It's a built-in warning system.
I've started bracing when I hear it. The opinions that follow the throat-clear tend to be firm.
reactions: Everyone has a preamble. {npc_name}'s is just more audible than most.

entries.intro: {npc_name} offered to stay late and help when everyone else had already left. That tells you everything.
details: The work wasn't theirs and the hour was bad, but they stayed anyway.
We finished in half the time with two pairs of hands.
reactions: Character shows up clearest when nobody's watching and nobody's asking.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} told me they stayed because an empty room with unfinished work reminds them too much of being alone with undone things. Helping someone else finish is easier than finishing alone.
statCheck.fail: Some people just don't like leaving work undone. Conscientiousness, nothing more.

entries.intro: You'd think the {npc_role} would notice what's happening right under their nose.
details: Things have slipped and everyone can see it except, apparently, the person whose job it is to see it.
I've mentioned it twice. Both times I got a reassuring nod and zero follow-through.
reactions: I've lowered my expectations to match the output.

statCheck.pass: The {npc_role} told me they see everything but can only address so much at once. The triage is invisible and the priorities aren't always what others expect.
statCheck.fail: Maybe they're just overwhelmed. It happens to anyone in that position long enough.

entries.intro: {npc_name} waved at me from across the way today. Small gesture, but it made the morning better.
details: I didn't expect it. Just a quick wave and a nod, then they went on with their day.
The acknowledgment is tiny but it makes you feel like part of the place.
reactions: Little recognitions from people who don't owe you attention: that's what makes a neighborhood.

entries.intro: {npc_name} and {npc_name_2} speak about each other warmly when the other isn't around. The respect is real.
details: I've heard {npc_name} praise {npc_name_2} without prompting, unprompted and specific.
The same goes the other way. The words match: steady mutual regard.
reactions: When two people say good things about each other in absence, that's the genuine article.

entries.intro: {npc_name} walks past me some days like I don't exist. Others, they're warm as can be.
details: The inconsistency is confusing. I never know which version I'm getting.
I've tried matching my behavior to theirs, but the switches come without warning.
reactions: I've stopped reading into it. Some people just run hot and cold.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} told me the cold days are when everything inside is too heavy to perform warmth. It's not about me: it's about what they're carrying.
statCheck.fail: Mood swings happen. Not every cold shoulder means something.

entries.intro: {npc_name} speaks softly and means every word. There's no wasted breath.
details: You have to lean in to hear them sometimes. It's worth the effort every time.
The quiet voice commands more attention than most people's shouting.
reactions: People who speak softly and carry weight in their words: those are the ones worth listening to.

entries.intro: {npc_name} looks tired lately. Not the 'bad sleep' kind: the 'carrying something' kind.
details: The eyes have that distant quality. Present but not entirely here.
I've asked if they're alright and they said yes, but the 'yes' was thin.
reactions: Not much you can do when someone doesn't want to talk about it. Just be nearby.

statCheck.pass: {npc_name} told me the tiredness is from a decision they keep turning over. They know what they need to do but can't bring themselves to do it yet.
statCheck.fail: Everyone has hard stretches. It'll pass, probably.

entries.intro: {npc_name} and {npc_name_2} have this quiet understanding that doesn't need explaining to each other.
details: They navigate shared space without collisions: a step left, a step right, never in each other's way.
The coordination is wordless and practiced, like two people who've been at this a long time.
reactions: That kind of unspoken fluency only comes from paying attention to someone for years.
